title: Gratitude To Parents
text: Allah interjects to say: وَوَصَّيْنَا ٱلْإِنسَنَ بِوَلِدَيْهِ حَمَلَتْهُ أُمُّهُۥ وَهْنًا عَلَىٰ وَهْنٍۢ وَفِصَلُهُۥ فِى عَامَيْنِ Gratitude to Allah begins with gratitude to parents - especially mothers أَنِ ٱشْكُرْ لِى وَلِوَلِدَيْكَ Ultimate gratitude belongs only to Allah and then to parents. إِلَىَّ ٱلْمَصِيرُ Allah will hold us accountable on the day of judgment for our gratitude towards him and parents.

title: Deceptive Friend
text: The word خزل in Arabic is close to the meaning of خضع which means deception. A خزول is a friend who only acts like your friend until the moment of truth arrives; and then you see their true colors. This is actually the description of شیطان in the Quran. His ultimate deception is to prevent us from Allah's remembrance. His game is to distract us and divert our attention towards دنیا . The Criterion [25:29] [ سورۃ ۲۵] الفرقان ‏لَّقَدْ أَضَلَّنِى عَنِ ٱلذِّكْرِ بَعْدَ إِذْ جَآءَنِى ۗ وَكَانَ ٱلشَّيْطَنُ لِلْإِنسَنِ خَذُولًۭا ‎ ۲۹

title: 1  Ilaqah
text: The first level is what they call علاقۃ and that is تَعَلُّقِ القَلب بِی المحبُوب The connection of the heart with the beloved. This is where the heart becomes connected or attached to the beloved.

title: 2  Iradah
text: The second level is called ارادۃ , which is willful love. This is where you actually begin looking for the beloved- At this stage, there is a pursuit of what the heart desires.

title: 3  Sababah
text: The third level is سبابۃ - Falling in Love The Arabs use this term to describe a waterfall.

title: 4  Gharaam
text: This غَرام is the state what the Arabs refer to as مُغْرَم بہ which means that they just want to be with the beloved. Distance becomes painful at this stage. Obsessed about the beloved

title: 5  Wud
text: The word وُد means pure love. It is صَفوَۃُ المَحَبَّۃ - the purity of love. A love that is pure from the inclinations of the self. This is one of the names of Allah. He is الودود - ‏وَٱسْتَغْفِرُوا۟ رَبَّكُمْ ثُمَّ تُوبُوٓا۟ إِلَيْهِ ۚ إِنَّ رَبِّى رَحِيمٌۭ وَدُودٌۭ ‎ Ask forgiveness of your master and turn to Him in repentance. Indeed my master is Most Merciful, Most Loving. Because he is selfless in his love. So الودود has 2 meanings: الوَادُّ فی عِبادِہِ والمَودُود Allah is the one who loves his servants and he is the one who his servants love.

title: 6  Shaghaf
text: The next stage is called شغف which is the pericardium. The heart is surrounded by love. The love encompasses the heart.

title: 7  Ishq
text: The stage of العشق is a very dangerous state of love. People who go into this state of love end up going insane. The word عشق comes from a vine named عَشَقَ that sucks out the life of that tree and eventually kills it. The story of کربلا is the story of عشق

title: SHAYTAN
text: The word شیطان is not the name of a specific person. It is not a proper noun, but an adjective. It describes the traits and characteristics of an entity The word شیطان comes from the root word َ شَطَن which means "to be far removed; to be distant; to stray away". Someone that is far removed from the mercy of Allah; Someone at a distance from Allah's رحمۃ Someone who has strayed from the straight path The Hebrew equivalent is Satan which means "the accuser, or the adversary". The same root شَطَنْ also means rope He is someone who ties and binds us with his various ropes of emotions and sentiments. Tactics He make us forget and overlook important things. He distracts us. He makes us feel sleepy; lazy or tired in the performance of good deeds. He makes us feel extremely scared, terrified or anxious Procrastination is from شیطان Method of Attack He attacks by whispering - وسوسۃ . His whispering is a relentless thought that he places in our mind, that would otherwise not be there. Thoughts of disobedience Lose control through rage He always works in the shadows; because he is a coward by nature. He can use objects such as filthy movies, corrupt TV shows , alcohol, drugs, vile and violent games شیاطین are all beings, which are or became a manifestation of evil and ugliness - they can be "evil among everything in shape" and "evil among everything invisible"

title: IBLIS
text: The word ابلیس comes from ابلس which means to despair. To completely and absolutely lose all hope without the possibility of a return. Suicide is a demonstration of complete despair ابلیس was a Jinn, who was the first created being to disobey Allah. In Christian theology this is the fallen angel Lucifer. But according to Quran, ابلیس was a Jinn and not an angel He is the leader and the command in chief, of شیاطین who are his followers and his minions. His efforts are geared towards preparing an enormous army of شیاطین in order to lead them into hellfire on the day of judgment.

title: JINN
text: The root word جن literally means "to conceal". It is anything that is hidden, veiled or concealed. The English word "Genie" is from this Arabic root جن The word جُنون means insanity or madness. A مجنون is a person whose intellect has become concealed or veiled. From from the same word جن we get جَنَّۃ which means paradise. It's beauty and magnificence has been concealed from our eyes, as they do not have the capacity to view its blinding beauty. The word جَنِین is a fetus that is concealed and hidden in the womb of its mother

title: MALIKAH
text: The word ملائکۃ comes from the root word مَلَک . A مَلَک is someone who is the sole owner of something; and the entity that he owns is called his ملکیۃ (property). The owner is responsible for his property. Linguistically a ملک is an entity that owns a responsibility, assigned to it by Allah(Subhanahu wa Ta'ala). The English word Angel comes from the Greek word "aggelos "which means "the messenger" Angels are not some separate, unique and perfect spiritual creatures Angels are pure, purified, perfect souls that have been assigned a responsibility. The angle of death ملک الموت has been assigned the responsibility of extracting the soul and detaching it from the body. The Prostration [32:11] [ سورۃ ۳۲] السجدۃ ‏ قُلْ يَتَوَفَّىٰكُم مَّلَكُ ٱلْمَوْتِ ٱلَّذِى وُكِّلَ بِكُمْ ثُمَّ إِلَىٰ رَبِّكُمْ تُرْجَعُونَ ‎ ۱۱ Say: "The angel of death, who is set over you, will take your souls, then you shall be brought to your Lord." There is اسرافل who has been assigned the task of resurrecting the dead and bringing them back to life on the day of judgment. There is منکر and نکیر who have been assigned the specific task of meeting us in our graves for questioning There is the angel جبرئیل who was assigned the task of bringing revelation to Rasul Allah(Salallahu alayhi wa aalihee wa sallam). There is میکائیل who is responsible for delivering sustenance. There are angels assigned to every drop of rain that falls from the sky, to ensure they fall exactly where they're supposed to. There are angles assigned to listen to our prayers and our calls of repentance. There are two angels assigned to every human being to accurately record their actions Qaf [50:17-18] [ سورۃ ۵۰] ق ‏إِذْ يَتَلَقَّى ٱلْمُتَلَقِّيَانِ عَنِ ٱلْيَمِينِ وَعَنِ ٱلشِّمَالِ قَعِيدٌۭ ‎ ۱۷ ‏مَّا يَلْفِظُ مِن قَوْلٍ إِلَّا لَدَيْهِ رَقِيبٌ عَتِيدٌۭ ‎ ۱۸ ( 17 ) that the two receivers receive, one sitting on the right and one on the left . ( 18 ) Not a word does he utter, but there is a watcher by him ready. The angel on the right records good actions; the angel on the left records bad actions.

title: Do Angels Not Have Free Will?
text: The Jinn, the angels and human beings are all essentially the same creation of Allah. Their titles vary based on their levels of belief, their piety, and their عبودیۃ They are created from a pure spiritual light called نور that represents the level of their knowledge and purity. The Jinn are created from smokeless fire They share free will with the human beings - which is the capacity of goodness and evil; Angels are incapable of disobedience. The knowledge that angels possess of divine realities overrides their own will. They are in a state where they have completely submitted their will to the will of Allah. Jinn and human beings are a much lower and weaker form of the same energy. We possess free will but lack the perfect knowledge of our creator or we have not developed our عبودیۃ . We learn from the story of Adam that ابلیس was a Jinn. He did not lack knowledge of Allah(Subhanahu wa Ta'ala). His problem was his lack of عبودیۃ . He lacked servitude. Jinn do not possess a material body (hence the metaphor of smokeless fire) whereas human beings possess a physical and material body. When Allah talks about our creation - he mentions both Jinn and humans, but he excludes angels: The Winnowing Winds [51:56] [ سورۃ ۵۱] الذاریات ‏وَمَا خَلَقْتُ ٱلْجِنَّ وَٱلْإِنسَ إِلَّا لِيَعْبُدُونِ ‎ ۵۶ And I did not create the Jinn and humans except they should submit their will to me Angels have already reached that desired state of submission. We share this earth with the Jinn. They interact with us in both, beneficial as well as harmful ways. They are good Jinn and bad Jinn, just as there are good and bad human beings. There are شیاطین among Jinn, just as there are شیاطین among men.

title: Applying the 10 Principles to Quran
text: Now that we understand what these 10 principles are, let's apply this criteria to determine to the Quran to help us determine, with absolute clarity, if studying the Quran is a valuable endeavor. اَلحَد Quran is the speech of Allah(Subhanahu wa Ta'ala), that was revealed to our Prophet Muhammad ( صلى الله عليه و سلم ) in the Arabic language through the medium of the angel جبرئیل علیہ السلام . The definition specifies 4 things: Divine speech In the Arabic language Revealed to the final messenger - Muhammad Mustafa (Salallahu alayhi wa aalihee wa sallam) Through the medium of angel JIBRAEEL (AS) و المَوضُوعُ Primarily deals with three subjects. Rights of Allah - حقوق اللہ Rights of the self - حقوق النفس Rights of others - حقوق العباد ثَمرَۃ It prevents us, or protects us from making mistakes in our understanding of Allah(Subhanhu Wa Ta'ala). Helps us accept and deal with trials and tribulations It teaches us the experience of his beloved messengers Helps us transform spiritually ُفَضل Its greatest virtue is that it helps us attain peace and satisfaction. نسبۃُ Quran is directly associated with, and is at the center of all individual and communal theories and practices of Islam الواضِعُ Allah is the primary source of this message Secondary sources are: Jibraeel (AS) Rasul Allah(Salallahu alayhi wa aalihee wa sallam) Maulana Ali Ibn Abu Talib IMAMS DUA'AT MANASEEB الأسمُ Quran teaches us the terminology to understand realities of the physical and metaphysical world. Quran was specifically revealed in Arabic for its preciseness الاستِمدادُ Quran is derived from the principles of توحید الشارع و الحکم Acquisition of this knowledge is فرض عین . طلب العلم فریضۃ علی کل مسلم و مسلمۃ The acquisition of knowledge is obligatory on every Muslim man and woman. المسائِلُ Details will span over the remainder of these sessions InshaAllah.

title: Purpose of these metaphors
text: Quran is the village of Allah We want to enter Allah's village, seeking his refuge from the dangers of the jungle of this world. We have to approach the Quran with the humility and the abasement of a refugee who is desperately trying to seek protection of Allah from the dangers of the jungle. Quran forces us to take responsibility which human beings find very difficult. The most dangerous aspect of the Quran is that it is criticizing you. It is a deeply disturbing book. It is not a book for entertainment. The task assigned to us within this village is عبادۃ - worship. Worship is based on 2 things. It is based on belief and action - ایمان and أحکام . It is based on علم and عمل . The citizenship of Quran is for the pious - متقین - Quran forces us to change into - متقین The Cow [2:1-2] [ سورۃ ۲] البقرۃ ‏الٓمٓ ‎ ۱ ‏ذَلِكَ ٱلْكِتَبُ لَا رَيْبَ ۛ فِيهِ ۛ هُدًۭى لِّلْمُتَّقِينَ ‎ ۲ That Book, which contains no doubts, is guidance for those who are pious. But in the end it is most beautiful place to be. It ends in the company of the Rasul Allah(Salallahu alayhi wa aalihee wa sallam). It ends in the company of Maulana Ali. It ends in the company of Maulatina Fatima It ends in the company of Imams from their progeny. And most important - it ends in the company of Allah(Subhanahu wa Ta'ala). where a merciful master will greet his slaves with salutations of peace Ya Sîn [36:58] [ سورۃ ۳۶] یس ‏سَلَمٌۭ قَوْلًۭا مِّن رَّبٍّۢ رَّحِيمٍۢ ‎ ۵۸ A salutation of peace and blessings for your master - the Most Merciful. May Allah grant us all the wisdom, the strength and courage, and the determination to truly take on this challenge and become from among the متقین .

title: Linguistic Meaning Of Sajda
text: The ancient Arabs used the word سجدۃ for a camel when it would lower itself before its rider, allowing him to mount it. It means to pay respect, or to honor someone It is a gesture of accepting someone else's superiority over oneself. The most essential ingredient of سجدۃ is humility Another beautiful image of the words سجدۃ is the bending and lowering of oneself because of the weight of the bounties that are placed upon ones soul. For example the Arabs would say سَجَدَتْ النَّخْلَۃ . The palm tree bent down by the abundance of its fruit. The weight of Allah's bounties and blessings in our lives should force us to bend and lower ourselves in سجدۃ before Him.

title: Soul: Purpose Of Creation
text: Scholars state that there are 3 reasons for the creation of human beings There are 2 types of خلافۃ - stewardship: To rule the self To rule others Sad [38:26] [ سورۃ ۳۸] ص ‏يَدَاوُۥدُ إِنَّا جَعَلْنَكَ خَلِيفَةًۭ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ فَٱحْكُم بَيْنَ ٱلنَّاسِ بِٱلْحَقِّ وَلَا تَتَّبِعِ ٱلْهَوَىٰ فَيُضِلَّكَ عَن سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ ۚ إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ يَضِلُّونَ عَن سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ لَهُمْ عَذَابٌۭ شَدِيدٌۢ بِمَا نَسُوا۟ يَوْمَ ٱلْحِسَابِ ‎ ۲۶

title: Akhlaq
text: The word أخلاق is the plural form of the word خُلق . The word خَلق means creation, which is related to the body that exists in the horizons. The word خُلق means character which belongs to the soul. The word خَلق is related to the body which is nourished by this physical world . The word خُلق is character, which takes its nourishment from the spiritual world. Both words begin with the letter خ , which is a sound of friction (effort) in Arabic. It takes effort to develop the body just as it takes effort to develop character Our real acquisition خُلق character! A soul that returns back to its master without a developed خُلق is an impoverished soul. A soul that is bankrupt of أخلاق is not going to be able to benefit from the pleasures of the after life Character is developed through revelation

title: Interactions In Islam
text: There are 3 types of interactions ( معاملات ) in Islam Interaction with Allah - حقوق اللہ Interaction with the self - حقوق النفس Interaction with the creation of Allah - حقوق العباد Rights of Allah - حقوق اللہ Rights Of the self - حقوق النفس Rights Of Others - حقوق العباد Right of Allah was submitting to him in willingly obedience out of love through عبادۃ . after recognizing him through his signs in the universe Right of the self is to internalize those divine attributes to build our character Right of others is the idea of reflecting those divine attributes of Allah in interactions within his creation Rasul Allah(Salallahu alayhi wa aalihee wa sallam) said in a HADEES: أتخذو من أخلاق اللہ Adopt the character and the qualities of Allah! Rasul Allah(Salallahu alayhi wa aalihee wa sallam) developed such an impeccable character that Allah himself acknowledged his accomplishment in the Quran by saying: ‏وَإِنَّكَ لَعَلَىٰ خُلُقٍ عَظِيمٍۢ Indeed! No doubt about it, You have achieved the utmost, and the most exalted level of character!

title: Protective Friend
text: The word ولی in the Arabic language comes from مُوَالات which is a kind of protection. A ولی is someone who takes care of your matters, and always has your back. One of the names of Allah is الولی . The Cow [2:257] [ سورۃ ۲] البقرۃ ‏ٱللَّهُ وَلِىُّ ٱلَّذِينَ آمَنُوا۟ يُخْرِجُهُم مِّنَ ٱلظُّلُمَتِ إِلَى ٱلنُّورِ ۖ وَٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوٓا۟ أَوْلِيَآؤُهُمُ ٱلطَّغُوتُ يُخْرِجُونَهُم مِّنَ ٱلنُّورِ إِلَى ٱلظُّلُمَتِ ۗ أُو۟لَٓئِكَ أَصْحَبُ ٱلنَّارِ ۖ هُمْ فِيهَا خَلِدُونَ ‎ ۲۵۷ Allah is the Walî of those who believe. He brings them out from darkness into light. But as for those who disbelieve, their Auliya are Taghut , they bring them out from light into darkness. Those are the dwellers of the Fire, and they will abide therein forever. The word مولا is used for that same caring and concerned friend when he takes on an authoritative role. Allah commands us to be warm protective friend to our enemies Fusilat [41:34] [ سورۃ ۴۱] فصلت ‏وَلَا تَسْتَوِى ٱلْحَسَنَةُ وَلَا ٱلسَّيِّئَةُ ۚ ٱدْفَعْ بِٱلَّتِى هِىَ أَحْسَنُ فَإِذَا ٱلَّذِى بَيْنَكَ وَبَيْنَهُۥ عَدَوَةٌۭ كَأَنَّهُۥ وَلِىٌّ حَمِيمٌۭ ‎ ۳۴ The good deed and the evil deed cannot be equal. Repel with one which is better, then verily! he, between whom and you there was enmity, as though he was a close friend.

title: Sincere Friend
text: The word صدیق comes from صدق , which means two things: Sincerity Truthfulness. A صدیق has no hidden agenda Allah describes the loss of this friendship on the day of judgment وَمَآ أَضَلَّنَآ إِلَّا ٱلْمُجْرِمُونَ Nobody misled us except the criminals فَمَا لَنَا مِن شَفِعِينَ We have nobody on our side to make a case for us وَلَا صَدِيقٍ حَمِيمٍۢ We have no true sincere honest friends that are close to us.

title: What Is Forbearance?
text: The expected behavior of a believer in the face of a calamity is forbearance. The Arabic word for forbearance is حلم The word for a person who has forbearance is حلیم . One of the words for intellect is also حلم . A dreamer is called a حَالَم . Allah says: Joseph [12:44] [ سورۃ ۱۲] یوسف ‏قَالُوٓا۟ أَضْغَثُ أَحْلَمٍۢ ۖ وَمَا نَحْنُ بِتَأْوِيلِ ٱلْأَحْلَمِ بِعَلِمِينَ ‎ ۴۴ They said, It is nothing more than a mixture of false dreams, and we are not learned in the interpretation of dreams." The word for nipple is حَلَمَ The root also means knowledge Forbearance has a deep connection with knowledge - linguistically as well as in practice. Going astray غَوَایَۃ is an inability to digest milk. Rasul Allah(Salallahu alayhi wa aalihee wa sallam) had the most forbearance of all.

title: Knowledge
text: قال رسول اللہ صلی اللہ علیہ و آلہ و سلم أنا عِنْدَ ظَنِّ عبدي بي ، وأنا معه إذا ذكرني ، فإن ذكرني في نفسه، ذكرته في نفسي ؛ وإن ذكرني في ملإ، ذكرته في ملإ خيرٌ مِنهُم ؛ وإن تقرب إلي بِشِبْر، تقربت إليه ذِرَاعاً ؛ وإن تقرب إلي ذِرَاعاً ، تقربت إليه بَاعاً ؛ وإن أتاني يمشي، أتيته هَرَوْلة I am in the opinion of my slave; and I am with him when he remembers me. When he remembers me inwardly, I remember him inwardly. When he remembers me in an assembly, I remember him in a much greater assembly (the assembly of angels). When he takes a step towards me, I move an arm's length towards him. When he moves an arms length towards me, I move a yard towards him. When he walks towards me, I run towards him.

title: What Is A Sin?
text: The first and greatest benefit of calamity is the removal of sins. Sin is also an archery term, which means to miss the mark Hitting the mark is the reality of "choice" or "free will" Falling short is transgression As believers, our Our purpose is glorification of Allah means to constantly strive to make the invisible qualities of Allah, visible. represent Allah in behavior Commandments are laws that reflect Allah's nature

title: How To Study The Quran
text: بسم اللہ الرحمن الرحیم۔ السلام علیکم و رحمۃ اللہ و برکاتہ Accept congratulations on the start of your new session. I wish to say a few words about it. The glorified Quran is an honored scripture that is the word of Allah. It is full of wisdom, lofty and elevated in its greatness, and unique in its content. One should exercise extreme care when reading or studying this book. Do not read this book like a novel. If you observe the the principles and conditions of studying this book, you will surely benefit from it. Quran is the light of remembrance and pure light. Achieve its blessings so that your heart is illuminated and your intellect is brightened with its light. When reciting this book, it is recommended to be in a state of وُضو (ablution), and facing the قبلۃ . Read this book with such humility, as if you are speaking to Allah(Subhanahu wa Ta'ala). You'll find certain verses in which Allah is directly speaking to you. When you read verses of warnings and threats, in which Allah mentions the torments and punishments of hell fire, your hearts should tremble, and your eyes should moisten with tears of repentance; and you should be moved to offer regret and remorse for your shortcomings. In other verses Allah mentions his blessings and bounties to you. When you come upon such verses, express gratitude for his blessings, mercy and gifts. Learn valuable lessons from the stories of Quran, and remember that nothing is new under this sun. What has happened before shall happen again. All of this is only possible when you study this Quran with comprehension, and extract the obvious and hidden meaning from its verses, and reflect deeply on its message. This book is only for people of intellects and not for the ignorant. The ignorant are among those for whom is said, "nothing can be achieved from nothing". I hope you all will take full advantage from these sessions and remember its teachings, and strengthen your faith and reverence for this religion. انشاء اللہ تعالی و مع السلام

title: Essence (LUBB)
text: لُب = Spiritual Heart ( قلب ) + Spiritual Intellect ( عقل ) Heart The spiritual heart is the center of free will - ارادۃ . When I say, "I want to eat cake" نفس - is what will eat the cake. The desire to eat is coming from the spiritual heart. Heart is the component that commands the نفس The heart is also the center of all emotions - The word قلب in Arabic means To flip something - to turn something around on its face; To invert something - like turning over a cloth. The word انقلاب also comes from the same root word, which means "a revolution". The physical heart called قلب because it never rests. It is constant flipping between two states, to pump and deliver oxygenated blood to all parts of the body. The spiritual heart is constantly feeding commands and desires to the نفس (the soul) through emotions. It's constantly pumping desires and free will into the نفس - Emotions constantly vary Intellect Desires and emotions are blind. Emotions need direction, boundaries and restraint. Even good and healthy emotions can be disastrous if there are no boundaries set for it. The word عقل means to bind or to restrain عِقال , in Arabic is a rope that is used to tie a camel with. As the child grows, the information that the نفس acquires from its surrounding environment - parents, family, friends, social media, movies - begins developing these components of the heart and the intellect. The soul or the "self" begins operating under the influence of the heart, and the guidance of the intellect . Once the child matures into an adult, the نفس has now taken complete control over his body. It is now able to obey the commands of his spiritual heart, under the guidance and confines of his intellect. The Quran speaks to this combination of the heart and intellect. Quran is a map or a guide. It is a believers navigation system (GPS). Without this map, this clear direction, the نفس would be lost. It would just wander off into any valley the heart would command, and end up destroying itself. Quran serves as an external intellect, that works with the internal intellect to direct the soul towards a specific goal.

title: HADEES On Intellect
text: قال رسول اللہ صلی اللہ علیہ وآلہ و سلم أول ما خلق اللہُ عزّ وجلّ العقل۔ ثمّ قال له: أقبل! فأقبل ثمّ قال له: أدبر! فأدبر۔ ثمّ قال: وعزّتي وجلالي! ما خلقت خلقاً هو أحبٌ إلىً منك ؛ ولا أكمَلتُكِ إلاّ فِي من أُحِبٌ۔ أما إنّي إيّاك آمر وإيّاك أنهى۔ وإيَاك أٌعاقب وإيّاك أُثيب The first thing that Allah created is the intellect. He then said to it: Step forward, so it stepped forward. Then He said: Go back! So it went back. Then he said: By My power and majesty! I did not create any creature dearer to me than you. I will not make you perfect, except in the one whom I love. Indeed, to you are my orders and prohibitions addressed. And for you are my rewards and retributions reserved

title: SAYYED (One With Authority)
text: The word سید means someone who has authority. It is possible to own things but not have complete authority over it; just as it is possible to have complete authority over something but not necessarily have ownership of it Allah as رب , he has ABSOLUTE ownership; and He has ABSOLUTE authority

title: MURABBEE (The One Who Nourishes)
text: The word means someone who ensures growth; who takes care of something; who nurtures something so that it can grow. The process of nurturing is called تربیۃ . The تربیۃ of a plant would be to take care of it. The تربیۃ of children is taking care of their physical needs; their diets, their clothing, their mental needs; educating them; protecting them. The act of تربیۃ is not quick and easy process. It is a very long and time consuming process? The primary ingredient of تربیۃ is mercy (love, care and concern)

title: MURSHAD (Guide)
text: He is the one who sets things in the right direction. The word مُرشَد comes from the word رُشد which in classical Arabic means to ripen ; He is the one who takes something in its raw form, and works on it till it ripens and matures

title: MUN'IM (Benefactor)
text: The مُنعِم is used for the one who bestows gifts It comes from the root نِعَم or نعمۃ which means gifts or blessings. Allah as our master does not owe us anything. Therefore anything and everything he gives us has to be a gift. It is not something he is obligated to give us at all.

title: QAYYIM (SUSTAINER)
text: The final meaning of the word رب comes from قَیِّم who is the one that makes sure that things stay together; that they don't fall apart. The one who has his constant attention and focus on things. The word قیوم is the superlative form of قَیِّم . The one who constantly sustains; who constantly maintains without taking any breaks. He planned out every creations destiny from beginning to end.

title: RABB (Master)
text: So now let's look at the complete definition of the word Rabb. الرب ھو المالک والسَّیِّد و المُرَبِّی والمُرشِد وَالمُنعِم والقَیِّم Allah as our رب is the sum of all these 6 meanings at the same time. His ownership includes his authority. which includes his nurturing, which includes his guidance and direction, which includes his blessings, which includes his sustenance, provisions and maintenance; all at the same time. The Family of Imran [3:190-192] [ سورۃ ۳] آل عمران ‏إِنَّ فِى خَلْقِ ٱلسَّمَوَتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ وَٱخْتِلَفِ ٱلَّيْلِ وَٱلنَّهَارِ لَآيَتٍۢ لِّأُو۟لِى ٱلْأَلْبَبِ ‎ ۱۹۰ ‏ٱلَّذِينَ يَذْكُرُونَ ٱللَّهَ قِيَمًۭا وَقُعُودًۭا وَعَلَىٰ جُنُوبِهِمْ وَيَتَفَكَّرُونَ فِى خَلْقِ ٱلسَّمَوَتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ رَبَّنَا مَا خَلَقْتَ هَذَا بَطِلًۭا سُبْحَنَكَ فَقِنَا عَذَابَ ٱلنَّارِ ‎ ۱۹۱ ‏ رَبَّنَآ إِنَّكَ مَن تُدْخِلِ ٱلنَّارَ فَقَدْ أَخْزَيْتَهُۥ ۖ وَمَا لِلظَّلِمِينَ مِنْ أَنصَارٍۢ ‎ ۱۹۲ ( 190 ) Verily! In the creation of the heavens and the earth, and in the alternation of night and day, there are indeed signs for men of understanding. ( 191 ) Those who remember Allah standing, sitting, and lying down on their sides, and think deeply about the creation of the heavens and the earth, :"Our Lord! You have not created this without purpose, glory to You!. Give us salvation from the torment of the Fire. ( 192 ) "Our Lord! Verily, whom You admit to the Fire, indeed, You have disgraced him, and never will the oppressors find any helpers. The very first ayah that was revealed to Rasul Allah (Salallahu alayhi wa aalihee wa sallam) was The Clot [96:1] [ سورۃ ۹۶] العلق ‏ٱقْرَأْ بِٱسْمِ رَبِّكَ ٱلَّذِى خَلَقَ ‎ ۱ Read! In the Name of your Lord, Who has created, Allah introduced himself as رب who and not by his name Allah. He ended his revelation with the verse The Table Spread [5:3] [ سورۃ ۵] المائدۃ ٱلْيَوْمَ أَكْمَلْتُ لَكُمْ دِينَكُمْ وَأَتْمَمْتُ عَلَيْكُمْ نِعْمَتِى وَرَضِيتُ لَكُمُ ٱلْإِسْلَمَ دِينًۭا ۚ فَمَنِ ٱضْطُرَّ فِى مَخْمَصَةٍ غَيْرَ مُتَجَانِفٍۢ لِّإِثْمٍۢ ۙ فَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ غَفُورٌۭ رَّحِيمٌۭ ‎ ۳ This day, those who disbelieved have given up all hope of your religion, so fear them not, but fear Me. This day, I have perfected your religion for you, completed My Favor upon you, and have chosen for you Islam as your religion. But as for him who is forced by severe hunger, with no inclination to sin, then surely, Allah is Oft ­Forgiving, Most Merciful.

title: Spiritual Components
text: Two things to note: This spiritual heart and the spiritual intellect, have nothing to do with the physical heart and the physical brain. Even though we're breaking the soul down into multiple components, we're still talking about a single entity - one روح .

title: The Body (JISM)
text: 23*12-14 وَلَقَدْ خَلَقْنَا ٱلْإِنسَنَ مِن سُلَلَةٍۢ مِّن طِينٍۢ And indeed We created man out of an extract of clay. The human body is extracted from this earth in the form of nutrition ثُمَّ جَعَلْنَهُ نُطْفَةًۭ فِى قَرَارٍۢ مَّكِينٍۢ Then we made him into a drop of sperm in a safe lodging. ثُمَّ خَلَقْنَا ٱلنُّطْفَةَ عَلَقَةًۭ ؛ فَخَلَقْنَا ٱلْعَلَقَةَ مُضْغَةًۭ ؛ فَخَلَقْنَا ٱلْمُضْغَةَ عِظَمًۭا فَكَسَوْنَا ٱلْعِظَمَ لَحْمًۭا ؛ ثُمَّ أَنشَأْنَهُ خَلْقًا آخَرَ Then we made the drop of sperm into a clinging clot, then we made the clot into a lump, from that lump we made bones, then we covered the bones with flesh; and we finally developed him into his final form. فَتَبَارَكَ ٱللَّهُ أَحْسَنُ ٱلْخَلِقِينَ So blessed is Allah the best of creators.

title: The Spirit (RUH & FITRAH)
text: روح is the pure, original, primordial energy that Allah(Subhanahu wa Ta'ala) created in the spiritual realm ( عالم روحانی ). The word روح comes from the root word ریح which means wind; because the spirit is "subtle" like the wind. : 17*85 وَيَسْـَٔلُونَكَ عَنِ ٱلرُّوحِ قُلِ ٱلرُّوحُ مِنْ أَمْرِ رَبِّى وَمَآ أُوتِيتُم مِّنَ ٱلْعِلْمِ إِلَّا قَلِيلًۭا And they ask you about the Spirit. Say: "The RUH is from the command of my master; وَمَآ أُوتِيتُم مِّنَ ٱلْعِلْمِ إِلَّا قَلِيلًۭا And you have been given very little of that knowledge. 7*172 وَإِذْ أَخَذَ رَبُّكَ مِنۢ بَنِىٓ آدَمَ مِن ظُهُورِهِمْ ذُرِّيَّتَهُمْ And when your master created descendants from the children of Adam وَأَشْهَدَهُمْ عَلَىٰٓ أَنفُسِهِمْ and he made them testify of themselves أَلَسْتُ بِرَبِّكُمْ Am I not your Lord? قَالُوا۟ بَلَىٰ ۛ شَهِدْنَآ They said, "Indeed - no doubt about it, we have testified that you are our lord!" Why did he make us testify? أَن تَقُولُوا۟ يَوْمَ ٱلْقِيَمَةِ إِنَّا كُنَّا عَنْ هَذَا غَفِلِينَ So that on the day of Resurrection you don't say, "We were unaware of this." Every spirit is born Muslim The روح not only possesses all the attributes of its lord, it is also in perfect submission to its master. The روح by accepting Allah(Subhanahu wa Ta'ala) as its master, accepted itself as a slave? The word اسلام comes from the root word سلَّم which means "to submit". The linguistic meaning of the word اسلام is "submission"; And the one submits is called a "Muslim". The روح by virtue of its testimony became Muslim. Rasul Allah(Salallahu alayhi wa aalihee wa sallam) said in a HADEES: مَا مِنْ مَوْلُودٍ إِلَّا يُولَدُ عَلَى الْفِطْرَةِ There is not a single child; that is not born upon this pure state "Fitrah"; of natural disposition فَأَبَوَاهُ يُهَوِّدَانِهِ وَيُنَصِّرَانِهِ وَيُمَجِّسَانِهِ It is then his parents (in other words, the external influences) that make him a Jew, Christian or a Zoroastrian. Nature of a child A child's nature is extremely pure. A child is an embodiment of all the teachings of Islam. It is in a state of pure فطرۃ and this is why we all love children. A child never lies. A child will exhibit "perfect", "unshakable" belief. You tell a child that Allah is merciful; he loves you; he loves you more than your parents, the child will believe that. A child will never blame Allah for its suffering; because it has no doubts about the love of Allah that was taught to it by its parents. A child is selfless by its pure nature A child exhibits perfect love, obedience and servitude to its parents. I child forgives unconditionally A child never overeats. Given the choice between alcohol and milk, the child will always choose milk. Children our extremely compassionate.

title: Soul (NAFS)
text: Modern psychology calls نفس "self" or the "ego". It's also referred to as أنا in the Arabic language, which means "I or me". The concept of self has to do with the responsibility. Even though humanity as a whole, is united in possessing the qualities of a "soul" - individually they are responsible for exercising the agencies of their "free will" that the soul provides to them. Infant begins to take control of its body "I am my body" changes to "I have a body". Childish behavior is a very negative trait. Childlike behavior is a very positive trait. Its a summation of all the angelic qualities of children. The child learns to say NO. Denial or rejection is the very first thing that children deeply connect with. This is an extremely important component of the child's spiritual development process - because this child was created to say "No". It was created to say لا الہ - It was created to refuse and reject false god, and testify for the one true God - لا الہ الا اللہ - There is no deity except Allah. Our creedal formula begins with a denial, and the word لا . Because we need to get a handle on rejecting and denying false gods, before we can affirm and accept the one true God.

title: Recap of spirit
text: The روح is what Allah(Subhanahu wa Ta'ala) created in the spiritual realm through a command. Before sending us in this physical realm, he asked us a fundamental question: الست بربکم؟ , "Am I not your master?" And we replied, بلی شھدنا "Indeed! we testify that you are!". Allah placed the conviction of that testimony in our spirits in the form of فطرۃ He then sent us into this physical realm, a place of numerous distractions, to prove the sincerity of our primordial testimony through action. The first thing that develops in the physical world is the body. The روح is joined to the body when the fetus is 4 months old The نفس (soul) is what psychologists calls the "self" or the "ego". This is the component that is conscious of its own existence. It has the ability to respond with free will. The spiritual heart ( قلب ). is the component that Allah(Subhanahu wa Ta'ala) designed for attachments. The word قلب means to turn over , because it was designed to turn over to face Allah To guide the heart, Allah(Subhanahu wa Ta'ala) created the intellect ( عقل ) which linguistically means "to bind". It's job and function is to bind the heart to its creator.

title: Theme Of Quran
text: We have been assigned a task by Allah(Subhanahu wa Ta'ala) which is to realize لا الہ الا اللہ . Assignments are always dictated by the one assigning the task The assignee must be prepared for the assignment, and given the necessary tools to complete the assignment. Allah(Subhanahu wa Ta'ala) created us, and gave us everything we need to reach him. He gave us physical and spiritual existence He gave us a conscience through our فطرۃ He gave us a sense of identity through our نفس He gave us a spiritual heart - a قلب that by its design yearns for attachments He gave us an intellect to guide and navigate our desires and attachments towards him. The problem is that we innovate and come up with the set of criteria of reaching Allah.

title: Humility
text: Allah states that it is He who created different cultures, different creations, different civilizations. people of different languages. He says in the Quran: يَبَنِىٓ إِسْرَٓءِيلَ ٱذْكُرُوا۟ نِعْمَتِىَ ٱلَّتِىٓ أَنْعَمْتُ عَلَيْكُمْ وَأَنِّى فَضَّلْتُكُمْ عَلَى ٱلْعَلَمِينَ I gave you preference over every other nation ( عالمین ) Allah is the creator and the master of all nations, therefore we better treat them properly. If we hurt his slaves, we will have to answer to him. When a slave is hurt the master gets offended ultimately Maulana Ali explains that what this title of رب العلمین does, is that it bring us all down on the same level in the eyes of the creator. Superiority in ranks can only exist through the eyes of the master - based on the servitude of the slave. Not through the eyes of the slave. إِنَّ أَكْرَمَكُمْ عِندَ اللَّهِ أَتْقَاكُمْ The most honorable in the eyes of Allah. is the one who is the most pious. Both, the highest ranking person as well as the lowest ranking person is Allah's slave Rasul Allah(Salallahu alayhi wa aalihee wa sallam) himself was honored with the title of the "slave" of Allah - اشھد ان محمد عبدہ و رسولہ Arrogance is the trait of ابلیس ? Desiring self-worship is the trait of فرعون . The greatest sign of the arrival of Rasul Allah(Salallahu alayhi wa aalihee wa sallam) prophesied in the bible was his humility All human beings came from Adam and Adam came from dust.

title: The Divine Formula
text: There are the two essential questions that mankind has grappled with from the beginning of time. Who is God What are his capabilities? What are his attributes? What is his personality like? What are his traits and attributes? What does he want from us? Why did he create this universe? What is our purpose on this earth? What are his expectations from us? The divine Formula: Horizon is the physicality that lies outside of us Spirit/Soul is the spiritual universe that lies inside of us

title: Horizons
text: The are two pre-requisite for understanding this formula. One must have a sound intellect and a pure heart, which the Quran refers to as لُب ; the core. One must possess an uncontaminated فطرۃ , which stems from a pure heart and a sound intellect The device that allows us to study the universe is the intellect - العقل by defining and limiting things. Knowledge very simply defined, knowledge is the understanding and the recognizing the true reality of things. The foundation of that understanding first involves a concept ( تصور ), followed by a حکم a ruling or judgment about the authenticity of that reality. Proposition A proposition is a statement or an assertion that expresses a judgment or an opinion. Scholars say that there are 3 types of propositions: Empirical Propositions These are based on our observation or experience, utilizing the 5 senses (sight, hearing, smell, taste, touch). These are also called sensory propositions. Deals with the seen or visible realm - عالم الشھادۃ through the 5 senses Rational Propositions These are logical propositions, like 3 + 2 equals 5, therefore 5 - 3 must be 2. These propositions deals with the seen or visible realm - عالم الشھادۃ through inference They are strengthened by abundance of empirical data. Philosophical / Revelatory Propositions These are the Quranic propositions that talk about عالم الغیب - The Unseen Realm. Our ایمان is grounded in these revelatory propositions. It is supported and strengthened by the combination of empirical and rational propositions Allah says: We will show them our signs, our evidence فِى ٱلأٓفَاقِ in the horizons, which contains empirical evidence; and we will show them our signs وَفِىٓ أَنفُسِهِمْ - within their own selves which contains the ability to deduce and infer rational propositions through empirical data! Therefore: ‏سَنُرِيهِمْ آيَتِنَا فِى ٱلأٓفَاقِ وَفِىٓ أَنفُسِهِمْ We will show them the evidence of the unseen realm in the form of empirical and rational data of this universe... حَتَّىٰ يَتَبَيَّنَ لَهُمْ أَنَّهُ ٱلْحَقُّ Unlimited evidence - until it becomes absolutely clear to them that these "revelatory" propositions are true.

title: Self/Soul
text: The second part answers the question of what Allah wants from us. Allah declared his intentions of creation of human beings as follows: The Cow [2:30] [ سورۃ ۲] البقرۃ ‏وَإِذْ قَالَ رَبُّكَ لِلْمَلَٓئِكَةِ إِنِّى جَاعِلٌۭ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ خَلِيفَةًۭ ۖ ‎ ۳۰ And when your Lord said to the angels: "Verily, I am going to place generations after generations on earth." The word خلیفۃ comes from the root word خَلَفَ , which mean "back" or "behind". سَلَفَ is the antonym which means the ahead. So خلف means the one who is behind the one who follows the one who succeeds The perfect representative. Allah said to the angels, I am planning to create my representative on earth. Now, think about this term - representative! A representative is a person chosen or appointed to act or speak on behalf of another person. After creating Adam AS, Allah also taught him all his traits The Cow [2:31] [ سورۃ ۲] البقرۃ ‏وَعَلَّمَ آدَمَ ٱلْأَسْمَآءَ كُلَّهَا ثُمَّ عَرَضَهُمْ عَلَى ٱلْمَلَٓئِكَةِ فَقَالَ أَنۢبِـُٔونِى بِأَسْمَآءِ هَٓؤُلَآءِ إِن كُنتُمْ صَدِقِينَ ‎ ۳۱ And He taught Adam all the names , then He showed them to the angels and said, "Tell Me the names of these if you are truthful." Allah trained Adam AS in all the divine traits that Allah himself possessed. This left the angels speechless The Cow [2:32] [ سورۃ ۲] البقرۃ ‏قَالُوا۟ سُبْحَنَكَ لَا عِلْمَ لَنَآ إِلَّا مَا عَلَّمْتَنَآ ۖ إِنَّكَ أَنتَ ٱلْعَلِيمُ ٱلْحَكِيمُ ‎ ۳۲ Initially the angels that they were capable of representing Allah on earth. They said: The Cow [2:30] [ سورۃ ۲] البقرۃ ‏وَإِذْ قَالَ رَبُّكَ لِلْمَلَٓئِكَةِ إِنِّى جَاعِلٌۭ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ خَلِيفَةًۭ ۖ قَالُوٓا۟ أَتَجْعَلُ فِيهَا مَن يُفْسِدُ فِيهَا وَيَسْفِكُ ٱلدِّمَآءَ وَنَحْنُ نُسَبِّحُ بِحَمْدِكَ وَنُقَدِّسُ لَكَ ۖ قَالَ إِنِّىٓ أَعْلَمُ مَا لَا تَعْلَمُونَ ‎ ۳۰ This is the concept of Allah creating man in his own image. We possess all the attributes of our creator He can see and hear He can act He can love and hate We have been created with the same divine attributes that Allah possesses, so that we could be his true representatives, his true خلیفۃ on this earth. To understand what Allah wants from you, we simply have to reflect on our own selves? Its very simple! Just look at your own self? There is not a single thing that Allah demands from us in the Quran, which we would not demand for our self from someone else. The one who comes to know of his own self, comes to know of his master, because the slave has been created in the image of his master.

title: Rights Of Allah
text: As human beings we are all entitled to certain fundamental rights. Similarly Allah has rights. Human rights are based on divine rights; divine rights are not based on human rights. Allah says: وَاللَّـهُ أَخْرَجَكُم مِّن بُطُونِ أُمَّهَاتِكُمْ لَا تَعْلَمُونَ شَيْئًا What is the right of Allah who brought you out from the wombs of your mothers in a state where you knew nothing. وَجَعَلَ لَكُمُ السَّمْعَ وَالْأَبْصَارَ وَالْأَفْئِدَةَۙ What is the right of Allah who then gave you hearing and sight and a beating heart? لَعَلَّكُمْ تَشْكُرُونَ So that you could become grateful! As human beings, when we provide for someone, the least that we can expect back is their gratitude, if not an equal response in kind. So then what about لَا نَسْأَلُكَ رِزْقًاۖ نَّحْنُ نَرْزُقُكَۗوَ We do not ask you for provisions; Indeed it is we that provide to you! وَمَا بِكُم مِّن نِّعْمَةٍ فَمِنَ اللَّـهِۖثُمَّ Any and every good thing that you have in your possession, has been given to you by Allah. وَمَا خَلَقْتُ الْجِنَّ وَالْإِنسَ إِلَّا لِيَعْبُدُونِ And indeed I have not created jinn and mankind except to worship me. How are we fulfilling that right of Allah? Does Allah have the right to get upset and angry when we do not fulfill his rights? A beautiful advise: If you do not consider Allah to be superior than you, then at least consider him an equal "

title: Disobeying Parents
text: Allah points out an exception in obedience to parents: Luqman [31:15] [ سورۃ ۳۱] لقمان ‏وَإِن جَهَدَاكَ عَلَىٰٓ أَن تُشْرِكَ بِى مَا لَيْسَ لَكَ بِهِۦ عِلْمٌۭ فَلَا تُطِعْهُمَا ۖ وَصَاحِبْهُمَا فِى ٱلدُّنْيَا مَعْرُوفًۭا ۖ وَٱتَّبِعْ سَبِيلَ مَنْ أَنَابَ إِلَىَّ ۚ ثُمَّ إِلَىَّ مَرْجِعُكُمْ فَأُنَبِّئُكُم بِمَا كُنتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ ‎ ۱۵ But if they strive to make you associate partners with Me that of which you have no knowledge, do not obey them but accompany them in [this] world with appropriate kindness and follow the way of those who turn back to Me [in repentance]. Then to Me will be your return, and I will inform you about what you used to do.

title: 10  Khullah
text: The next stage of love is called خُلۃ and it is the level of the خلیل . The prophet said ان اللہ أِتَّخَذَنِی خَلِیلاً کَمَا التَّخَذَ ابراھیمَ خلیلا Allah took me as a خلیل just as he took Ibrahim as a خلیل . This is the highest level of the love where the love: تَتَخَلَّلُو خَلَایَ الجَسَد Where the love completely permeates every cell of the body.

title: Muhabbah  Combination Of All
text: The final stage of love is called محبۃ There really is no word in the English language that can encompass the depth of this love. The word محبۃ comes from the root word حُب which means seed. It is a never ending, eternal kind of love.

title: Iblis Succeeds In His Mission
text: The Heights [7:22] [ سورۃ ۷] الاعراف ‏فَدَلَّىٰهُمَا بِغُرُورٍۢ ۚ فَلَمَّا ذَاقَا ٱلشَّجَرَةَ بَدَتْ لَهُمَا سَوْءَتُهُمَا وَطَفِقَا يَخْصِفَانِ عَلَيْهِمَا مِن وَرَقِ ٱلْجَنَّةِ ۖ وَنَادَىٰهُمَا رَبُّهُمَآ أَلَمْ أَنْهَكُمَا عَن تِلْكُمَا ٱلشَّجَرَةِ وَأَقُل لَّكُمَآ إِنَّ ٱلشَّيْطَنَ لَكُمَا عَدُوٌّۭ مُّبِينٌۭ ‎ ۲۲ So he misled them with deception. Then when they tasted of the tree, that which was hidden from them of their shame became manifest to them and they began to stick together the leaves of Paradise over themselves. And their Lord called out to them :"Did I not forbid you that tree and tell you: Verily, Shaitan is an open enemy unto you?" فَلَمَّا ذَاقَا ٱلشَّجَرَةَ The word اذا would indicate when they did it. حین would indicate the same. The word فلما means eventually when they did it. Adam and Hawwa never even reached the fruit. Just accessing the tree compromised their obedience, and they lost their paradisal garments. Allah did not remove their clothes. Their disobedience to Allah resulted in their loss of honor and exposed their weakness and ugliness. They بَدَتْ لَهُمَا سَوْءَتُهُمَا That which was hidden from them of their shame became manifest to them. وَطَفِقَا يَخْصِفَانِ عَلَيْهِمَا مِن وَرَقِ ٱلْجَنَّةِ They both try to wrap themselves using the leaves of جنۃ A state of desperate humiliation is being described here; revealing how their disobedience lowered them from royalty to being destitute. Allah calls upon them: وَنَادَىٰهُمَا رَبُّهُمَا أَلَمْ أَنْهَكُمَا عَن تِلْكُمَا ٱلشَّجَرَةِ وَأَقُل لَّكُمَآ إِنَّ ٱلشَّيْطَنَ لَكُمَا عَدُوٌّۭ مُّبِينٌۭ Here Allah uses the distant pointer تلک الشجرۃ - THAT tree, because of the distancing from Allah's mercy

title: Response Of Adam And Hawwa
text: The Heights [7:23] [ سورۃ ۷] الاعراف ‏قَالَا رَبَّنَا ظَلَمْنَآ أَنفُسَنَا وَإِن لَّمْ تَغْفِرْ لَنَا وَتَرْحَمْنَا لَنَكُونَنَّ مِنَ ٱلْخَسِرِينَ ‎ ۲۳ They said: "Our Lord! We have wronged ourselves. If You forgive us not, and bestow not upon us Your Mercy, we shall certainly be of the losers." They said, master we have wronged ourselves and if you don't forgive us for our mistake ; and if you don't have mercy on us, we will definitely become from the losers. There is a staunch difference here, between Adam and Iblis. They both disobeyed Allah. Iblis did not do something he was commanded to do Adam and Hawwa did something they were commanded not to do There is disobedience of commission and there is disobedience of omission . The lesson from these verses is in the response of Iblis and Adam Iblis responded with arrogance - and a complete lack of responsibility Adam & Hawwa responded with humility - completely taking responsibility for this mistake Iblis tempts us to blame others for our mistakes and not take responsibility for our actions We cannot help but make mistakes. It is our response to our mistake that makes us Adam or Iblis The Heights [7:24-25] [ سورۃ ۷] الاعراف ‏قَالَ ٱهْبِطُوا۟ بَعْضُكُمْ لِبَعْضٍ عَدُوٌّۭ ۖ وَلَكُمْ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ مُسْتَقَرٌّۭ وَمَتَعٌ إِلَىٰ حِينٍۢ ‎ ۲۴ ‏قَالَ فِيهَا تَحْيَوْنَ وَفِيهَا تَمُوتُونَ وَمِنْهَا تُخْرَجُونَ ‎ ۲۵ ( 24 ) [ Allah ] said, "Descend, being to one another enemies. And for you on the earth is a place of settlement and enjoyment for a time." ( 25 ) He said: "Therein you shall live, and therein you shall die, and from it you shall be brought out."

title: How Did Iblis Humiliate Adam?
text: The Heights [7:20] [ سورۃ ۷] الاعراف ‏فَوَسْوَسَ لَهُمَا ٱلشَّيْطَنُ لِيُبْدِىَ لَهُمَا مَا وُۥرِىَ عَنْهُمَا مِن سَوْءَتِهِمَا ‎ ۲۰ Then Satan whispered suggestions to them both in order to uncover that which was hidden from them of their private parts The anger of Iblis comes from his feeling of humiliation that came from his dismissal of جنۃ The phrase فَوَسْوَسَ لَهُمَا indicates that he whispered to them with a very specific intent The لام before يُبْدِیَ . points to his specific reason which was to expose that which was covered of their ugliness. The agenda of Iblis is for mankind to lose their clothing Allah created us, he referred to us as بشر . Al-Hijr [15:28] [ سورۃ ۱۵] الحجر ‏وَإِذْ قَالَ رَبُّكَ لِلْمَلَٓئِكَةِ إِنِّى خَلِقٌۢ بَشَرًۭا مِّن صَلْصَلٍۢ مِّنْ حَمَإٍۢ مَّسْنُونٍۢ ‎ ۲۸ And when your Lord said to the angels: "I am going to create a man from sounding clay of altered black smooth mud. The word بشر comes from بِشَر which means skin. Allah adorned us with the gift of clothing - Iblis hates us and wants our honor removed at all cost The word سوئا is used in Arabic for something that is Ugly, disgusting and evil Weak

title: Reason Meets Revelation
text: The Family of Imran [3:190-194] [ سورۃ ۳] آل عمران ‏إِنَّ فِى خَلْقِ ٱلسَّمَوَتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ وَٱخْتِلَفِ ٱلَّيْلِ وَٱلنَّهَارِ لَآيَتٍۢ لِّأُو۟لِى ٱلْأَلْبَبِ ‎ ۱۹۰ Verily! In the creation of the heavens and the earth, and in the alternation of night and day, there are indeed signs for men of understanding. ‏ٱلَّذِينَ يَذْكُرُونَ ٱللَّهَ قِيَمًۭا وَقُعُودًۭا وَعَلَىٰ جُنُوبِهِمْ وَيَتَفَكَّرُونَ فِى خَلْقِ ٱلسَّمَوَتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ رَبَّنَا مَا خَلَقْتَ هَذَا بَطِلًۭا سُبْحَنَكَ فَقِنَا عَذَابَ ٱلنَّارِ ‎ ۱۹۱ Those who remember Allah standing, sitting, and lying down on their sides, and think deeply about the creation of the heavens and the earth, :"Our Lord! You have not created this without purpose, glory to You!. Give us salvation from the torment of the Fire. ‏رَبَّنَآ إِنَّكَ مَن تُدْخِلِ ٱلنَّارَ فَقَدْ أَخْزَيْتَهُۥ ۖ وَمَا لِلظَّلِمِينَ مِنْ أَنصَارٍۢ ‎ ۱۹۲ "Our Lord! Verily, whom You admit to the Fire, indeed, You have disgraced him, and never will the Zalimun find any helpers. ‏رَّبَّنَآ إِنَّنَا سَمِعْنَا مُنَادِيًۭا يُنَادِى لِلْإِيمَنِ أَنْ آمِنُوا۟ بِرَبِّكُمْ فَـَٔامَنَّا ۚ رَبَّنَا فَٱغْفِرْ لَنَا ذُنُوبَنَا وَكَفِّرْ عَنَّا سَيِّـَٔاتِنَا وَتَوَفَّنَا مَعَ ٱلْأَبْرَارِ ‎ ۱۹۳ "Our Lord! Verily, we have heard the call of one calling to Faith: 'Believe in your Lord,' and we have believed. Our Lord! Forgive us our sins and remit from us our evil deeds, and make us die in the state of righteousness along with Al-Abrar . ‏رَبَّنَا وَآتِنَا مَا وَعَدتَّنَا عَلَىٰ رُسُلِكَ وَلَا تُخْزِنَا يَوْمَ ٱلْقِيَمَةِ ۗ إِنَّكَ لَا تُخْلِفُ ٱلْمِيعَادَ ‎ ۱۹۴ "Our Lord! Grant us what You promised unto us through Your Messengers and disgrace us not on the Day of Resurrection, for You never break Promise."

title: Compassion
text: Compassion towards others Allah enables us to show compassion to people in tribulation Whoever relieves a calamity of this world for a believer, Allah will relieve a calamity for him on the day of judgment یوم القیامۃ . So people in tribulation become an opportunity for a believer. Because one of the purposes of suffering, is that it engenders, and activates the virtue of vulnerability within us; and how we respond to that. Calamities bring out the best or worst in humanity It helps us appreciate the suffering of others that we've been unable to really experience until today. In one of the narrations عیسی علیہ السلام was reported to have said الناسُ مُعافٌ أو مُبتَلا، فَرحَمُ اھل البلآءِ والشکُر اللہَ علی العافیۃ People are in one of two conditions: In peace or tribulation. Show compassion to people in tribulation and be grateful if you're in the state of well-being Experience Compassion Of Allah There is a very strange HADEES in دعائم الاسلام - that when you visit a sick man, ask him to pray for you. This is because Allah is closest to his slave and extremely compassionate to his needs when he or she is in a state of tribulation. It is a grave sin to despair in the mercy of Allah The Troops [39:53] [ سورۃ ۳۹] الزمر ‏ قُلْ يَعِبَادِىَ ٱلَّذِينَ أَسْرَفُوا۟ عَلَىٰٓ أَنفُسِهِمْ لَا تَقْنَطُوا۟ مِن رَّحْمَةِ ٱللَّهِ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ يَغْفِرُ ٱلذُّنُوبَ جَمِيعًا ۚ إِنَّهُۥ هُوَ ٱلْغَفُورُ ٱلرَّحِيمُ ‎ ۵۳ Say, "O My servants who have transgressed against themselves [by sinning], do not despair of the mercy of Allah . Indeed, Allah forgives all sins. Indeed, it is He who is the Forgiving, the Merciful."

title: Contentment
text: The last benefit is achieving the state of contentment with Allah(Subhanahu wa Ta'ala) - الرضی . الرِّضی مُوجِبُ لِرِضوَانِ اللہ تعالی ۔ فأنَّ المَصَائِبَ تَنْزِلُ بالبَرِّ و الفاجِر To be content with the رضی of Allah(Subhanahu wa Ta'ala); for tribulations afflict the good and evil.

title: What Is A Calamity?
text: The Arabic word for a calamity or trial is مصیبۃ . The term مصیبت can apply to minor events as well as major catastrophes. Does not apply to self-inflected situations Defined as an unwanted event that is from the decree of Allah that occurs in two ways: It is when you receive something that you do not want It is when do not receive something you do want There are two appropriate responses to a calamity Allah has provided the means to overcome it - respond with gratitude ( شکر ) You do not have the means to overcome it - respond with patience ( صبر ) There are 3 groups of people in this world in terms of dealing with calamities Common people ( عوام الناس ) Believers The Elite (خاص) High ranking believers The Elite of the Elite (خاص الخاص) Prophets and Imams

title: Our Purpose In Life
text: Allah says: The Winnowing Winds [51:56] [ سورۃ ۵۱] الذاریات ‏وَمَا خَلَقْتُ ٱلْجِنَّ وَٱلْإِنسَ إِلَّا لِيَعْبُدُونِ ‎ ۵۶ The essence of worship is توحید . The recognition of Allah(Subhanahu wa Ta'ala) End goal of عبادۃ is spiritual elevation There is nothing wrong about enjoying the pleasures of the world as long as the final goal is not compromised, which is إِلَّا لِيَعْبُدُونِ - recognizing our creator, and achieving spiritual elevation.

title: Linguistic View Of Calamity
text: I want to start by taking a linguistic look at this word مصیبۃ . This word مصیبۃ , is a Quranic word that Allah uses quite a bit in the Quran. The Cow [2:156] [ سورۃ ۲] البقرۃ ‏ٱلَّذِينَ إِذَآ أَصَبَتْهُم مُّصِيبَةٌۭ قَالُوٓا۟ إِنَّا لِلَّهِ وَإِنَّآ إِلَيْهِ رَجِعُونَ ‎ ۱۵۶ Who, when disaster strikes them, say, "Indeed we belong to Allah, and indeed to Him we will return." The word مصیبۃ - comes from the Arabic verb أصابۃ - It is an archery term It means to hit a target perfectly. Another derivative is صواب , which means correct, or accurate. The ۃ مربوطۃ - at the end adds the concept of حدف a target. Every single cause and effect leading to the calamity was precisely intended

title: Summary
text: Principle Meaning Quran 1 الحد Definition Quran is the speech of Allah(Subhanahu wa Ta'ala), revealed to his final messenger ( صلى الله عليه و سلم ) in the Arabic language, through the medium of جبرئیل علیہ السلام 2 موضوع Subject Essentially deals with 3 subjects: Rights of Allah (حقوق اللہ), Rights of the self (حقوق النفس), Rights of others (حقوق العباد) 3 ثمرۃ Benefit To know our creator, and not make mistakes in our understanding of him 4 فضل Virtue It provides us with peace of mind and contentment of the heart in this world 5 نسبۃ Associations It is related to, and is at the center of all branches of Islam 6 واضع Sources Its primary source is Allah(Subhanahu wa Ta'ala). Other sources include جبرئیل علیہ السلام, prophet Muhammad (Salallahu alayhi wa aalihee wa sallam), Maulana Ali (AS), IMAMS, DUA'T, MANASEEB, HUDOOD 7 أسم Identifiers It provides with the names and terminology that help us understand realities of this seen world, as well as the unseen meta-physical world. 8 أستمداد Principles All wisdom of Quran originate from توحید , which is the unity and the one-ness of Allah(Subhanahu wa Ta'ala) 9 حکم الشارع Legal rulings Acquisition of this knowledge is obligatory for all Muslim men and women. 10 مسائل Details The actual details of the Quran including its stories

title: Forgiveness
text: One of the most amazing qualities of the womb (the رحم ) is that it expands to accommodate the needs of the fetus. It provides a beautiful reminder that Allah's رحمۃ expands to fulfill not only the needs of his creation, through the forgiveness of their shortcomings, sins and transgressions. His immense forgiveness is one of the manifestations of his infinite compassion towards his creation. The Troops [39:53] [ سورۃ ۳۹] الزمر ‏ قُلْ يَعِبَادِىَ ٱلَّذِينَ أَسْرَفُوا۟ عَلَىٰٓ أَنفُسِهِمْ لَا تَقْنَطُوا۟ مِن رَّحْمَةِ ٱللَّهِ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ يَغْفِرُ ٱلذُّنُوبَ جَمِيعًا ۚ إِنَّهُۥ هُوَ ٱلْغَفُورُ ٱلرَّحِيمُ ‎ ۵۳ Say, "O My servants who have transgressed against themselves [by sinning], do not despair of the mercy of Allah . Indeed, Allah forgives all sins. Indeed, it is He who is the Forgiving, the Merciful."

title: Reliance On Allah
text: Calamities help us develop our reliance on Allah - توکل علی اللہ . مَا فی نِعمَتِھا مِنَ الفوائدَ الخَفِیَّۃ What is hidden inside the folds of these calamities are blessings. That is another benefit. The Cow [2:216] [ سورۃ ۲] البقرۃ ‏كُتِبَ عَلَيْكُمُ ٱلْقِتَالُ وَهُوَ كُرْهٌۭ لَّكُمْ ۖ وَعَسَىٰٓ أَن تَكْرَهُوا۟ شَيْـًۭٔا وَهُوَ خَيْرٌۭ لَّكُمْ ۖ وَعَسَىٰٓ أَن تُحِبُّوا۟ شَيْـًۭٔا وَهُوَ شَرٌّۭ لَّكُمْ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ يَعْلَمُ وَأَنتُمْ لَا تَعْلَمُونَ ‎ ۲۱۶ Jihad is ordained for you though you dislike it, and it may be that you dislike a thing which is good for you and that you like a thing which is bad for you. Allah knows but you do not know.

title: Forgiveness Through Repentance
text: Seeking Forgiveness Calamities engenders the need for forgiveness through repentance. Am I ready to stand before Allah? If not, realize the need to seek forgiveness. Forgiving Others Provides opportunity to opens up our hearts to forgive others. Calamities turns enemies into friends There is a great reward associated with forgiving people; especially those who have done you a wrong The Family of Imran [3:134] [ سورۃ ۳] آل عمران ‏ٱلَّذِينَ يُنفِقُونَ فِى ٱلسَّرَّآءِ وَٱلضَّرَّآءِ وَٱلْكَظِمِينَ ٱلْغَيْظَ وَٱلْعَافِينَ عَنِ ٱلنَّاسِ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ يُحِبُّ ٱلْمُحْسِنِينَ ‎ ۱۳۴ Who spend [in the cause of Allah ] during ease and hardship and who restrain anger and who forgive people - and Allah loves the ones who do beautiful deeds Counsel [42:40] [ سورۃ ۴۲] الشوری ‏وَجَزَٓؤُا۟ سَيِّئَةٍۢ سَيِّئَةٌۭ مِّثْلُهَا ۖ فَمَنْ عَفَا وَأَصْلَحَ فَأَجْرُهُۥ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ لَا يُحِبُّ ٱلظَّلِمِينَ ‎ ۴۰

title: Two Manifestations Of Mercy
text: There are two famous derivations of the word رحمۃ Both الرحمن and الرحیم are manifestations of the word رحمۃ . When we accept him as having رحمۃ we essentially acknowledge these 4 qualities about Allah. Unconditional love Constant Care Genuine concern Mercy and compassion Allah states this fact over and over in the Quran وَمِن رَّحْمَتِهِۦ جَعَلَ لَكُمُ ٱلَّيْلَ وَٱلنَّهَارَ Out of his love. he created the night and the day مِّن رَّحْمَتِهِۦ وَيُهَيِّئْ لَكُم مِّنْ أَمْرِكُم مِّرْفَقًۭا Out of his love he will make your tasks easy for you وَمِنْ آيَتِهِۦٓ أَن يُرْسِلَ ٱلرِّيَاحَ مُبَشِّرَتٍۢ وَلِيُذِيقَكُم مِّن رَّحْمَتِهِ Out of his love he sends the winds that delivers good tidings, so that you can recognize his love for you مَّا يَفْتَحِ ٱللَّهُ لِلنَّاسِ مِن رَّحْمَةٍۢ فَلَا مُمْسِكَ لَهَاۖ When Allah opens the doors of his love for someone, nothing can close them The purpose of all such verses is to to explain that the last thing you can expect from a worldly master is him having رحمۃ for his slaves No other master in this world will show unconditional love to their slaves, even when they do take care of their slaves once in a while Masters will always show love to their own, never to their slave.

title: Womb  Reham
text: The word رحمۃ comes from the word رَحَم which means the womb of a mother when she is carrying a child. The womb of a mother describes the relationship between Allah and his creation. This is what makes it so beautiful. Allah spoke to the womb and said: اِنِّی سَمَّیتُکَ بِاسْمی - I named you from my names. He made the womb a symbol of his رحمۃ and his unconditional love, because every single human being that will exist through time, will come through the womb

title: Comparison of womb with mercy
text: There is no concept of punishment for the child in the womb of its mother The womb always exists within every woman before and after the baby is born The child is completely being taken care of within the womb of its mother The child is offered complete protection The mother puts up with a lot of difficulties for her child The mother gives everything to the child, while the child gives back nothing The womb grows in size to accommodate the needs of the child After the child is born, the mother only increases in her love for the child Right after the birth the mother has to begin feeding her child - no time off! Although a mother child relationship is incredibly beautiful, it is also an extremely insane relationship

title: Verses Related To Calamity
text: Ya Sîn [36:36] [ سورۃ ۳۶] یس ‏سُبْحَنَ ٱلَّذِى خَلَقَ ٱلْأَزْوَجَ كُلَّهَا مِمَّا تُنۢبِتُ ٱلْأَرْضُ وَمِنْ أَنفُسِهِمْ وَمِمَّا لَا يَعْلَمُونَ ‎ ۳۶ We appreciate what we have by experiencing its opposite. We take blessings for granted Abraham [14:7] [ سورۃ ۱۴] ابراھیم ‏وَإِذْ تَأَذَّنَ رَبُّكُمْ لَىِٕن شَكَرْتُمْ لَأَزِيدَنَّكُمْ ۖ وَلَىِٕن كَفَرْتُمْ إِنَّ عَذَابِى لَشَدِيدٌۭ ‎ ۷ The Cow [2:155-157] [ سورۃ ۲] البقرۃ ‏وَلَنَبْلُوَنَّكُم بِشَىْءٍۢ مِّنَ ٱلْخَوْفِ وَٱلْجُوعِ وَنَقْصٍۢ مِّنَ ٱلْأَمْوَلِ وَٱلْأَنفُسِ وَٱلثَّمَرَتِ ۗ وَبَشِّرِ ٱلصَّبِرِينَ ‎ ۱۵۵ ‏ٱلَّذِينَ إِذَآ أَصَبَتْهُم مُّصِيبَةٌۭ قَالُوٓا۟ إِنَّا لِلَّهِ وَإِنَّآ إِلَيْهِ رَجِعُونَ ‎ ۱۵۶ ‏أُو۟لَٓئِكَ عَلَيْهِمْ صَلَوَتٌۭ مِّن رَّبِّهِمْ وَرَحْمَةٌۭ ۖ وَأُو۟لَٓئِكَ هُمُ ٱلْمُهْتَدُونَ ‎ ۱۵۷ ( 155 ) And We will surely test you with something of fear and hunger and a los wealth and lives and fruits, but give good tidings to the patient, ( 156 ) Who, when disaster strikes them, say, "Indeed we belong to Allah, and indeed to Him we will return." ( 157 ) Those are the ones upon whom are blessings from their Lord and mercy. And it is those who are the [rightly] guided. The prophet (Salallahu alayhi wa aalihee wa sallam) said about plagues: They are an invasion of the Unseen World upon you, but in it are blessings for believers. A believer views calamity very differently from a disbeliever Calamities can be empowering Examples found in life of Rasul Allah(Salallahu alayhi wa aalihee wa sallam), Maulana Ali (AS) and Imam Hussain

title: The Spiritual Heart
text: Heart desires and craves attachment. In its default state it will attach to anything it finds pleasing or comforting Who is the primary target of the heart's attachment? To answer this question, Allah(Subhanahu wa Ta'ala) in his divine wisdom, he made the physical heart, a symbol for that spiritual heart. Only the spiritual heart can recognize Allah(Subhanahu wa Ta'ala). It is only through the remembrance of Allah that the heart finds peace The Thunder [13:28] [ سورۃ ۱۳] الرعد ‏ٱلَّذِينَ آمَنُوا۟ وَتَطْمَىِٕنُّ قُلُوبُهُم بِذِكْرِ ٱللَّهِ ۗ أَلَا بِذِكْرِ ٱللَّهِ تَطْمَىِٕنُّ ٱلْقُلُوبُ ‎ ۲۸ Those who have believed and whose hearts are satisfied by the remembrance of Allah. Unquestionably! It is by the remembrance of Allah hearts are satisfied

title: Validity Of Taveel
text: This document summarizes the author's argument for the necessity and validity of interpreting religious texts beyond their literal meaning. It highlights how all things have an inner and outer aspect, drawing parallels from creation and prophetic teachings. The Necessity of Interpretation (Ta'wil) The book "Limits of Knowledge" extensively proved the science of interpretation ( علم التأويل ) and the inner meaning ( الباطن ) of religious texts. This current book will also provide arguments for interpretation for its readers. Every tangible thing possesses both an outer aspect ( ظاهر ), perceivable by the senses, and an inner aspect ( باطن ), encompassing its contents and associated knowledge. This aligns with the Quranic principle that "We created pairs of all things" (Quran). Just as a human is a body (outer) and a spirit (inner), everything, even if seemingly singular, has a pair to signify the Creator's oneness. The inner aspect is frequently mentioned in the Quran, for example, in verses about Allah's "apparent and hidden" ( ظاهرة وباطنة ) favors, and the command to "leave the outer and inner forms of sin" ( ظاهر الإثم وباطنه ). Neglecting the inner meaning of "blessings" ( النعيم ) or "sin" ( الإثم ) can lead to ignorance and misguidance. The Quran itself contains both clear verses ( محكمات ) and allegorical verses ( متشابهات ), and only Allah (SWT) and those "firm in knowledge" ( الراسخون في العلم ) understand their full interpretation. The Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) stated that every Quranic verse has an outer and an inner meaning ( ظهر وبطن ), a tradition universally accepted by the Muslim community. Understanding Parables and Prophetic Narratives The Quran uses parables ( الأمثال ) to convey deeper meanings, understood only by "those of knowledge" ( العالمون ). Examples of metaphorical language in the Quran include "life" meaning faith, "light" meaning guidance, and "darkness" meaning misguidance. Prophetic narratives ( الأحاديث ) also possess interpretation ( تأويل ), as exemplified by the story of Prophet Yusuf (Joseph), who was taught the "interpretation of narratives" ( تأويل الأحاديث ). The Quran's unique composition lies in its single words having both outer and inner meanings ( ظاهره وباطنه ). The outer meaning serves as the miracle of the Prophet Muhammad (PBUH), while the inner meaning is the miracle preserved with the Imams from his household. This extensive knowledge is safeguarded by the Imams, who impart it according to the audience's understanding, as per Allah's (SWT) command to "give or withhold without reckoning." Approach to Explanation This book will interpret the inner meanings discussed in "Pillars of Islam," tailoring the language to the reader's comprehension. The approach avoids the complex terminology of logicians and philosophers, aiming for clarity and accessibility. The author places reliance on Allah (SWT), our Lord, as He is sufficient for those who trust Him.

title: Book Of Faith
text: Islam and Iman: A Unified Foundation This session examines the relationship between الإسلام (outward submission) and الإيمان (inner faith), asserting that they originate from the same source: submission to Allah. The core practices of الإسلام include the five pillars: شهادة (testimony of faith), صلاة (prayer), زكاة (charity), صوم (fasting), and حج (pilgrimage). الإيمان is the internal conviction, expressed through belief in the heart ( تصدیق بالجنان ), affirmation with the tongue ( قول بالسان ), and action through the limbs ( عمل بالارکان ). The relationship is likened to a tree: الإسلام is the visible part (trunk, branches, leaves), while الإيمان is the unseen root system, with both being essential. The Outward and Inward Foundations of Messengers Each Messenger of strong will ( أُولِي الْعَزْمِ ) brought a divine law ( شريعة ) with two foundations: an outward ( ظاهر ) one and an inward ( باطن ) one. These foundations, though different in appearance, share a single essence. Prophet Noah ( نوح ): His foundation was the Ark, a physical vessel representing salvation for those who believed and entered it. Prophet Ibrahim ( إِبْرَاهِيم ): His foundation was the Kaaba, the focal point for pilgrimage and devotion. Prophet Moses ( مُوسَى ): His foundation was the staff, a symbol of divine power overcoming falsehood during his confrontation with the Pharaoh's magicians. Prophet Jesus ( عِيسَى ): His foundation was the cross, which Christians see as a symbol of sacrifice and commitment, representing the willingness to endure hardship for faith. Prophet Muhammad ( مُحَمَّد ): His foundation is the testimony of faith, شهادة أن لا إله إلا الله , which serves as the core of the divine law and invites people to believe in it. The All-Encompassing Nature of the Shahada The testimony of faith ( شهادة ) is not merely a verbal statement but a comprehensive principle that contains all the requirements of Islamic law. A common misconception is that the شهادة was a simple first step to ease people into Islam, with harder duties like Jihad added later. However, this is considered a form of deception that Allah would not employ; true allegiance requires a full understanding from the beginning. Sincerity ( الإخلاص ) in the شهادة means believing in it and knowing its limits ( اعْتِقَادُهَا وَمَعْرِفَةُ حُدُودِهَا ), as stated by Prophet Muhammad. The Prophet's teaching confirms that الإيمان is "speech by the tongue, confirmation by the heart, and action by the limbs," which means the شهادة must be accompanied by belief and righteous deeds. The testimony is so profound that it encompasses the Oneness of Allah ( توحيد ), the affirmation of His Messenger, and the Imams of the religion. It obligates obedience and acceptance of their authority ( ولاية ). The Significance of the Covenant and Testimony The declaration of faith is a sacred covenant that requires genuine understanding and commitment. Just as a legal testimony is invalid without full knowledge, the شهادة is not accepted without sincerity, understanding, and action. The text references the story of Prophet Ibrahim, who declared: " أسلمت لرب العالمين " (I have submitted to the Master of the worlds), highlighting submission as the core of faith. The testimony ( شهادة ) is a miracle and the foundation of all divine laws because it contains everything by which Allah is to be worshipped. The text concludes with a call to "empty your hearts" and reflect deeply on this wisdom, allowing its truths to illuminate one's understanding.

title: Interpretation Of Faith
text: فَهَذِهِ شَهَادَةُ أَنْ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ الَّتِي تُوجِبُ فِي ظَاهِرِهَا، أَنَّ اللَّهَ تَعَالَى هُوَ الْإِلَهُ الْوَاحِدُ الْأَحَدُ لَا شَرِيكَ لَهُ، قَدْ تَفَرَّدَ بِالْإِلَهِيَّةِ، وَبَانَ بِالْوَحْدَانِيَّةِ دُونَ جَمِيعِ خَلْقِهِ. This testifies that there is no deity but Allah (SWT), which outwardly implies that Allah the Exalted is the One, a unique deity with no partner. He stands alone in divinity and is distinct in oneness, separate from all of His creation. وَقَدْ يَقَعُ اسْمُ الْوَاحِدِ عَلَى الْمَخْلُوقِ، وَيَقُومُ ذَلِكَ فِي الْعَيَانِ الظَّاهِرِ. The term 'one' can refer to a created being, as is evident. فَإِذَا رَجَعَ مِنْهُ إِلَى الْبَاطِنِ، كَانَ مُزْدَوِجًا مُحْتَاجًا إِلَى مَا زَاوَجَهُ وَقَارَنَهُ، كَالْإِنْسَانِ وَغَيْرِهِ مِنَ الْحَيَوَانِ، . However, shifting the focus from its external appearance to its internal essence reveals a partnership — a harmony of body and spirit, as seen in humans and animals. وَهُوَ شَخْصٌ وَاحِدٌ فِي الْعَدَدِ، وَقَائِمٌ كَذَلِكَ لِلْعَيَانِ، فَإِذَا اعْتُبِرَ كَانَ جِسْمًا وَرُوحًا، فَالْجِسْمُ هُوَ الظَّاهِرُ، وَالرُّوحُ هِيَ الْبَاطِنُ، وَقَدْ ذَكَرْنَا ذَلِكَ فِيمَا تَقَدَّمَ At first glance, it appears as a single entity. However, on closer examination, it comprises both a body, symbolizing the external, and a spirit, reflecting the internal, as discussed earlier. وَكَذَلِكَ كُلُّ شَيْءٍ فِي الْعَالَمِ فَلَا يَخْلُو مِنْ أَنْ يَكُونَ فِيهِ جَوْهَرَانِ مُرَكَّبَانِ، إِمَّا حَارٌّ وَرَطْبٌ، وَإِمَّا حَارٌّ وَيَابِسٌ، أَوْ بَارِدٌ وَرَطْبٌ، أَوْ بَارِدٌ وَيَابِسٌ، لَا يَقُومُ إِلَّا بِذَلِكَ. Similarly, all things in the world fall into two categories: either hot and wet, hot and dry, cold and wet, or cold and dry; they exist solely through these combinations. وَلَا يَخْلُو مِنْ حَالَتَيْنِ إِمَّا أَنْ يَكُونَ شَاهِدًا أَوْ غَائِبًا. And it is not without having two states: either being present or being absent. وَلَا مِنْ صُورَتَيْنِ إِمَّا أَنْ يَكُونَ قَلِيلًا أَوْ كَثِيرًا. And not without possessing two quantities: either being little or being a lot. وَلَا مِنْ نَوْعَيْنِ إِمَّا أَنْ يَكُونَ حَيَوَانًا أَوْ إِنْسَانًا، أَوْ لَطِيفًا أَوْ كَثِيفًا، أَوْ ثَقِيلًا أَوْ خَفِيفًا، وَإِنَّ التَّضَادَّ وَالتَّغَايُرَ كَثِيرٌ يَطُولُ ذِكْرُهُ. And not without recognizing two categories: either as an animal or a human, or subtle or dense, or heavy or light. The oppositions and differences are numerous and extensive in detail. فَالْبَارِي جَلَّ ذِكْرُهُ بَائِنٌ عَنْ ذَلِكَ كُلِّهِ مِنْ أَحْوَالِ خَلْقِهِ، So the Creator, Glorified be His mention, is distinct from all of that concerning the states of His creation. غَيْرُ مَوْصُوفٍ بِشَيْءٍ مِنْهَا، He is not described by anything from them. لَا مَوْجُودٌ غَيْرُ عَدَمٍ، Not existent as non-existent, وَلَا عَدَمٌ غَيْرُ مَوْجُودٍ، Nor non-existent as existent. بَلْ هُوَ مَوْجُودٌ، Rather, He is Existent. ثَبَتَ بِآيَاتِهِ وَدَلَائِلِ مَا خَلَقَ، عَلَى أَنَّهُ بَائِنٌ عَنْ جَمِيعِهَا He is proven by His signs and the indications of what He created that He is distinct from all of them. لَيْسَ يُشْبِهُهُ شَيْءٌ مِنْهَا، Nothing from them resembles Him. وَلَيْسَ كَمِثْلِهِ شَيْءٌ. And there is nothing like Him. قَالَ اللَّهُ تَعَالَى: ﴿ لَيْسَ كَمِثْلِهِ شَيْءٌ ﴾. Allah the Exalted said: "Nothing is like Him." وَهَذَا كُلُّهُ لَهُ شَرْحٌ يَأْتِي ذِكْرُهُ إِنْ شَاءَ اللَّهُ تَعَالَى فِي حَدِّهِ، وَقَدْ يَطُولُ لَوْ ذَكَرْنَاهُ، وَيُقْطَعُ مِنْ شَرْحِ الشَّهَادَةِ. All of this has an explanation that will be mentioned at the appropriate time if Allah (SWT) wills. It would be lengthy if we covered it now, and it would disrupt the explanation of the testimony. When we declare these words, we are outwardly affirming that Allah(SWT) is the One true Deity, absolutely unique, with no partner whatsoever. Now, think about how we use the word 'one' in our everyday experience. We might point to a person, an animal, or an object and call it 'one.' Outwardly, it appears as a single unit. But if we look closer, beneath the surface, we find complexity, a combination of elements. A person, for instance, is outwardly one individual, but inwardly, we understand them as a combination of body and spirit – the visible and the invisible working together. The body is the outward form (the ظاهر), and the spirit is the inner reality (the باطن). This same principle extends to everything in the created world. Nothing exists in absolute simplicity. Everything exists within pairs of possibilities or states: something is either present or absent, it's either a little or a lot, it can be subtle or dense, heavy or light. Think of all the opposites and contrasts that define our reality. This inherent duality or composition is a characteristic of creation. Allah(SWT) is entirely distinct from all these characteristics of His creation. He cannot be described using these pairs, these combinations, these limitations. His Oneness isn't like the 'oneness' of a created thing that is made up of parts or defined by its opposite. His existence isn't like ours, which comes after non-existence or could potentially cease. No, He simply is Existent. His existence is absolute and proven through His signs and the wonders of the universe He created – precisely because the creation, in its complexity and duality, points towards an utterly unique Creator. Nothing in creation resembles Him. لَيْسَ كَمِثْلِهِ شَيْءٌ – "There is nothing whatsoever like unto Him." Similarly, the Shahada appears straightforward and singular at first glance, much like a human being - there is no god but Allah. That defines Islam. Yet, upon examining its parts and delving into ایمان , numerous layers emerge, indicating a distinct hierarchy. The Testimony وَجَدْتُ الشَّهَادَةَ عَلَى فَصْلَيْنِ: أَحَدُهُمَا نَفْيٌ، وَالثَّانِي إِثْبَاتٌ. And I found the testimony to be in two parts: one of them is negation, and the second is affirmation. فَقَوْلُهُ: (لَا إِلَهَ) نَفْيٌ وَ (إِلَّا اللَّهُ) إِثْبَاتٌ. So, his saying (`La ilaha`), "There is no deity," is a negation, and (`illa Allah`), "except Allah," is an affirmation. وَكُلُّ فَصْلٍ مِنْهَا كَلِمَتَانِ، الْأُولَى مِنْهَا مُتَّصِلَةٌ بِالثَّانِيَةِ، وَدُونَهَا، وَالثَّانِيَةُ أَعْلَى مِنْهَا. Each part has two 'words' (conceptual parts): the first is connected to the second and is positioned below it, while the second is positioned higher than it. فَمَثَلُ الْحَدِّ الْأَخِيرِ الَّذِي هُوَ الْإِثْبَاتُ فِي الْبَاطِنِ، مَثَلٌ عَلَى الْحَدِّ الْعُلْوِيِّ وَمَثَلُ الْحَدِّ الَّذِي هُوَ النَّفْيُ فِي الْبَاطِنِ مَثَلٌ عَلَى الْحَدِّ السُّفْلِيِّ. The likeness of the last limit, which represents affirmation in the inward aspect, serves as a likeness for the upper limit. Conversely, the likeness of the limit representing negation in the inward aspect functions as a likeness for the lower limit. وَمَثَلُ الْكَلِمَةِ الْأَخِيرَةِ مِنَ الْفَصْلِ الْأَخِيرِ (أَيِ الْإِثْبَاتِ) مَثَلٌ عَلَى الْحُدُودِ الْعُلْوِيَّةِ، وَمَثَلُ الَّتِي قَبْلَهَا مَثَلُ الْحَدِّ الَّذِي يَلِيهِ. The likeness of the final 'word' in the last section (representing affirmation) symbolizes the upper boundaries, while the likeness preceding it corresponds to the limit that follows it (beneath it). وَمَثَلُ الْكَلِمَةِ الثَّانِيَةِ مِنَ الْفَصْلِ الْأَوَّلِ فِي الْبَاطِنِ مَثَلٌ عَلَى الْحُدُودِ السُّفْلِيَّةِ، وَمَثَلُ الَّتِي قَبْلَهَا مَثَلٌ عَلَى الْحَدِّ الْمُتَّصِلِ بِهِ دُونَهُ. And the likeness of the second 'word' from the first part, in the inward aspect, is a likeness for the lower limits, and the likeness of the one before it is a likeness for the limit connected to it but below it. فَالْكَلِمَتَانِ الْمُتَّصِلَتَانِ فِي حَدِّ الْإِثْبَاتِ وَحَدِّ النَّفْيِ لِكَلِمَتَيْنِ مُسْتَعْمَلَتَيْنِ وَهُمَا لَا وَإِلَّا So the two connected words in the sphere of affirmation and the sphere of negation are two commonly used words, and they are "No" ( لا ) and "Except" ( إلا ). وَالْكَلِمَتَانِ الْمُتَّصِلَتَانِ بِهِمَا مِنَ الْحَدَّيْنِ أَعْلَى وَأَجَلُّ، وَهُمَا إِلَهَ وَاللَّهُ، The two words connected to them from the two spheres are higher and more majestic, and they are "deity" ( إله ) and "Allah" ( الله ). فَقَدْ دَلَّ ذَلِكَ عَلَى أَشْرَفِ الْحُدُودِ الْعُلْوِيَّةِ وَالسُّفْلِيَّةِ، So that indicates the most noble of the higher and lower realms. وَإِنْ كَانَ كُلُّ وَاحِدٍ مِنَ الْآخَرَيْنِ مُتَّصِلًا بِهِ وَدُونَهُ وَأَقَلَّ حُرُوفٍ مِنْهُ، Even though each one of the other two is connected to it, is below it, and has fewer letters than it. وَإِنَّ الْكَلِمَةَ الْأَخِيرَةَ حَمَلَتْ ثَلَاثَةَ أَحْرُفٍ جُمِعَتْ فِيهَا كَافَّةُ حُرُوفِ الشَّهَادَةِ وَهِيَ: الْأَلِفُ وَاللَّامُ وَالْهَاءُ. And indeed, the last word carried three letters in which all the letters of the Testimony are gathered, and they are: the Alif, the Lam, and the Ha. The concepts of "negation" ( النفی La ilaha ) and "affirmation" ( الاثبات illa Allah ) within the Shahada are referred to as "limits" ( حدود - singular حدّ ) because they function as defining boundaries or demarcations of the core concept of Tawhid (the Oneness of Allah).
