Here's a breakdown of why: Defining Boundaries: The Arabic word حدّ literally means a boundary, limit, or definition. It refers to prescribed limits or boundaries set by Allah. Negation as a Lower Limit: The first part, La ilaha ("There is no deity"), acts as a "lower limit" ( الْحَدِّ السُّفْلِيِّ ). It sets a boundary by excluding and negating the possibility of divinity for anything or anyone besides Allah. It clears the ground, defining what is not God. Affirmation as an Upper Limit: The second part, illa Allah ("except Allah"), acts as the "upper limit" ( الْحَدِّ الْعُلْوِيِّ ). It sets a boundary by affirming and establishing that the sole right of divinity belongs exclusively to Allah. It defines the ultimate, singular Truth. Conceptual Hierarchy: The reason he uses the "upper" and "lower" terminology is to describe the components within these limits, positioning Allah as the highest and most sublime concept ("noblest of the upper limits"), representing the ultimate boundary of affirmation, while the negation ( La ilaha ) forms the foundational or "lower" boundary. Establishing Tawhid: Together, these two "limits" precisely define the concept of Tawhid. They establish the absolute boundary between belief in the One True God and disbelief or shirk (associating partners with God). The negation excludes all falsehood, and the affirmation establishes the singular Truth. In essence, they are called "limits" because they delineate the fundamental conceptual and theological boundaries that define the very core of Islamic belief as expressed in the Shahada. They mark the line between what is affirmed (Allah's sole divinity) and what is negated (any other claim to divinity). Two Main Sections: Negation (Denial): La ilaha ("There is no god") - This is considered the "lower" part. It clears away all false ideas of gods first. Affirmation (Confirmation): illa Allah ("except Allah") - This is considered the "higher" part. It confirms the one true God, Allah. Analogy: Think of cleaning a space (lower action) before placing something precious there (higher action). You deny/remove the clutter ( La ilaha ) before affirming/placing the precious item ( Allah ). Words within Each Section: Each section is further broken down into two "words" or concepts: In La ilaha : You have La (No) and ilaha (god). La comes first and is "below" ilaha in this structure. In illa Allah : You have illa (except) and Allah (The God). illa Comes first and is "below" Allah . Connection and Importance: These "words" are connected. La leads to ilaha , and illa leads to Allah . There's a hierarchy. Allah is the highest and most important concept. Ilaha (the general idea of a god being denied) is next. Then come the connecting words illa and La . Even though La and illa have fewer letters; they connect to the more significant concepts ( ilaha and Allah ). Simple Analogy: Imagine building with blocks. La is a base block. Ilaha sits on it. Illa is like a connector block leading upwards to the most crucial block, Allah , at the very top. What it Symbolizes: The structure represents a spiritual journey: Start by rejecting falsehoods (the lower limit/negation: La ilaha ), then move to affirm the ultimate truth (the upper limit/affirmation: Allah ). The final word, Allah , is described as the highest and most noble. The Word "Allah": The word Allah itself contains key letters (Alif, Lam, Ha) that represent the essence of the entire testimony of faith. Pen and the tablet وَهَذِهِ الْجُمْلَةُ مِنَ الْقَوْلِ فِي حُدُودِ الشَّهَادَةِ نُوَضِّحُهَا لِلسَّامِعِينَ بِفَضْلِ اللَّهِ عَلَيْنَا وَعَلَيْهِمْ فَنَقُولُ: In this summary of the discourse regarding the limits of testimony, we aim to clarify it for the listeners by the grace of Allah (SWT) upon us and them. Thus, we say: إِنَّ مَا رَوَاهُ الْخَاصُّ وَالْعَامُّ عَنْ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ (صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَآلِهِ وَسَلَّمَ) أَنَّهُ قَالَ: « أَوَّلُ مَا خَلَقَ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ مِنْ خَلْقِهِ الْقَلَمَ، ثُمَّ أَجْرَى نَهْرًا أَحْلَى مِنَ الْعَسَلِ، وَأَشَدَّ بَيَاضًا مِنَ اللَّبَنِ، وَأَلْيَنَ مِنَ الزُّبْدِ، ثُمَّ خَلَقَ اللَّوْحَ الْمَحْفُوظَ الَّذِي ذَكَرَهُ اللَّهُ تَعَالَى فِي كِتَابِهِ الْعَزِيزِ ». Both the select few and the general populace tell us that Muhammad PBUH said: "The first creation of Allah (SWT) Almighty and Majestic was the Pen. Then He caused a river to flow, which is sweeter than honey, whiter than milk, and softer than butter. After that, He created the Preserved Tablet, which Allah (SWT) the Exalted referenced in His Mighty Book." فَحَمَلَتِ الْعَامَّةُ هَذَا الْخَبَرَ عَلَى مَا تَعْرِفُهُ، مِنْ أَنَّ الْقَلَمَ مِنْ قَصَبٍ، وَأَنَّ اللَّوْحَ مِنْ خَشَبٍ عَلَى مَا نُشَاهِدُهُ، وَلَمْ تَدْرِ أَنَّ هَذِهِ هِيَ أَسْمَاءٌ وَأَلْقَابٌ وَأَمْثَالٌ تَحْتَاجُ إِلَى إِيضَاحٍ، وَكَشْفٍ وَبَيَانٍ وَتَأْوِيلٍ، وَذَلِكَ مِنْ تَأْوِيلِ الْأَحَادِيثِ الَّتِي ذَكَرَهَا اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ. The general public understood this report based on their existing knowledge- that the Pen is crafted from reed and the Tablet from wood, much like what is visible. However, they failed to recognize that these terms are names, titles, and allegories that need clarification, unveiling, explanation, and interpretation, which is essential to understanding the Hadiths mentioned by Allah (SWT), the Almighty, and the Majestic. فَالْقَلَمُ هُوَ أَعْلَى حَدٍّ مِنَ الْحُدُودِ الَّتِي حَدَّهَا اللَّهُ بِعِبَادِهِ، وَنَهَى عَنْ تَعَدِّيْهَا بِقَوْلِهِ سُبْحَانَهُ وَتَعَالَى: ﴿ وَمَنْ يَتَعَدَّ حُدُودَ اللَّهِ فَقَدْ ظَلَمَ نَفْسَهُ ﴾ So the Pen is the highest limit among the limits that Allah (SWT) set for His servants, and He forbade them from transgressing these limits by His saying, Glorified and Exalted is He: "And whoever transgresses the limits of Allah (SWT) has certainly wronged himself." وَارْتَفَعَ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ عَنِ الْحُدُودِ وَنَصَبَهَا لِخَلْقِهِ مِنْ دُونِهِ، لِيَتَوَصَّلُوا مِنْهَا إِلَى مَعْرِفَتِهِ وَيُؤَدُّوا بِوَاسِطَتِهَا إِلَى الْخَلْقِ أَمْرَهُ وَنَهْيَهُ. The Almighty and Majestic transcend all limits and have established boundaries for His creation below Him, enabling them to gain knowledge of Him and to communicate His commands and prohibitions to the creation through these limits. فَالْقَلَمُ هُوَ الْحَامِلُ لِلْعِلْمِ الَّذِي اسْتَمَدَّهُ مِنَ اللَّهِ سُبْحَانَهُ، وَثَبَّتَهُ فِي اللَّوْحِ، وَاللَّوْحُ حَدٌّ مِنْ حُدُودِ اللَّهِ وَدُونَهُ (أَيْ دُونَ الْقَلَمِ) أَخَذَ الْعِلْمَ عَنِ الْقَلَمِ، وَصَارَ إِلَيْهِ مِنْهُ، وَهُمَا الْحَدَّانِ الْعُلْوِيَّانِ اللَّذَانِ قَدَّمْنَا ذِكْرَهُمَا. The Pen serves as the vessel of knowledge obtained from Allah (SWT), and this knowledge is inscribed in the Tablet. The Tablet represents a boundary established by Allah (SWT) and lies beneath the Pen; it receives knowledge from the Pen, which is its source. These two entities are the upper limits we referenced previously. وَالنَّهْرُ الَّذِي أَجْرَاهُ اللَّهُ سُبْحَانَهُ وَتَعَالَى هُوَ الْقَلَمُ. The river that Allah (SWT) caused to flow is through the pen. قَالَ سُبْحَانَهُ وَتَعَالَى: ﴿ وَلَوِ اسْتَقَامُوا عَلَى الطَّرِيقَةِ لَأَسْقَيْنَاهُمْ مَاءً غَدَقًا ﴾. He, Glorified and Exalted is He, said: "And if they had remained on the path, We would have given them abundant water to drink." فَالطَّرِيقَةُ هِيَ النَّبِيُّ النَّاطِقُ فِي زَمَانِهِ، وَالْإِمَامُ فِي أَوَانِهِ مِنْ بَعْدِهِ، وَالْمَاءُ الْغَدَقُ هُوَ الْعِلْمُ الْغَزِيرُ. So, the path is the speaking Prophet ( لنَّبِيُّ النَّاطِقُ ) in his time and the Imam ( وَالْإِمَامُ ) in his era after him; the abundant water is plentiful spiritual knowledge. وَقَالَ سُبْحَانَهُ وَهُوَ أَصْدَقُ الْقَائِلِينَ: ﴿ وَلَقَدْ خَلَقْنَا فَوْقَكُمْ سَبْعَ طَرَائِقَ وَمَا كُنَّا عَنِ الْخَلْقِ غَافِلِينَ ﴾. And Allah(SWT) is the most truthful of speakers. He said: "And We have created above you seven paths, and We have never been unaware of the creation." ‏لِّنَفْتِنَهُمْ فِيهِ ۚ وَمَن يُعْرِضْ عَن ذِكْرِ رَبِّهِۦ يَسْلُكْهُ عَذَابًۭا صَعَدًۭا That We might try them thereby, and whosoever turns away from the Reminder of his Lord, He will cause him to enter in a severe torment. وَسَنَذْكُرُ هَذَا بِتَمَامِهِ فِي مَوْضِعِهِ. And we will mention this fully in its proper place. هَذَا مَا أَقَرَّتْ بِهِ الْعَامَّةُ مِنْ تَأْوِيلِ الْبَاطِنِ إِذْ إِنَّهَا لَمْ تَجِدْ مِنْهُ مَحِيصًا، أَعْنِي أَنَّهَا أَوَّلَتِ الْمَاءَ بِالْعِلْمِ لِأَنَّ الْمَاءَ الظَّاهِرَ سَقَاهُ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ إِلَى الْبَرِّ وَالْفَاجِرِ، فَأَخْبَرَ سُبْحَانَهُ أَنَّ الْخَلْقَ لَوِ اسْتَقَامُوا لِوَلِيِّ أَمْرِهِمْ لَأَوْسَعَهُمْ عِلْمًا. The general populace recognized this internal interpretation, realizing there was no way to escape it. They saw water as knowledge, for outward water is bestowed by Allah (SWT) to both the righteous and the wicked. Therefore, Allah(SWT) revealed that had creation remained steadfast in their obedience to their authority, He would have enhanced their knowledge. وَقَوْلُهُ سُبْحَانَهُ: ﴿ لِنَفْتِنَهُمْ فِيهِ ﴾ أَيْ نَمْتَحِنُهُمْ وَنُخْبِرُ قَوْلَهُمْ وَعِلْمَهُمْ بِهِ. His saying, "That We might test them in it," means that we test them and reveal their thoughts and knowledge concerning it. ثُمَّ قَالَ سُبْحَانَهُ: ﴿ وَمَنْ يُعْرِضْ عَنْ ذِكْرِ رَبِّهِ يَسْلُكْهُ عَذَابًا صَعَدًا ﴾. Then He said: "And whoever turns away from the remembrance of his Lord, He will put him into increasing punishment." فَذَكَرَ سُبْحَانَهُ هَهُنَا وَلِيَّ أَمْرِهِمْ مَنْ أَعْرَضَ عَنْهُ صَارَ إِلَى عَذَابِ اللَّهِ وَسَمَّاهُ ذِكْرًا أَيْ إِقَامَةً لِذِكْرِهِ فَسُمِّيَ بِاسْمِهِ. So Allah(SWT) mentioned their guardian of authority here; whoever turns away from him will face the punishment of Allah (SWT). He named him "Dhikr" (remembrance), meaning an establishment of His remembrance, so His name named him.

title: Interpretation Of Faith
text: فَهَذِهِ شَهَادَةُ أَنْ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ الَّتِي تُوجِبُ فِي ظَاهِرِهَا، أَنَّ اللَّهَ تَعَالَى هُوَ الْإِلَهُ الْوَاحِدُ الْأَحَدُ لَا شَرِيكَ لَهُ، قَدْ تَفَرَّدَ بِالْإِلَهِيَّةِ، وَبَانَ بِالْوَحْدَانِيَّةِ دُونَ جَمِيعِ خَلْقِهِ. This testifies that there is no deity but Allah (SWT), which outwardly implies that Allah the Exalted is the One, a unique deity with no partner. He stands alone in divinity and is distinct in oneness, separate from all of His creation. وَقَدْ يَقَعُ اسْمُ الْوَاحِدِ عَلَى الْمَخْلُوقِ، وَيَقُومُ ذَلِكَ فِي الْعَيَانِ الظَّاهِرِ. The term 'one' can refer to a created being, as is evident. فَإِذَا رَجَعَ مِنْهُ إِلَى الْبَاطِنِ، كَانَ مُزْدَوِجًا مُحْتَاجًا إِلَى مَا زَاوَجَهُ وَقَارَنَهُ، كَالْإِنْسَانِ وَغَيْرِهِ مِنَ الْحَيَوَانِ، . However, shifting the focus from its external appearance to its internal essence reveals a partnership — a harmony of body and spirit, as seen in humans and animals. وَهُوَ شَخْصٌ وَاحِدٌ فِي الْعَدَدِ، وَقَائِمٌ كَذَلِكَ لِلْعَيَانِ، فَإِذَا اعْتُبِرَ كَانَ جِسْمًا وَرُوحًا، فَالْجِسْمُ هُوَ الظَّاهِرُ، وَالرُّوحُ هِيَ الْبَاطِنُ، وَقَدْ ذَكَرْنَا ذَلِكَ فِيمَا تَقَدَّمَ At first glance, it appears as a single entity. However, on closer examination, it comprises both a body, symbolizing the external, and a spirit, reflecting the internal, as discussed earlier. وَكَذَلِكَ كُلُّ شَيْءٍ فِي الْعَالَمِ فَلَا يَخْلُو مِنْ أَنْ يَكُونَ فِيهِ جَوْهَرَانِ مُرَكَّبَانِ، إِمَّا حَارٌّ وَرَطْبٌ، وَإِمَّا حَارٌّ وَيَابِسٌ، أَوْ بَارِدٌ وَرَطْبٌ، أَوْ بَارِدٌ وَيَابِسٌ، لَا يَقُومُ إِلَّا بِذَلِكَ. Similarly, all things in the world fall into two categories: either hot and wet, hot and dry, cold and wet, or cold and dry; they exist solely through these combinations. وَلَا يَخْلُو مِنْ حَالَتَيْنِ إِمَّا أَنْ يَكُونَ شَاهِدًا أَوْ غَائِبًا. And it is not without having two states: either being present or being absent. وَلَا مِنْ صُورَتَيْنِ إِمَّا أَنْ يَكُونَ قَلِيلًا أَوْ كَثِيرًا. And not without possessing two quantities: either being little or being a lot. وَلَا مِنْ نَوْعَيْنِ إِمَّا أَنْ يَكُونَ حَيَوَانًا أَوْ إِنْسَانًا، أَوْ لَطِيفًا أَوْ كَثِيفًا، أَوْ ثَقِيلًا أَوْ خَفِيفًا، وَإِنَّ التَّضَادَّ وَالتَّغَايُرَ كَثِيرٌ يَطُولُ ذِكْرُهُ. And not without recognizing two categories: either as an animal or a human, or subtle or dense, or heavy or light. The oppositions and differences are numerous and extensive in detail. فَالْبَارِي جَلَّ ذِكْرُهُ بَائِنٌ عَنْ ذَلِكَ كُلِّهِ مِنْ أَحْوَالِ خَلْقِهِ، So the Creator, Glorified be His mention, is distinct from all of that concerning the states of His creation. غَيْرُ مَوْصُوفٍ بِشَيْءٍ مِنْهَا، He is not described by anything from them. لَا مَوْجُودٌ غَيْرُ عَدَمٍ، Not existent as non-existent, وَلَا عَدَمٌ غَيْرُ مَوْجُودٍ، Nor non-existent as existent. بَلْ هُوَ مَوْجُودٌ، Rather, He is Existent. ثَبَتَ بِآيَاتِهِ وَدَلَائِلِ مَا خَلَقَ، عَلَى أَنَّهُ بَائِنٌ عَنْ جَمِيعِهَا He is proven by His signs and the indications of what He created that He is distinct from all of them. لَيْسَ يُشْبِهُهُ شَيْءٌ مِنْهَا، Nothing from them resembles Him. وَلَيْسَ كَمِثْلِهِ شَيْءٌ. And there is nothing like Him. قَالَ اللَّهُ تَعَالَى: ﴿ لَيْسَ كَمِثْلِهِ شَيْءٌ ﴾. Allah the Exalted said: "Nothing is like Him." وَهَذَا كُلُّهُ لَهُ شَرْحٌ يَأْتِي ذِكْرُهُ إِنْ شَاءَ اللَّهُ تَعَالَى فِي حَدِّهِ، وَقَدْ يَطُولُ لَوْ ذَكَرْنَاهُ، وَيُقْطَعُ مِنْ شَرْحِ الشَّهَادَةِ. All of this has an explanation that will be mentioned at the appropriate time if Allah (SWT) wills. It would be lengthy if we covered it now, and it would disrupt the explanation of the testimony. When we declare these words, we are outwardly affirming that Allah(SWT) is the One true Deity, absolutely unique, with no partner whatsoever. Now, think about how we use the word 'one' in our everyday experience. We might point to a person, an animal, or an object and call it 'one.' Outwardly, it appears as a single unit. But if we look closer, beneath the surface, we find complexity, a combination of elements. A person, for instance, is outwardly one individual, but inwardly, we understand them as a combination of body and spirit – the visible and the invisible working together. The body is the outward form (the ظاهر), and the spirit is the inner reality (the باطن). This same principle extends to everything in the created world. Nothing exists in absolute simplicity. Everything exists within pairs of possibilities or states: something is either present or absent, it's either a little or a lot, it can be subtle or dense, heavy or light. Think of all the opposites and contrasts that define our reality. This inherent duality or composition is a characteristic of creation. Allah(SWT) is entirely distinct from all these characteristics of His creation. He cannot be described using these pairs, these combinations, these limitations. His Oneness isn't like the 'oneness' of a created thing that is made up of parts or defined by its opposite. His existence isn't like ours, which comes after non-existence or could potentially cease. No, He simply is Existent. His existence is absolute and proven through His signs and the wonders of the universe He created – precisely because the creation, in its complexity and duality, points towards an utterly unique Creator. Nothing in creation resembles Him. لَيْسَ كَمِثْلِهِ شَيْءٌ – "There is nothing whatsoever like unto Him." Similarly, the Shahada appears straightforward and singular at first glance, much like a human being - there is no god but Allah. That defines Islam. Yet, upon examining its parts and delving into ایمان , numerous layers emerge, indicating a distinct hierarchy. The Testimony وَجَدْتُ الشَّهَادَةَ عَلَى فَصْلَيْنِ: أَحَدُهُمَا نَفْيٌ، وَالثَّانِي إِثْبَاتٌ. And I found the testimony to be in two parts: one of them is negation, and the second is affirmation. فَقَوْلُهُ: (لَا إِلَهَ) نَفْيٌ وَ (إِلَّا اللَّهُ) إِثْبَاتٌ. So, his saying (`La ilaha`), "There is no deity," is a negation, and (`illa Allah`), "except Allah," is an affirmation. وَكُلُّ فَصْلٍ مِنْهَا كَلِمَتَانِ، الْأُولَى مِنْهَا مُتَّصِلَةٌ بِالثَّانِيَةِ، وَدُونَهَا، وَالثَّانِيَةُ أَعْلَى مِنْهَا. Each part has two 'words' (conceptual parts): the first is connected to the second and is positioned below it, while the second is positioned higher than it. فَمَثَلُ الْحَدِّ الْأَخِيرِ الَّذِي هُوَ الْإِثْبَاتُ فِي الْبَاطِنِ، مَثَلٌ عَلَى الْحَدِّ الْعُلْوِيِّ وَمَثَلُ الْحَدِّ الَّذِي هُوَ النَّفْيُ فِي الْبَاطِنِ مَثَلٌ عَلَى الْحَدِّ السُّفْلِيِّ. The likeness of the last limit, which represents affirmation in the inward aspect, serves as a likeness for the upper limit. Conversely, the likeness of the limit representing negation in the inward aspect functions as a likeness for the lower limit. وَمَثَلُ الْكَلِمَةِ الْأَخِيرَةِ مِنَ الْفَصْلِ الْأَخِيرِ (أَيِ الْإِثْبَاتِ) مَثَلٌ عَلَى الْحُدُودِ الْعُلْوِيَّةِ، وَمَثَلُ الَّتِي قَبْلَهَا مَثَلُ الْحَدِّ الَّذِي يَلِيهِ. The likeness of the final 'word' in the last section (representing affirmation) symbolizes the upper boundaries, while the likeness preceding it corresponds to the limit that follows it (beneath it). وَمَثَلُ الْكَلِمَةِ الثَّانِيَةِ مِنَ الْفَصْلِ الْأَوَّلِ فِي الْبَاطِنِ مَثَلٌ عَلَى الْحُدُودِ السُّفْلِيَّةِ، وَمَثَلُ الَّتِي قَبْلَهَا مَثَلٌ عَلَى الْحَدِّ الْمُتَّصِلِ بِهِ دُونَهُ. And the likeness of the second 'word' from the first part, in the inward aspect, is a likeness for the lower limits, and the likeness of the one before it is a likeness for the limit connected to it but below it. فَالْكَلِمَتَانِ الْمُتَّصِلَتَانِ فِي حَدِّ الْإِثْبَاتِ وَحَدِّ النَّفْيِ لِكَلِمَتَيْنِ مُسْتَعْمَلَتَيْنِ وَهُمَا لَا وَإِلَّا So the two connected words in the sphere of affirmation and the sphere of negation are two commonly used words, and they are "No" ( لا ) and "Except" ( إلا ). وَالْكَلِمَتَانِ الْمُتَّصِلَتَانِ بِهِمَا مِنَ الْحَدَّيْنِ أَعْلَى وَأَجَلُّ، وَهُمَا إِلَهَ وَاللَّهُ، The two words connected to them from the two spheres are higher and more majestic, and they are "deity" ( إله ) and "Allah" ( الله ). فَقَدْ دَلَّ ذَلِكَ عَلَى أَشْرَفِ الْحُدُودِ الْعُلْوِيَّةِ وَالسُّفْلِيَّةِ، So that indicates the most noble of the higher and lower realms. وَإِنْ كَانَ كُلُّ وَاحِدٍ مِنَ الْآخَرَيْنِ مُتَّصِلًا بِهِ وَدُونَهُ وَأَقَلَّ حُرُوفٍ مِنْهُ، Even though each one of the other two is connected to it, is below it, and has fewer letters than it. وَإِنَّ الْكَلِمَةَ الْأَخِيرَةَ حَمَلَتْ ثَلَاثَةَ أَحْرُفٍ جُمِعَتْ فِيهَا كَافَّةُ حُرُوفِ الشَّهَادَةِ وَهِيَ: الْأَلِفُ وَاللَّامُ وَالْهَاءُ. And indeed, the last word carried three letters in which all the letters of the Testimony are gathered, and they are: the Alif, the Lam, and the Ha. The concepts of "negation" ( النفی La ilaha ) and "affirmation" ( الاثبات illa Allah ) within the Shahada are referred to as "limits" ( حدود - singular حدّ ) because they function as defining boundaries or demarcations of the core concept of Tawhid (the Oneness of Allah). Here's a breakdown of why: Defining Boundaries: The Arabic word حدّ literally means a boundary, limit, or definition. It refers to prescribed limits or boundaries set by Allah. Negation as a Lower Limit: The first part, La ilaha ("There is no deity"), acts as a "lower limit" ( الْحَدِّ السُّفْلِيِّ ). It sets a boundary by excluding and negating the possibility of divinity for anything or anyone besides Allah. It clears the ground, defining what is not God. Affirmation as an Upper Limit: The second part, illa Allah ("except Allah"), acts as the "upper limit" ( الْحَدِّ الْعُلْوِيِّ ). It sets a boundary by affirming and establishing that the sole right of divinity belongs exclusively to Allah. It defines the ultimate, singular Truth. Conceptual Hierarchy: The reason he uses the "upper" and "lower" terminology is to describe the components within these limits, positioning Allah as the highest and most sublime concept ("noblest of the upper limits"), representing the ultimate boundary of affirmation, while the negation ( La ilaha ) forms the foundational or "lower" boundary. Establishing Tawhid: Together, these two "limits" precisely define the concept of Tawhid. They establish the absolute boundary between belief in the One True God and disbelief or shirk (associating partners with God). The negation excludes all falsehood, and the affirmation establishes the singular Truth. In essence, they are called "limits" because they delineate the fundamental conceptual and theological boundaries that define the very core of Islamic belief as expressed in the Shahada. They mark the line between what is affirmed (Allah's sole divinity) and what is negated (any other claim to divinity). Two Main Sections: Negation (Denial): La ilaha ("There is no god") - This is considered the "lower" part. It clears away all false ideas of gods first. Affirmation (Confirmation): illa Allah ("except Allah") - This is considered the "higher" part. It confirms the one true God, Allah. Analogy: Think of cleaning a space (lower action) before placing something precious there (higher action). You deny/remove the clutter ( La ilaha ) before affirming/placing the precious item ( Allah ). Words within Each Section: Each section is further broken down into two "words" or concepts: In La ilaha : You have La (No) and ilaha (god). La comes first and is "below" ilaha in this structure. In illa Allah : You have illa (except) and Allah (The God). illa Comes first and is "below" Allah . Connection and Importance: These "words" are connected. La leads to ilaha , and illa leads to Allah . There's a hierarchy. Allah is the highest and most important concept. Ilaha (the general idea of a god being denied) is next. Then come the connecting words illa and La . Even though La and illa have fewer letters; they connect to the more significant concepts ( ilaha and Allah ). Simple Analogy: Imagine building with blocks. La is a base block. Ilaha sits on it. Illa is like a connector block leading upwards to the most crucial block, Allah , at the very top. What it Symbolizes: The structure represents a spiritual journey: Start by rejecting falsehoods (the lower limit/negation: La ilaha ), then move to affirm the ultimate truth (the upper limit/affirmation: Allah ). The final word, Allah , is described as the highest and most noble. The Word "Allah": The word Allah itself contains key letters (Alif, Lam, Ha) that represent the essence of the entire testimony of faith. Pen and the tablet وَهَذِهِ الْجُمْلَةُ مِنَ الْقَوْلِ فِي حُدُودِ الشَّهَادَةِ نُوَضِّحُهَا لِلسَّامِعِينَ بِفَضْلِ اللَّهِ عَلَيْنَا وَعَلَيْهِمْ فَنَقُولُ: In this summary of the discourse regarding the limits of testimony, we aim to clarify it for the listeners by the grace of Allah (SWT) upon us and them. Thus, we say: إِنَّ مَا رَوَاهُ الْخَاصُّ وَالْعَامُّ عَنْ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ (صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَآلِهِ وَسَلَّمَ) أَنَّهُ قَالَ: « أَوَّلُ مَا خَلَقَ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ مِنْ خَلْقِهِ الْقَلَمَ، ثُمَّ أَجْرَى نَهْرًا أَحْلَى مِنَ الْعَسَلِ، وَأَشَدَّ بَيَاضًا مِنَ اللَّبَنِ، وَأَلْيَنَ مِنَ الزُّبْدِ، ثُمَّ خَلَقَ اللَّوْحَ الْمَحْفُوظَ الَّذِي ذَكَرَهُ اللَّهُ تَعَالَى فِي كِتَابِهِ الْعَزِيزِ ». Both the select few and the general populace tell us that Muhammad PBUH said: "The first creation of Allah (SWT) Almighty and Majestic was the Pen. Then He caused a river to flow, which is sweeter than honey, whiter than milk, and softer than butter. After that, He created the Preserved Tablet, which Allah (SWT) the Exalted referenced in His Mighty Book." فَحَمَلَتِ الْعَامَّةُ هَذَا الْخَبَرَ عَلَى مَا تَعْرِفُهُ، مِنْ أَنَّ الْقَلَمَ مِنْ قَصَبٍ، وَأَنَّ اللَّوْحَ مِنْ خَشَبٍ عَلَى مَا نُشَاهِدُهُ، وَلَمْ تَدْرِ أَنَّ هَذِهِ هِيَ أَسْمَاءٌ وَأَلْقَابٌ وَأَمْثَالٌ تَحْتَاجُ إِلَى إِيضَاحٍ، وَكَشْفٍ وَبَيَانٍ وَتَأْوِيلٍ، وَذَلِكَ مِنْ تَأْوِيلِ الْأَحَادِيثِ الَّتِي ذَكَرَهَا اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ. The general public understood this report based on their existing knowledge- that the Pen is crafted from reed and the Tablet from wood, much like what is visible. However, they failed to recognize that these terms are names, titles, and allegories that need clarification, unveiling, explanation, and interpretation, which is essential to understanding the Hadiths mentioned by Allah (SWT), the Almighty, and the Majestic. فَالْقَلَمُ هُوَ أَعْلَى حَدٍّ مِنَ الْحُدُودِ الَّتِي حَدَّهَا اللَّهُ بِعِبَادِهِ، وَنَهَى عَنْ تَعَدِّيْهَا بِقَوْلِهِ سُبْحَانَهُ وَتَعَالَى: ﴿ وَمَنْ يَتَعَدَّ حُدُودَ اللَّهِ فَقَدْ ظَلَمَ نَفْسَهُ ﴾ So the Pen is the highest limit among the limits that Allah (SWT) set for His servants, and He forbade them from transgressing these limits by His saying, Glorified and Exalted is He: "And whoever transgresses the limits of Allah (SWT) has certainly wronged himself." وَارْتَفَعَ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ عَنِ الْحُدُودِ وَنَصَبَهَا لِخَلْقِهِ مِنْ دُونِهِ، لِيَتَوَصَّلُوا مِنْهَا إِلَى مَعْرِفَتِهِ وَيُؤَدُّوا بِوَاسِطَتِهَا إِلَى الْخَلْقِ أَمْرَهُ وَنَهْيَهُ. The Almighty and Majestic transcend all limits and have established boundaries for His creation below Him, enabling them to gain knowledge of Him and to communicate His commands and prohibitions to the creation through these limits. فَالْقَلَمُ هُوَ الْحَامِلُ لِلْعِلْمِ الَّذِي اسْتَمَدَّهُ مِنَ اللَّهِ سُبْحَانَهُ، وَثَبَّتَهُ فِي اللَّوْحِ، وَاللَّوْحُ حَدٌّ مِنْ حُدُودِ اللَّهِ وَدُونَهُ (أَيْ دُونَ الْقَلَمِ) أَخَذَ الْعِلْمَ عَنِ الْقَلَمِ، وَصَارَ إِلَيْهِ مِنْهُ، وَهُمَا الْحَدَّانِ الْعُلْوِيَّانِ اللَّذَانِ قَدَّمْنَا ذِكْرَهُمَا. The Pen serves as the vessel of knowledge obtained from Allah (SWT), and this knowledge is inscribed in the Tablet. The Tablet represents a boundary established by Allah (SWT) and lies beneath the Pen; it receives knowledge from the Pen, which is its source. These two entities are the upper limits we referenced previously. وَالنَّهْرُ الَّذِي أَجْرَاهُ اللَّهُ سُبْحَانَهُ وَتَعَالَى هُوَ الْقَلَمُ. The river that Allah (SWT) caused to flow is through the pen. قَالَ سُبْحَانَهُ وَتَعَالَى: ﴿ وَلَوِ اسْتَقَامُوا عَلَى الطَّرِيقَةِ لَأَسْقَيْنَاهُمْ مَاءً غَدَقًا ﴾. He, Glorified and Exalted is He, said: "And if they had remained on the path, We would have given them abundant water to drink." فَالطَّرِيقَةُ هِيَ النَّبِيُّ النَّاطِقُ فِي زَمَانِهِ، وَالْإِمَامُ فِي أَوَانِهِ مِنْ بَعْدِهِ، وَالْمَاءُ الْغَدَقُ هُوَ الْعِلْمُ الْغَزِيرُ. So, the path is the speaking Prophet ( لنَّبِيُّ النَّاطِقُ ) in his time and the Imam ( وَالْإِمَامُ ) in his era after him; the abundant water is plentiful spiritual knowledge. وَقَالَ سُبْحَانَهُ وَهُوَ أَصْدَقُ الْقَائِلِينَ: ﴿ وَلَقَدْ خَلَقْنَا فَوْقَكُمْ سَبْعَ طَرَائِقَ وَمَا كُنَّا عَنِ الْخَلْقِ غَافِلِينَ ﴾. And Allah(SWT) is the most truthful of speakers. He said: "And We have created above you seven paths, and We have never been unaware of the creation." ‏لِّنَفْتِنَهُمْ فِيهِ ۚ وَمَن يُعْرِضْ عَن ذِكْرِ رَبِّهِۦ يَسْلُكْهُ عَذَابًۭا صَعَدًۭا That We might try them thereby, and whosoever turns away from the Reminder of his Lord, He will cause him to enter in a severe torment. وَسَنَذْكُرُ هَذَا بِتَمَامِهِ فِي مَوْضِعِهِ. And we will mention this fully in its proper place. هَذَا مَا أَقَرَّتْ بِهِ الْعَامَّةُ مِنْ تَأْوِيلِ الْبَاطِنِ إِذْ إِنَّهَا لَمْ تَجِدْ مِنْهُ مَحِيصًا، أَعْنِي أَنَّهَا أَوَّلَتِ الْمَاءَ بِالْعِلْمِ لِأَنَّ الْمَاءَ الظَّاهِرَ سَقَاهُ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ إِلَى الْبَرِّ وَالْفَاجِرِ، فَأَخْبَرَ سُبْحَانَهُ أَنَّ الْخَلْقَ لَوِ اسْتَقَامُوا لِوَلِيِّ أَمْرِهِمْ لَأَوْسَعَهُمْ عِلْمًا. The general populace recognized this internal interpretation, realizing there was no way to escape it. They saw water as knowledge, for outward water is bestowed by Allah (SWT) to both the righteous and the wicked. Therefore, Allah(SWT) revealed that had creation remained steadfast in their obedience to their authority, He would have enhanced their knowledge. وَقَوْلُهُ سُبْحَانَهُ: ﴿ لِنَفْتِنَهُمْ فِيهِ ﴾ أَيْ نَمْتَحِنُهُمْ وَنُخْبِرُ قَوْلَهُمْ وَعِلْمَهُمْ بِهِ. His saying, "That We might test them in it," means that we test them and reveal their thoughts and knowledge concerning it. ثُمَّ قَالَ سُبْحَانَهُ: ﴿ وَمَنْ يُعْرِضْ عَنْ ذِكْرِ رَبِّهِ يَسْلُكْهُ عَذَابًا صَعَدًا ﴾. Then He said: "And whoever turns away from the remembrance of his Lord, He will put him into increasing punishment." فَذَكَرَ سُبْحَانَهُ هَهُنَا وَلِيَّ أَمْرِهِمْ مَنْ أَعْرَضَ عَنْهُ صَارَ إِلَى عَذَابِ اللَّهِ وَسَمَّاهُ ذِكْرًا أَيْ إِقَامَةً لِذِكْرِهِ فَسُمِّيَ بِاسْمِهِ. So Allah(SWT) mentioned their guardian of authority here; whoever turns away from him will face the punishment of Allah (SWT). He named him "Dhikr" (remembrance), meaning an establishment of His remembrance, so His name named him.

title: Regarding The Seven & Twelve
text: وَالسَّمَاوَاتُ السَّبْعُ مَثَلٌ لِلسَّبْعَةِ النُّطَقَاءِ، وَالْأَرَضُونَ السَّبْعُ مَثَلٌ لِلْحُجَجِ السَّبْعِ، وَأَمْثَالُهُمْ أَيْضًا السَّبْعَةُ نُجُومُ الْمُدَبِّرَاتُ الَّتِي ذَكَرَهَا اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ فِي كِتَابِهِ فَقَالَ: ﴿ فَالْمُدَبِّرَاتِ أَمْرًا ﴾. The seven heavens symbolize the seven Speakers, while the seven earths represent the seven Proofs. Their representations include the seven managing stars that Allah (SWT) referenced in His book, stating: "And [by] those who arrange [each] matter." وَمَثَلُهُمْ فِي الْأَرْضِ الْأَقَالِيمُ السَّبْعُ، And their likeness on earth is the seven regions. وَمَثَلُهُمْ فِي الْإِنْسَانِ هُوَ أَنَّ الْإِنْسَانَ خُلِقَ مِنْ سَبْعَةِ أَشْيَاءَ كَمَا قَالَ سُبْحَانَهُ وَتَعَالَى: وَلَقَدْ خَلَقْنَا الْإِنْسَانَ مِنْ سُلَالَةٍ مِنْ طِينٍ ثُمَّ جَعَلْنَاهُ نُطْفَةً فِي قَرَارٍ مَكِينٍ ثُمَّ خَلَقْنَا النُّطْفَةَ عَلَقَةً فَخَلَقْنَا الْعَلَقَةَ مُضْغَةً فَخَلَقْنَا الْمُضْغَةَ عِظَامًا فَكَسَوْنَا الْعِظَامَ لَحْمًا ثُمَّ أَنْشَأْنَاهُ خَلْقًا آخَرَ فَتَبَارَكَ اللَّهُ أَحْسَنُ الْخَالِقِينَ The resemblance in humans is that they were created through seven stages, as Allah(SWT) stated: "And verily We have created man from an extract of clay. Then We made him a sperm drop in a firm lodging. Then We made the sperm drop into a clinging substance, and We made the clinging substance into a lump of flesh, and We made the lump of flesh into bones, and We clothed the bones with flesh; then We developed him into another creation. So blessed is Allah (SWT), the best of creators." وَأَجْزَاءُ الْإِنْسَانِ سَبْعَةٌ هِيَ: شَعْرٌ وَجِلْدٌ وَلَحْمٌ وَدَمٌ وَعَظْمٌ وَعُرُوقٌ وَمُخٌّ، وَطُولُهُ سَبْعَةُ أَشْبَارٍ بِشِبْرِهِ، وَعَرْضُهُ كَذَلِكَ، وَهُوَ مَا بَيْنَ طَرَفَيْ يَدَيْهِ إِذَا فَتَحَهَا. The parts of the human body are seven: hair, skin, flesh, blood, bone, veins, and marrow. His height is seven spans according to his own span, and his width is also seven spans, measured between the tips of his hands when he extends them. وَيَسْجُدُ عَلَى سَبْعَةِ أَعْضَاءَ وَجْهِهِ وَيَدَيْهِ وَرُكْبَتَيْهِ وَرِجْلَيْهِ. And he prostrates on seven limbs: his face, his two hands, his two knees, and his two feet. وَفِي جَبْهَتِهِ سَبْعَةُ مَنَافِذَ عَيْنَانِ وَأُذُنَانِ وَمِنْخَرَانِ وَفَمٌ. And on his face (or forehead) are seven openings: two eyes, two ears, two nostrils, and a mouth. وَهَذِهِ بَعْضُ الشَّوَاهِدِ لِآيَاتِ اللَّهِ وَحُدُودِهِ وَأَمْثَالِهَا وَبَوَاطِنِهَا فِي الْعَالَمِينَ الْأَكْبَرِ وَالْأَصْغَرِ. These represent various signs of Allah (SWT) along with their manifestations and deeper meanings in both the greater and lesser worlds. وَهَذِهِ بَعْضُ الدَّلَائِلِ عَلَى السَّبْعَةِ الَّتِي ذَكَرْنَاهَا. And these are some of the indications regarding the seven which we mentioned. Regarding the 12 فَأَمَّا الِاثْنَا عَشَرَ اللَّوَاحِقُ فَإِنَّهُمْ أَسْبَابٌ لِكُلِّ نَاطِقٍ فِي كُلِّ زَمَانٍ As for the twelve followers (Lawahiq), they are the means (or channels) for every Speaker (Natiq) in every era. أَرْبَعَةٌ مِنْهُمْ أَعْلَاهُمْ وَأَقْرَبُهُمْ، Four of them are the highest among them and the closest. وَلِكُلِّ وَاحِدٍ مِنْهُمُ اثْنَانِ كَالْيَمِينِ وَالشِّمَالِ، And for each one of them, there are two, like the right and the left. وَلِذَلِكَ كَانُوا اثْنَيْ عَشَرَ. And for that reason, they were twelve. وَمَثَلُ الْأَرْبَعَةِ مِنْهُمْ مَثَلُ طَيْرِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ فِي قِصَّتِهِ كَمَا قَالَ سُبْحَانَهُ وَتَعَالَى: ﴿ قَالَ فَخُذْ أَرْبَعَةً مِنَ الطَّيْرِ فَصُرْهُنَّ إِلَيْكَ ﴾ وَهُمْ مِنَ الِاثْنَيْ عَشَرَ حَدًّا. The similarity among the four reflects the birds in Abraham's story. As He, Glorified and Exalted, states: "He said, 'So take four of the birds and incline them to you.'" These four signify a boundary within the twelve. وَكَالْأَشْهُرِ الْأَرْبَعَةِ الْحُرُمِ لِقَوْلِهِ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ: ﴿ إِنَّ عِدَّةَ الشُّهُورِ عِنْدَ اللَّهِ اثْنَا عَشَرَ شَهْرًا فِي كِتَابِ اللَّهِ يَوْمَ خَلَقَ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضَ مِنْهَا أَرْبَعَةٌ حُرُمٌ ذَلِكَ الدِّينُ الْقَيِّمُ فَلَا تَظْلِمُوا فِيهِنَّ أَنْفُسَكُمْ ﴾. Similar to the four sacred months, as stated by the Almighty and Majestic: "Indeed, the number of months with Allah (SWT) is twelve [lunar] months in the register of Allah (SWT) [from] the day He created the heavens and the earth; from these [months] are four sacred. That is the correct religion, so do not wrong yourselves during them." وَأَمْثَالُهُمْ مِنَ الشَّهَادَةِ حُرُوفُهَا فَهِيَ اثْنَا عَشَرَ حَرْفًا [لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ]. And their likenesses from the testimony are its letters, for they are twelve letters [La ilaha illa Allah]. وَأَمْثَالُهُمْ فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ الْبُرُوجُ الِاثْنَا عَشَرَ، وَأَمْثَالُهُمْ مِنَ الْأَرْضِ الْجَزَائِرُ الِاثْنَا عَشَرَ لِكُلِّ وَاحِدٍ مِنْهُمْ جَزِيرَةٌ. The twelve zodiac signs represent their forms in the heavens, while their earthly representations are the twelve islands, with each island corresponding to one zodiac sign. وَأَمْثَالُهُمْ مِنَ الرُّوحَانِيِّينَ الِاثْنَا عَشَرَ الْمَلَائِكَةُ الرُّسُلُ أُولِي الْأَجْنِحَةِ And their counterparts among the spiritual beings are the twelve Messenger angels, those possessing wings. لِأَنَّهُمْ بِرِسَالَةِ صَاحِبِ الْأَمْرِ يَتَوَجَّهُونَ، Because they proceed with the message of the Owner of the Command (Sahib al-Amr). وَأَجْنِحَتُهُمُ الدُّعَاةُ، And their wings are the callers (to the faith). لِأَنَّ كُلَّ وَاحِدٍ مِنْهُمْ يُبْعَثُ إِلَى إِحْدَى الْجَزَائِرِ حَدًّا لَهُ Because each one of them is sent to one of the "islands" as a boundary for him. لِقَوْلِهِ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ: ﴿ الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ فَاطِرِ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ جَاعِلِ الْمَلَائِكَةِ رُسُلًا أُولِي أَجْنِحَةٍ مَثْنَى وَثُلَاثَ وَرُبَاعَ يَزِيدُ فِي الْخَلْقِ مَا يَشَاءُ إِنَّ اللَّهَ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ مَا يَفْتَحِ اللَّهُ لِلنَّاسِ مِنْ رَحْمَةٍ فَلَا مُمْسِكَهَا وَمَا يُمْسِكْ فَلَا مُرْسِلَ لَهُ مِنْ بَعْدِهِ وَهُوَ الْعَزِيزُ الْحَكِيمُ ﴾. According to His saying, the Mighty and Majestic: "All praise is due to Allah, the Originator of the heavens and the earth, who made the angels messengers having wings, two or three or four. He increases in creation what He wills. Indeed, Allah is over all things competent. Whatever Allah opens for the people of mercy, none can withhold it, and whatever He withholds, none can send it forth after Him. And He is the Almighty, the Wise." (Quran 35:1-2) فَهُمْ أَيِ الْحُدُودُ الِاثْنَا عَشَرَ فِي الْعَالَمِ السُّفْلِيِّ نَظِيرُ الْمَلَائِكَةِ فِي الْعَالَمِ الْعُلْوِيِّ لِقَوْلِهِ تَعَالَى: ﴿ وَمِنْ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ خَلَقْنَا زَوْجَيْنِ اثْنَيْنِ ﴾. So they, meaning the twelve limits in the lower world, are the counterparts of the angels in the upper world because of His saying, the Exalted: "And from everything We created two mates." فَالسَّبْعَةُ، وَالِاثْنَا عَشَرَ مَجْمُوعُهُمْ تِسْعَةَ عَشَرَ، وَهُمُ الَّذِينَ عَنَاهُمُ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ بِقَوْلِهِ ﴿ عَلَيْهَا تِسْعَةَ عَشَرَ ﴾، وَقَدْ جَمَعَتْهُمُ الشَّهَادَةُ. So the seven and the twelve, their sum is nineteen, and they are the ones whom Allah (SWT) Almighty and Majestic meant by His saying: "Over it are nineteen." And the testimony has gathered them. فَمَنْ عَرَفَهُمْ وَاعْتَقَدَ بِوَلَايَتِهِمْ، وَعَمِلَ بِمَا أَخَذَهُ عَنْهُمْ، فَهُوَ مُؤْمِنٌ مِنْ أَهْلِ الْجَنَّةِ مَقْبُولُ الشَّهَادَةِ. So whoever knows them, believes in their guardianship, and acts upon what he has taken from them, he is a believer from the people of Paradise, whose testimony is accepted. وَمَنْ عَجَزَ عَنْ شَيْءٍ مِنْ ذَلِكَ، أَوْ قَصَّرَ فِيهِ وَشَهِدَ الشَّهَادَةَ بِلِسَانِهِ، فَهُوَ مُسْلِمٌ غَيْرُ مَقْبُولِ الشَّهَادَةِ، وَحِسَابُهُ عَلَى اللَّهِ. Anyone unable to fulfill any part of that or who falls short and testifies verbally is a Muslim whose testimony is not entirely accepted, and his accountability rests with Allah (SWT). فَلِأَجْلِ هَذَا جَاءَ فِي كِتَابِ «دَعَائِمِ الْإِسْلَامِ»، مِنْ قَوْلِ الصَّادِقِ صَلَوَاتُ اللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ: « إِنَّ الْإِيمَانَ يُشْرِكُ الْإِسْلَامَ وَالْإِسْلَامُ لَا يُشْرِكُ الْإِيمَانَ، وَالْإِنْسَانُ يَكُونُ مُسْلِمًا غَيْرَ مُؤْمِنٍ، وَلَا يَكُونُ مُؤْمِنًا إِلَّا بَعْدَ أَنْ يَكُونَ مُسْلِمًا ». And for this reason, it came in the book "Da'a'im al-Islam," from the saying of As-Sadiq, Allah's blessings be upon him: "Indeed, faith includes Islam, but Islam does not include faith. And a person can be a Muslim without being a believer, but one cannot be a believer until after he has been a Muslim." وَهَذِهِ هِيَ الشَّهَادَةُ قَدْ بَيَّنَّا حُدُودَهَا عَلَى الْأُصُولِ، وَتَتَّصِلُ بِهَا شَهَادَةٌ ثَانِيَةٌ عَلَى الْفُرُوعِ، وَهِيَ (مُحَمَّدٌ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَآلِهِ وَسَلَّمَ). And this is the testimony. We have clarified its limits concerning the fundamentals (Usul), and a second testimony concerning the branches (Furu') is connected to it, which is (Muhammad is the Messenger of Allah PBUH). وَهِيَ ثَلَاثُ كَلِمَاتٍ مِثْلُ الثَّلَاثَةِ الْحُدُودِ الرُّوحَانِيِّينَ الْعُلْوِيِّينَ الْفَرْعِيِّينَ، وَهُمْ: إِسْرَافِيلُ وَمِيكَائِيلُ وَجَبْرَائِيلُ. There are three 'words,' corresponding to the three secondary upper spiritual limits: Israfil, Michael, and Gabriel. وَهِيَ كَذَلِكَ مِثْلُ الثَّلَاثَةِ الْحُدُودِ الْجُسْمَانِيِّينَ الْأَرْضِيِّينَ بِمَدِّ النَّاطِقِ وَالْأَسَاسِ، وَهُمْ: الْإِمَامُ وَالْحُجَّةُ وَاللَّاحِقُ. Similarly, it reflects the three earthly physical boundaries concerning the Speaker and the Foundation, which are: the Imam, the Proof, and the Successor. وَهِيَ أَيْضًا سِتَّةُ فُصُولٍ مِثْلُ النُّطَقَاءِ السِّتَّةِ أُولِي الْعَزْمِ وَقَدْ ذَكَرْنَاهُمْ. And it is also six parts (or sections), like the six Speakers of strong will, and we have already mentioned them. وَهِيَ كَذَلِكَ اثْنَا عَشَرَ حَرْفًا مِثْلُ اللَّوَاحِقِ الِاثْنَيْ عَشَرَ، وَقَدْ مَضَى ذِكْرُهُمْ. And it is likewise twelve letters, like the twelve successors, and their mention has already passed. فَالشَّهَادَةُ لِلَّهِ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ هِيَ الدَّرَجَةُ الْعُلْيَا، وَهِيَ الْأُصُولُ The testimony for Allah (SWT), who is Almighty and Majestic, represents the higher degree and serves as the fundamentals (Usul). وَالشَّهَادَةُ لِلرَّسُولِ (صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَآلِهِ وَسَلَّمَ) هِيَ الدَّرَجَةُ الدُّنْيَا، وَهِيَ الْفُرُوعُ In contrast, the testimony for the Messenger PBUH reflects the lower degree and constitutes the branches (Furu'). وَقَدْ دَخَلَتْ أَيِ الْحُدُودُ الْعُلْيَا وَالدُّنْيَا فِي حُرُوفِ الشَّهَادَةِ. And the limits, meaning the upper and lower limits, have entered into the letters of the testimony. فَظَاهِرُ الشَّهَادَةِ قَوْلٌ بِاللِّسَانِ، وَبَاطِنُهَا قَوْلٌ وَعِلْمٌ وَاعْتِقَادُهَا عَمَلٌ، فَظَاهِرُهَا الْإِسْلَامُ، وَبَاطِنُهَا الْإِيمَانُ. So, the outward aspect of the testimony is speech by the tongue, and its inward aspect is speech and knowledge, and its belief is action. So, its outward aspect is Islam, and its inward aspect is Iman (Faith). وَفِي ذَلِكَ قَالَ الصَّادِقُ صَلَوَاتُ اللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ فِيمَا أَثْبَتْنَاهُ عَنْهُ فِي كِتَابِ «دَعَائِمِ الْإِسْلَامِ» لَمَّا سُئِلَ عَنِ الْإِيمَانِ، أَقَوْلٌ أَمْ عَمَلٌ؟ قَالَ: «الْ إِيمَانُ عَمَلٌ كُلُّهُ وَالْقَوْلُ بَعْضُ ذَلِكَ ». And concerning that, As-Sadiq, Allah's blessings be upon him, said in what we have established from him in the book "Da'a'im al-Islam", when he was asked about faith, "Is it speech or action?" He said: "Faith is entirely action, and speech is part of that." فَاخْلِصُوا مَعْشَرَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ الشَّهَادَةَ الَّتِي دُعِيتُمْ إِلَيْهَا، وَأَخْلِصُوا لَهَا، وَقَدْ شَرَحْنَا لَكُمُ الْإِخْلَاصَ مِنْهَا، وَقَدْ نَهَاكُمُ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ أَنْ تَأْبَوْا ذَلِكَ بِقَوْلِهِ: ﴿وَلَا يَأْبَ الشُّهَدَاءُ إِذَا مَا دُعُوا﴾. So be sincere, O assembly of believers, in the testimony to which you were called, and be sincere in it. We have already explained our sincerity to you concerning it. And Allah (SWT) Almighty and Majestic has forbidden you from refusing that by His saying: "And the witnesses should not refuse when they are called." فَاخْلِصُوا لَهَا بِالْقَوْلِ وَالْعَمَلِ وَالْوَفَاءِ بِعَهْدِهِ لَكُمْ فِي قَوْلِهِ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ: ﴿أَوْفُوا بِعَهْدِي أُوفِ بِعَهْدِكُمْ﴾. So be sincere in it through speech, action, and fulfilling His covenant with you in His saying, Almighty and Majestic: "Fulfill My covenant, I will fulfill Your covenant." فَقَدْ وَعَدَكُمُ اللَّهُ سُبْحَانَهُ بِلِسَانِ رَسُولِهِ بِالْجَنَّةِ إِنْ أَخْلَصْتُمُ الشَّهَادَةَ، وَقَدْ عَرَفْتُمْ ذَلِكَ فَاعْتَقِدُوا، وَلَا تَشُكُّوا، وَلَا تَرْتَابُوا So Allah Glorified be He has promised you, through the tongue of His Messenger, Paradise if you are sincere in the testimony. And you have known that, so believe firmly, and do not doubt, and do not waver. وَاعْمَلُوا بِذَلِكَ، وَلَا تُضَيِّعُوا، وَلَا تَهَاوَنُوا، وَلَا تُسَوِّفُوا، فَتَنَالُوا مَا وَعَدَكُمُ اللَّهُ مِنَ الْجَنَّةِ ظَاهِرًا وَبَاطِنًا And act upon that, and do not waste time, and do not be negligent, and do not procrastinate. So you will attain what Allah (SWT) has promised you of Paradise, outwardly and inwardly. فَقَدْ قَالَ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ: ﴿وَلِمَنْ خَافَ مَقَامَ رَبِّهِ جَنَّتَانِ﴾۔ For He, Almighty and Majestic, said: "But for he who has feared the position of his Lord are two gardens." فَتَنَالُوا فِي الْعَاجِلِ مِنَ الْعِلْمِ الْحِكْمَةَ مَا تَسْرَحُونَ فِي رِيَاضِهَا، So you will attain in this life, from knowledge and wisdom, that in whose gardens you may roam freely, وَتَنَالُونَ بِهِ مِنْ ثِمَارِهَا وَأَزْوَاجِهَا And by it you will obtain from its fruits and its varieties, وَتَصِيرُونَ إِلَى جَنَّةِ الْخُلْدِ فِي دَارِ الْبَقَاءِ فِي الْآجِلِ، And you will eventually arrive at the Garden of Eternity in the abode of everlastingness in the Hereafter, فَتَفُوزُوا فِيهَا بِثَوَابِ اللَّهِ الَّذِي وَعَدَكُمْ So you will succeed in it with the reward of Allah (SWT) which He promised you, وَتَأْمَنُوا عَذَابَهُ الَّذِي بِهِ تَوَعَّدَكُمْ إِنْ خَالَفْتُمْ مَا أَمَرَكُمْ بِهِ، And you will be safe from His punishment with which He warned you if you disobeyed what He commanded you, وَارْتَكَبْتُمْ مَا نَهَاكُمْ عَنْهُ، And you committed what He forbade you from, وَأَعْرَضْتُمْ عَمَّا نَدَبَكُمْ إِلَيْهِ And you turned away from what He encouraged you towards, وَدَعَاكُمْ إِلَى الْإِخْلَاصِ بِالشَّهَادَةِ الَّتِي جُمِعَ فِيهَا جَمِيعُ مَا تُعُبِّدْتُمْ بِهِ. And He called you to sincerity with the Testimony in which is gathered all that by which you are commanded to worship Him. وَقَدْ ذَكَرْنَا فِي هَذَا الْفَصْلِ، وَهُوَ فَصْلُ الْإِيمَانِ مَا فِيهِ الْكِفَايَةُ مِنْ شَرْحِ الشَّهَادَةِ، وَمَا دَلَّتْ عَلَيْهِ مِنَ الْحُدُودِ. And we have mentioned in this section, which is the section on Faith, what is sufficient concerning the explanation of the testimony and what it indicated of the limits. وَسَنَذْكُرُ فِي أَبْوَابِ الْكِتَابِ كُلِّهَا مِثْلَ ذَلِكَ عِنْدَ ذِكْرِ الْفَرَائِضِ And we will mention something similar to that in all the chapters of the book when discussing the obligatory duties, لِتَعْلَمُوا أَنَّ هَذِهِ الشَّهَادَةَ، قَدْ حَوَتْ وَأَحَاطَتْ بِجَمِيعِ مَا تُعُبِّدْتُمُ اللَّهَ بِهِ، So that you may know that this Testimony has encompassed and included all that by which you worship Allah (SWT), وَلِذَلِكَ كَانَتْ دَعْوَةُ الرَّسُولِ (صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَآلِهِ وَسَلَّمَ) إِلَيْهَا، And that is why the call of the Messenger (peace and blessings of Allah (SWT) be upon him and his family) was to it, وَأَنَّهُ أَخَذَ عَلَيْكُمُ الْعَهْدَ أَنْ تَشْهَدُوهَا بَعْدَهُ بِإِخْلَاصٍ، And that he took a covenant from you that you would bear witness to it after him with sincerity, وَقَدْ كُنْتُمْ شَهِدْتُمُوهَا لَفْظًا، And you had already testified to it verbally, وَأَنْ تَشْهَدُوهَا شَهَادَةَ اعْتِقَادٍ بِقُلُوبِكُمْ، And that you should bear witness to it as a testimony of belief in your hearts, وَعَمَلًا بِجَوَارِحِكُمْ، And by action with your physical selves, وَعِلْمًا بِتَعْيِينِكُمْ مُحِيطِينَ بِحُدُودِهَا الَّتِي حُدِّدَتْ لَكُمْ، And by knowledge through your comprehension, being fully aware of its boundaries that have been defined for you, وَشَوَاهِدِهَا الَّتِي دَلَّتْ عَلَى السَّبِيلِ الْقَوِيمِ الَّذِي بِهِ نَجَاتُكُمْ، And its proofs which pointed to the straight path by which is your salvation. أَعَانَكُمُ اللَّهُ عَلَى ذَلِكَ، May Allah (SWT) assist you in that, وَوَفَّقَكُمْ إِلَيْهِ. And grant you success in it. وَصَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَى مُحَمَّدٍ النَّبِيِّ، وَعَلَى أَبْرَارِ عِتْرَتِهِ الْأَئِمَّةِ الصَّادِقِينَ مِنْ ذُرِّيَّتِهِ الْمُتَعَاقِبِينَ إِلَى يَوْمِ الدِّينِ، وَالْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ رَبِّ الْعَالَمِينَ. May Allah (SWT) send blessings upon Muhammad the Prophet and the righteous ones from his progeny, the truthful Imams from his successive descendants until the Day of Judgment. And praise be to Allah (SWT), Lord of the worlds.

title: Allah  Name Or Title?
text: If Allah is a name, who named him? Naming is done by someone superior. Throughout history, various names have been used to call him in different languages. Aramaic: The language spoken during the time of Jesus, he was called ʼĔlāhā Syriac: A dialect of Aramaic. He was called Alāhā (ܐܰܠܳܗܳܐ), Hebrew: The language of the Torah. He has been called El (אֵל): A generic term for "god" or "God." Eloah (אֱלוֹהַּ): A singular form for "God," directly related to Aramaic Elaha and Arabic Allah . Elohim (אֱלֹהִים): A plural form grammatically, but often used with a singular meaning to refer to the one God in the Hebrew Bible. It shares the same root. Akkadian: He was called Ilu . Sanskrit Scriptures: He was called Īśvara (ईश्वर) and Parameśvara (परमेश्वर) - the supreme Being. Islam: He is called Allah. Various names have been used across different religions to refer to God throughout history, but they all convey the same essence. The one who bewilders the mind The one Supreme Being who is truly worthy of submission, worship, and obedience. Definition Of Allah Ahad : Must be unique, meaning that during his reign, no one can match his capabilities or powers. Samad : Must be whole and self-sufficient, free from any deficiencies, flaws, or weaknesses. Transcendence: Lam Yalid (Did Not Beget) - He has no offspring, indicating there is no resemblance to His creation. The progeny of a cat is a cat, a dog’s offspring is a dog, and a man's is a child. Offspring share similarities in appearance, qualities, traits, and abilities with their father. While we reproduce and He created us, our nature is not comparable. Think about the difference between an artisan crafting a unique masterpiece through skill and intent, distinct from the artist himself, and a living being giving birth (which suggests shared nature and biological connection). Lam Yalid emphasizes that Allah's connection to creation mirrors the former. Despite His act of creation, He remains unique and separated from it, just as the artist remains entirely distinct from his sculpture. Lam Yulad (Was not Begotten) - He was not created - meaning his existence did not require the fusion of sperm and egg, nor did he go through the seven stages of creation, like a fetus goes through in its mother's womb. Kufu : He is incomparable in every possible sense of the word. No other being possesses his rank, his infallible knowledge, his authority, or his functions. In other words, he has no equal in his dominion. What does the combination of these attributes produce? An entity that bewilders. An entity that is worthy of submission and obedience. If we accept that "Allah" is a title rather than a name, then metaphorically, anyone embodying the attributes described in Surah Ikhlas can be referred to as "Allah." This idea does not imply "Shirk" since Surah Ikhlas defines the concept in a way that rules out a partnership. It doesn't mean that we deny the existence of the one Supreme being who created the universe and everything within it. Certainly not. He remains the only deity deserving of our worship and prostration. My point is that the title "Allah" can apply to anyone who fulfills the definition of Surah Ikhlas in their context. This distinction will become clearer shortly. For now, can we all agree that Allah is a "title" and not a "name"? And anyone who meets this criterion can be called "Allah"? The Imam as Allah Now, let's see if the definition of Surah al-Ikhlas can be applied esoterically to the Imam. "Qul Huwa Allahu Ahad" (Say: He is Allah, the One) In every age, the rightful Imam is unique . He is احد because he is the source of infallible divine guidance and authentic تاویل . There is only one true Imam for all of humanity at any given time. His authority in interpreting the faith and guiding the community is singular and unparalleled within the human realm for that era. He represents the unity of divinely guided authority on Earth. "Allahu al-Samad" (Allah, the Eternal Refuge / Self-Sufficient) The Imam serves as the spiritual refuge and support for the believers. We all turn to him for spiritual sustenance, knowledge ( علم ), interpretation ( تاویل ), and intercession. He is the focal point for the community's spiritual needs and the reliable source we turn to for guidance on the path to salvation. Therefore, he is Samad - completely self-sufficient with absolutely no weakness. "Lam yalid wa lam yulad" (He neither begets nor is born/begotten) . However, the تاویل relates to the spiritual reality and authority of the Imamate: Lam yūlad (spiritually "unbegotten" ) does not have a father. What does this mean? Because we know the Imam is physically born ( یولد ) and has offspring ( یلد ), including his son, who becomes his successor. So, how does this not being born apply to the Imam? The core idea is that while the Imam's physical existence begins at birth, the Divine Light ( نور الامامۃ ) , infallible knowledge ( علم ), and the spiritual authority he embodies are not derived from his physical birth or earthly sources. Instead, they represent a pre-existing, divinely ordained reality that emerges from the spiritual realms, ultimately rooted in God's Command ( Amr ). Additionally, concerning physical birth, a child undergoes seven stages in the womb before being born into the world. From an esoteric perspective, the Imam does not progress through stages of learning to gain knowledge, wisdom, and authority. Instead, he receives these directly from Allah via a divine light emanating from Allah's command. Thus, in this regard, he lacks a father. Why? Typically, a father teaches his trade skills to his son, but the Imam does not learn from his father; Allah directly grants him knowledge. Thus, he exists without needing a father in this esoteric context. So the term لم یولد applies to an Imam in that esoteric context. Analogy: Think of the Imam as the Moon in the night sky. The Moon itself is a physical body that exists in space, just like the Imam's physical body, which is born. However, the beautiful light the Moon shines does not originate from the Moon's substance; it is not "begotten" by the Moon itself. The light is a reflection of the Sun (representing the ultimate Divine Source or the higher spiritual emanations like the Universal Intellect). Lam yalid (spiritually "non-begetting" Does not have a son. This relates to the concept I just discussed. The fundamental idea is that the Imam, serving as the focal point of Divine Light (نور) and Guidance (علم), does not produce or originate this spiritual essence from within himself, like how a parent begets a child who shares their nature, traits, and DNA. Instead, the Imam manifests, reflects, or channels the divine light he receives from the spiritual realm to the believers, similar to the moon analogy I mentioned earlier. Another analogy would be a mirror. A clean, perfect mirror accurately shows the image of whatever is before it. The image appears in the mirror, but the mirror doesn't create or "beget" the object or its image. The infallible Imam, due to his purity and divine designation, perfectly reflects the Divine Will and Guidance for the people of his time. The truth appears through him, but he isn't the ultimate originator of that truth. He doesn't "beget" the divine reality that he reflects. Lam Yalid here signifies that the mirror (Imam) doesn't generate the object (Divine Reality/Guidance) but rather reflects it for the believers. "Wa lam yakun lahu kufuwan ahad" (And there is none comparable/equivalent unto Him) In the hierarchy of creation and spiritual authority of his time, the Imam stands unmatched ( کفوا ). No other individual holds his status, infallible knowledge ( 'ilm ), his role as the Proof of God, or his unique position as the sole legitimate interpreter of the faith's more profound meaning. In his divinely appointed position, he had no peer among humanity during his time.

title: Conclusion
text: We were born in to a world dominated and dictated by physicality. Muslims around the world are just as much a victim of this switch in priorities from spirituality to physicality Reality Check We're all starting a journey towards the very 3 things the world around us has rejected: God, Soul and Afterlife. We're starting from the depths of physicality. It's not going to be easy, and most of all, it's not going to be comfortable. Physicality is a prison! It's a trap - and we're all trapped in it. Quran and Rasul Allah(Salallahu alayhi wa aalihee wa sallam) cane to set us free from this trap. The only way to escape the trap is to "see" it. Once we see the trap, we're no longer stuck in it, because we can work our way around it. May Allah give us the strength and help us all in this journey towards him, and may He help us do it for the right reasons and in the right way.

title: Relationship of Quran and Intellect
text: What Is Creed? Creed guides a persons actions. عَقِیدۃ comes from the root عقد , which means a knot. عَقِیدۃ is we are bound to; what we have conviction about We believe and put our faith in narrations - that are transmitted down by people called the مُحَدِّثُون (the narrators). Creed is basically نَقَل . The word نَقَل in Arabic means to "transmit or transport". Quran and HADEES are transmissions coming down through time, by a series of narrators, which are then received by the intellect (or the عقل ). The intellect is a necessary condition for نَقَل . Intellect was created to bear the responsibility of creed The narrator is like a pharmacist - not a doctor. Imam is the spiritual doctor who can put these narrations in the proper context for us. Intellect without religion, and religion without intellect are both disastrous states. A basic belief is a belief that is not built on an intellectual foundation. Downfall of religion has been brought about by two types of people. Close minded teachers who are not willing to look at any problem that defies their belief system Lazy and irresponsible students who are not willing to put in the time and effort to understand the Quran within its proper context.

title: Level 1 Of Quranic Understanding - TAZAKKUR
text: There are 3 levels at which the Quran can be understood: Remembrance ( تذکر) The Enshrouded One [73:19] [ سورۃ ۷۳] المزمل ‏إِنَّ هَذِهِۦ تَذْكِرَةٌۭ ۖ فَمَن شَآءَ ٱتَّخَذَ إِلَىٰ رَبِّهِۦ سَبِيلًا ‎ ۱۹ Verily, this is an admonition, therefore whosoever will, let him take a Path to His Lord! Provides the easiest, surface level understanding of the Quran that every human being can benefit from. Has to do with understanding the fundamental concepts of our theology. Resonates with our internal moral compass - ( فطرۃ ) making it easy to accept and understand.

title: Living a good life in this world
text: The reason Allah created this default mechanism within our souls is because he wants us to take advantage of this world. Without this attraction, it would have be impossible for us to function in this world, because all our needs come from this world. وَلَقَدْ مَكَّنَّكُمْ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ وَجَعَلْنَا لَكُمْ فِيهَا مَعَيِشَ؛ قَلِيلًۭا مَّا تَشْكُرُونَ We settled you down on the earth, and we especially made for you good housing. عیش is to have a good life. The same word is used to describe life in paradise فھو فی عیشۃ راضیۃ They will live a great life in paradise. The key is to be in control of the world and not let it control us Rasul Allah(Salallahu alayhi wa aalihee wa sallam) said: مَالِي وَلِلدُّنْيَا ، إِنَّمَا مَثَلِي وَمَثَلُ الدُّنْيَا ؛كَمَثَلِ رَاكِبٍ ظَلَّ تَحتَ شَّجَرَة ؛ ثُمَّ رَاحَ وَ تَرَكَهَا People what concern do I have of this world of yours? My life in this world, is like the rider who takes rest under the shadow of a tree for a few moments. He then bids farewell to the tree and moves on towards his destination. انَّمَا الدُّنیَا خُلِقَتْ لَکُم ؛ وَأَنتُمْ خُلِقتُم لِی الآخَرَۃ Indeed this world has been created for you ; but you have been created for the afterlife.

title: Linguistic Analysis Of Alameen
text: The term العالمین , is generally translated as "worlds" or "universe" The word العالمین is the plural form of the word العالَم . Both words عالَم and عالِم have the exact same spelling. and share the same root word which is علم meaning knowledge. The عالِم is the one who knows - the cause; The عالَم is that which can be known - the effect! The device through which the عالِم learns about the عالَم is the intellect which in Arabic is العقل . Human beings can know many many things, but not everything Allah knows everything there is to know. He is علیم . The علیم is the one who has absolute and complete knowledge of everything. The علیم created the عالَم , so that we can study it, reflect upon it, and through it become knowledgeable about the علیم Allah cannot be "defined"; but human beings can only understand by "defining" realities. Definition is what "defines" or gives "Finite-ness to something. It limits reality for us so that we can put things into perspective. That is the function of the Intellect. The عقل sets boundaries, confines, defines. Allah(Subhanahu wa Ta'ala) created this عالَم so human beings that are creatures of "finite-ness" and "definition", could understand a creator who is "infinite" and cannot be defined; and become aware of the علیم . The عالَم acts as an interpreter

title: Universe Manfiests Existence Of Allah
text: The universe acts as a medium through which we can recognize Allah(Subhanahu wa Ta'ala) because every sculpture necessitates the presence of a sculptor. The poet says: وُجُودُہُ لَہُ دَلِیلُ قَاطِعٌ حَاجَتُ کُلِّ مُحدَثٍ لِصَانعٌ وَلِکُلِّ عالَمُ لِحَاجَّتاً الی العَالِمٌ The proof of how we know Allah (Subhanhu Wa Ta'ala) exists, is through the آثار - through his signs and effects left behind in creation. The word عالَم is anything and everything that can be known by the عالِم in order to enable him to recognize the علیم . The translation "world" limits the scope of its meaning. If Allah truly meant world, then he would have used the term العوالم not العالمین . The عالَم is waiting for the عالِم to learn about it through reflection, so that he can recognize the علیم . The definition of رب العالمین becomes broader than master of this world or this universe to encompass the reality that He is also the master of all the different species He is also the master of all the distinct groups of creation Every group, every category, every concept, every idea, every postulate, every physical matter, every chemical matter, every spiritual entity, everything in existence - physical or meta-physical is now included in the definition of the word عالَم . Everything exists for one and one reason alone - so that their creator could be recognized and understood.

title: What is Worship?
text: The word عبادۃ is translated as worship - which is an action; The word عبودیۃ is the sentiment that makes me want to worship. Linguistically the word عبادۃ means two things: It means عبودیۃ و الطاعۃ It means worship and it also means obedience - both at the same time. The word عبادۃ includes obedience that comes out of love - not force! In Arabic, the word for undesired forceful obedience is رِقَّۃ which is equivalent to غلامی in The essence of all forms of worship is willing obedience, and the essence of willing obedience is love. The word Allah describes who our creator is, and what he is generally supposed to mean in our lives The word رب describes his personal relationship with us. In other words All forms of obedience are considered عبادۃ in Islam.

title: Judging Our RAB
text: Allah gives us complete freedom of choice which is the greatest honor! He gives the most and expects the least in return He explains his the reasons for his commands through the Quran and the life example of Rasul Allah(Salallahu alayhi wa aalihee wa sallam). All acts of obedience are designed and put in place to benefit us He judges our actions based on our capabilities and level of knowledge He is in constant service to us He allows us to exist despite our disobedience and ingratitude He is loved and worshiped by his slaves The greatest challenge, the greatest difficulty, the greatest hardship that we face in life - every single waking moment - is to accept Allah as رب

title: Q&A
text: 1 - If our relationship with Allah is of master and slave, then what is the concept of freedom in Islam? 2 - If Allah is so merciful then why did Imam Hussain and his friends suffer in Karbala?

title: The blaming soul
text: The root word for لوامۃ is لوم , which means to blame, or to incriminate. ملامت is a derivative from this same root, which is also used in Urdu. This is where the نفس is leaving its beastly nature and moving up into a level of sophistication Unrestricted emotions جھل It is the opposite of عقل It means "without restraint" which leads to an ill-mannered, uneducated and ignorant mannerisms. The Criterion [25:63] [ سورۃ ۲۵] الفرقان ‏وَعِبَادُ ٱلرَّحْمَنِ ٱلَّذِينَ يَمْشُونَ عَلَى ٱلْأَرْضِ هَوْنًۭا وَإِذَا خَاطَبَهُمُ ٱلْجَهِلُونَ قَالُوا۟ سَلَمًۭا ‎ ۶۳ And the slaves of the Most Merciful are those who walk on the earth softly and in humility; and when people with uncontrolled emotions address them, they reply back with SALAAM ٱلَّذِينَ يَمْشُونَ عَلَى ٱلْأَرْضِ هَوْنًۭا , is an Arabic expression which means to be in control of oneself. It takes more effort to walk gently than normal walking. The people of the most merciful are those who exert and restrain themselves; and are in control of their emotions and their tongues. When someone who cannot exercise self restraint speaks to them, they give them a controlled response. نفس الوامۃ is where the soul has risen to a higher state, and is moving out of its abject slavery of the body, and beginning to recognize a type of moral imperative. Should want vs want This نفس is still doing wrong by committing sins, but it's doing so with a moral awareness. There is a difference between should want , and actually wanting it, and what the heart truly wants is Allah(Subhanahu wa Ta'ala) And according to the Quran, the نفس wants Allah(Subhanahu wa Ta'ala). Wan" is a Latin word that comes from "vanta", and it means to be impoverished, which in the Islamic framework is this idea of افتِقار . Creation is المُفتَقِرُو الی اللہ - creation is in want and need of Allah (Subhanhu Wa Ta'ala)- Allah is the one who is free of all want المُستَغنِی عَنِ الکُل .

title: Linguistic meaning of NAFS
text: Within the meaning of the word, we find the concept of having two aspects to our nature: One is our angelic nature ( نَفِسَ ) The other is our beastly nature (َ نَفُس ) Similarly we find two more variations َ نَفَّس means to relieve someone of their constriction َ نَفَس means to undermine someone; or to compete against someone. A child displays both behavior at a very early stage. It can smile and melt the mother's heart It can yell and scream and drive the mother insane. The reason we have both these qualities with our soul is becayse Allah created us for negation and affirmation We were created to negate this world, in order to affirm the the next world. We have to negate the submission of a غلام , in order to achieve the submission of a عبد

title: Consistency in Allah's design
text: 3 States Of The Soul The first state called نفس الامارۃ بالسوء . The soul that commands to evil The second state is called النفس الوامۃ - the self-reproaching, the self-blaming, the guilty soul. The word لوم means to blame or to reprimand. The third and the highest state the soul is called النفس المطمئنۃ - the satisfied or content soul. The end goal of the entire Quranic narrative; of the teachings of our messenger (Salallahu alayhi wa aalihee wa sallam); of the principles and practices of Islam and شریعۃ , is to bring the soul to this final state. 3 Capabilities Within Human Nature: There are 3 inherent powers within the human nature. The first power is called قوۃ الشھوانیۃ which is the power of base appetite (food and sex) The second is قوۃ الغضبیۃ The irascible power or the emotional power. The third and the highest power that exists within human nature, is قوۃ العقلیۃ , the rational power; the intellectual power; or the power of reason. Plato explains this same concept through: epithumia, thumos, and logos Epithumia is located in the abdomen and the genitals, which is the life of the beasts, and corresponds to قوۃ الشھوانیۃ , which is the power of base appetite (food and sex) Thumos , is located in the breast, and relates to قوۃ الغضبیۃ Logos , is the soul of the "proper" man, that reflects, the divine element within human nature, and corresponds to قوۃ العقلیۃ , the rational power, that elevates us from the level of beasts. The appetite strives after things, that the intellect forbids, and anger is the emotion that rises up in opposition to reason. 3 Parts Of The Brain Our physical brain is divided into 3 parts. We have the Reptilian Brain , which is the brain of appetites - it is brain of base desires - قوۃ الشھوانیۃ Then we have the mid-brain (also called the limbic system) which is the emotional brain. This is where قوۃ الغضبیۃ comes from - the irascible power And then finally we have the" neocortex ", from where we get our قوۃ العقلیۃ , reasoning power. Physical Placement Of Entities The 3 states are perfectly aligned with the 3 powers within human nature, which are perfectly aligned with the 3 parts of the brain Allah placed the human brain, right at the top - in the head - because it corresponds to the highest state of our soul ( نفس المطمئنۃ ), and the greatest power that we have as human beings - قوۃ العقلیۃ He placed guilt and the irascible power ( قوۃ الغضبیۃ ) in the heart, which is in the center, just as the mid-brain is located in the center And then he placed the abdomen and genitals right at the bottom of the human body because they represent the lowest aspect of human nature - قوۃ الشھوانیۃ . In the creation of this universe: Allah placed all the heavenly bodies, stars and planets at the top. Under the heavens he placed the 4 spheres of elements: Fire, Water, Air, Earth. At the lowest level, he created beasts. In the creation of creatures: Angels are creatures that made from pure light, which is the highest and purest material that we can imagine, and therefore Allah situated in them in the angelic realm - عالم الملکوت . Under the angelic realm are the jinn, that are creatures made from invisible smoke, which is lower in rank than pure light but higher than physical mass. And on the lowest level (on this physical earth), he created the human being, that is made up of both, soul and body. Human beings are dense and massive in their creation, and therefore placed at the lowest level.

title: Soul that commands to evil
text: Soul That Commands Evil Reptilian Brain It is the part of the human brain that deals with simple, reactive aspects of our human nature such as: Dilation of pupils, their expansion and contraction based on the presence or absence of light; Function of breathing We share this with the lowest of all creatures. This is when the نفس is in a "reactionary" mode to the need of physical sensations, through the acquisition of material objects. It is a reptilian response to desire - without any intervention or opposition from the intellect. When the body enslaves the soul, human beings get reduced to the level beasts, and man starts acting like reptiles. The Quran calls this state of the soul: النفس الامارۃ بالسوء . We see these words used in سورۃ یوسف when the ministers wife tries to seduce یوسف علیہ السلام and he says to her: وَمَآ أُبَرِّئُ نَفْسِىٓ ۚ إِنَّ ٱلنَّفْسَ لَأَمَّارَةٌۢ بِٱلسُّوٓءِ إِلَّا مَا رَحِمَ رَبِّىٓ ۚ إِنَّ رَبِّى غَفُورٌۭ رَّحِيمٌۭ And I do not acquit myself. Indeed, the soul commands to evil, except those upon which my master has mercy. Indeed, my master is Forgiving and Merciful. نفس means soul الامارۃ comes from the word أمر which means to command - or to give an order. Implies a force Loss of freedom and choice سُوء means ugly. The word سَوْءَةَ which means a corpse This world turns things ugly. النفس الامارۃ بالسوء means, the soul that commands to that which is ugly In psychological terms, this state of the soul is called the id or the libido. It has no concern for what is permissible and what is forbidden. All it cares is to satisfy its hunger. All it desires is a relief - which is the exact same principle on which lust operates. Lust requires relief - and the only way it knows how to find relief, is by chasing and acquiring what it desires. Allah has associated this lowest power of our natures قوۃ الشھوانیۃ with the genitals, The word شھوۃ comes from the root word ھوی , which means passion or lust in Arabic. Another word is غلم which also means lust or shackles. Our lust shackles and traps us. The prophet (Salallahu alayhi wa aalihee wa sallam) said wretched is the slave of the دینار and the درھم . Wretched is the slave of his clothes. Wretched is the slave of his riding beast. Because we have this natural tendency to become slaves of material possessions Allah placed a covenant on that organ, through the practice of circumcision ( خطنۃ ) in Islam. It is a reminder that we have an oath with Allah(Subhanahu wa Ta'ala) that we will not become slaves to that lowest part of our bodies. It's a sign of liberation, and a declaration of our freedom as Muslims - that we are the ones who have rejected the slavery of appetites and the base pleasures of this world. It's a recognition of the fact that we are اشرف المخلوقات - we are the best of creation. We are slaves that have been honored with the freedom of choice. We are عبد! We are not غلام of our base desires. Freedom is one of the greatest mercy of Allah It is only by Allah's mercy that we can escape the default state of our souls The mercy of Allah manifests in the form of Quran Messengers Muhammad Mustafa (Salallahu alayhi wa aalihee wa sallam), Maulana Ali (AS), Maulatina Fatima, Imams of the اھل البیت دعات مناصیب They are all manifestations of the mercy of our master, because they show us how to break the shackles of lust and appetites, and rise above them to activate the dormant potential of our فطرۃ , that we were born to do

title: 10 Fundamental Principles of knowledge
text: Definition ( الحد ) A definition is something that gets to the essence of a thing. It should "include" everything that the thing is; and "exclude" everything that the thing is not. Logicians define human being as a "rational animal". To be "human", is to be specifically from the animal kingdom (and not from any other kingdom, such as the mineral or plant kingdom) Additionally to be human is to also be rational Subject Matter ( الموضوع ) Subject of the knowledge that is being studied Benefit ( ثمرۃ ) What are the benefits of acquiring this knowledge? Virtue ( فضلہ ) What are the honored or dignified qualities of the subject? Will learning this subject make me noble? Will it make me respectable? Will it bring me honor? Who will it make me honorable before? Associations ( نسبۃ ) What other types of knowledge are associated with the subject being studied? What are the companion fields of knowledge that link in with this one? Source ( الواضع ) Who is the inventor or the designer of this knowledge? Where is this knowledge coming from? Names ( الأسماء ) The name or names of this field of knowledge? The word أسم is used for name as well as noun. It provides an identity to nouns. Identifying and understanding the terminology of the subject. Principles ( أستمداد ) The basic principles from which a particular knowledge is derived. What is the foundation of this knowledge? Legal Rulings ( الشارع و الحکم ) Is it فرض العین ? is it فرض الکِفایۃ ? Is it obligatory knowledge, or is it simply recommended? Details ( المسائل ) The intricate details of the subject. The topics that make up the body of that knowledge. و مَن دَرَى الجِميعُ حَازْ الشُّرَفَا The one who understands these 10 fundamentals, he attains the most honorable rank in that knowledge.

title: How do we come to know Allah?
text: Allah(Subhanahu wa Ta'ala) created man in his own image Allah is أحد (unique,) and each human soul is also unique Allah is القادر . and the human soul is in complete control of its body. Allah is سمیع , he is بصیر Allah can hear and see - and so can human beings. The Quran says: وَفِىٓأَنفُسِكُمْ ۚ أَفَلَا تُبْصِرُونَ I am within your own souls! Do you not see? سَنُرِيهِمْ آيَتِنَا فِى ٱلأٓفَاقِ وَفِىٓ أَنفُسِهِمْ حَتَّىٰ يَتَبَيَّنَ لَهُمْ أَنَّهُ ٱلْحَقُّ We will show them Our Signs in the universe, and in their own selves, until it becomes absolutely clear to them, that this is the truth. The more we study and reflect upon Allah(Subhanahu wa Ta'ala)'s creation, the more we will come to know him.

title: Slavery of the body
text: Slave is someone who has no free will of his own His only purpose and function in life is to serve the will of his master. It is the master that controls and dictates the slave. In its default state, the pleasures of the body are dictating the actions of the soul. Slave works round the clock to serve the master People work constantly to acquire material possessions in the hopes of finding satisfaction through things Slave does not get to enjoy what it serves to its master People hardly get to enjoy the possessions they have slaved to acquire Slave has to respond to its master immediately. It's worth observing how we react to certain "objects" in life, such as cell phones, TV Shows etc. As نفس - we are meant to be higher in the hierarchy than the body Whenever someone (something) lower in a hierarchy, enslaves someone higher in that hierarchy, it serves as a horrible master (tyrant).

title: The purpose and end goal of intellect
text: Third sentence: قال رسول اللہ صلی اللہ علیہ وآلہ و سلم إنّي إيّاك آمر وإيّاك أنهى۔ وإيَاك أٌعاقِب وإيّاك أُثِيب Indeed! to you are my commands and my prohibitions. And for you are my rewards and punishments. There are other words that could have been used for the intellect such as نُھی or وُقوف . But Allah specifically used the word العقل which means to bind . The intellect has been designed to bind the heart to Allah(Subhanahu wa Ta'ala). Being bound alludes to the idea of gradually pulling ourselves closer to Allah till we can come face to face with him

title: Studying the heartbeat
text: The word قلب and قبل are closely related, just as the words علم and عمل are closely related. The word قبل means to precede, and it is related to the قلب , because the spiritual heart precedes in the recognition of its master. The recognizes the answer to the question - الست بربکم؟ Allah made us witness his glory and majesty in the spiritual realm and sent us in this world to recognize him once again through his signs in the horizons and the soul. Fusilat [41:53] [ سورۃ ۴۱] فصلت ‏سَنُرِيهِمْ آيَتِنَا فِى ٱلأٓفَاقِ وَفِىٓ أَنفُسِهِمْ حَتَّىٰ يَتَبَيَّنَ لَهُمْ أَنَّهُ ٱلْحَقُّ ۗ أَوَلَمْ يَكْفِ بِرَبِّكَ أَنَّهُۥ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍۢ شَهِيدٌ ‎ ۵۳ We will show them Our Signs in the universe, and in their ownselves, until it becomes manifest to them that this is the truth. Is it not sufficient in regard to your Lord that He is a Witness over all things? This world presents itself to us as an الہ . These are lifeless gods called idols Created by the hands of human beings Allah is not a lifeless god He is an ever living God ( الحی القیوم - the ever living and the eternal maintainer) and a life giving God. Freedom of choice is the ultimate test The heart beat symbolizes purification and rejection of idols from our spiritual lives The heart beat is saying "Allah" The heart beat is extremely subtle and we are not consciously aware of it Muslims face the same problems in terms of mechanical motions. This is why every practice in Islam begins with an intention - نیۃ

title: Protecting the heart
text: The spiritual heart must be protected at all cost. Allah placed it in the most secure position in the body The genitals and the abdomen are left without protection A successful attack on the groin or abdomen will take down the strongest man on earth.

title: Negation vs Affirmation
text: The command of لا الہ الا اللہ is the what the intellect has been designed to understand - because it includes both states لا الہ , is negation and الا اللہ is affirmation The negation and affirmation that stands before us is دنیا and آخرۃ The نفس wants the دنیا ; the عقل wants the آخرۃ . And the heart is stuck in between the two

title: How are we doing as Caliphs?
text: Allah says that he shows us our reality in the horizons and the soul: سَنُرِيهِمْ آيَتِنَا فِى ٱلأٓفَاقِ وَفِىٓ أَنفُسِهِمْ حَتَّىٰ يَتَبَيَّنَ لَهُمْ أَنَّهُ ٱلْحَقُّ ۗ أَوَلَمْ يَكْفِ بِرَبِّكَ أَنَّهُۥ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍۢ شَهِيدٌ We will show them Our Signs in the horizons, and in our own souls. Allah will let us know through signs of what we see around us - our own truth and realities! Allah says: The Romans [30:41] [ سورۃ ۳۰] الروم ‏ظَهَرَ ٱلْفَسَادُ فِى ٱلْبَرِّ وَٱلْبَحْرِ بِمَا كَسَبَتْ أَيْدِى ٱلنَّاسِ لِيُذِيقَهُم بَعْضَ ٱلَّذِى عَمِلُوا۟ لَعَلَّهُمْ يَرْجِعُونَ ‎ ۴۱ Mischief, corruption, pollution has appeared on the land and sea, because of what the hands of men have earned. The reason Allah makes our negative actions have negative consequences is for us to return back to him. Scholars say that ‏ظَهَرَ ٱلْفَسَادُ - is actually النقص فِى ٱلْبَرِّ وَٱلْبَحْرِ - diminished blessings of the earth. It also means القحط which is droughts and famines and all the harms that come in agriculture. Human beings, even though created in the best of forms with all his divine attributes is capable of extreme corruption when he deviates from his divinity The angles expressed concern when Allah mentioned the creation of Adam to them. قَالُوٓا۟ أَتَجْعَلُ فِيهَا مَن يُفْسِدُ فِيهَا وَيَسْفِكُ ٱلدِّمَآءَ The word فساد embodies the meanings of rottenness, corruption, decay, decomposition, putrid, depravity, wickedness, viciousness, inequity, immorality, weakness, perversion, wrongness, incorrectness. Pollution in the English dictionary is defined as: An undesirable state of the natural environment being contaminated with harmful substances as consequences of human activities. Human beings are responsible for the following types of corruptions: Fires due to extreme climate changes Air pollution Noise pollution Desertification Degradation of health in sentient beings Endangered Species Adam (AS) was created in جنۃ so that he could witness and experience the beauty of heaven in order to cultivate the earth in its image. Human beings, as خلیفۃ اللہ have failed miserably!

title: Conviction In Faith
