text: Faith requires the combination of reflection with revelation The essence of the teachings of the messenger is wisdom The Congregation [62:2] [ سورۃ ۶۲] الجمعۃ ‏هُوَ ٱلَّذِى بَعَثَ فِى ٱلْأُمِّيِّۦنَ رَسُولًۭا مِّنْهُمْ يَتْلُوا۟ عَلَيْهِمْ آيَتِهِۦ وَيُزَكِّيهِمْ وَيُعَلِّمُهُمُ ٱلْكِتَبَ وَٱلْحِكْمَةَ وَإِن كَانُوا۟ مِن قَبْلُ لَفِى ضَلَلٍۢ مُّبِينٍۢ ‎ ۲ He it is Who sent among the unlettered ones a Messenger from among themselves, reciting to them His Verses, purifying them, and teaching them the Book and wisdom. And verily, they had been before in manifest error; A pure heart with an intact فطرۃ and a sound intellect are preconditions for attaining wisdom If a person tries to solve the mysteries of this universe only by the use of their intellect; without taking into consideration his فطرۃ ; he will fail in finding Allah. Pure intellect produces philosophy . The destination of all philosophy is either atheism or agnosticism. Conviction in faith is born when the finding of the intellect are combined with the فطرۃ of the heart. When one's reflections are affirmed and validated by the teachings of the messenger, faith comes forth with confidence and a strong conviction

title: Disbelief Vs Transgression
text: There are two words used for Iblis by the Quran. He is called a کافر and he called him a فاسق . The Cow [2:34] [ سورۃ ۲] البقرۃ ‏وَإِذْ قُلْنَا لِلْمَلَٓئِكَةِ ٱسْجُدُوا۟ لِآدَمَ فَسَجَدُوٓا۟ إِلَّآ إِبْلِيسَ أَبَىٰ وَٱسْتَكْبَرَ وَكَانَ مِنَ ٱلْكَفِرِينَ ‎ ۳۴ And [mention] when We said to the angels, "Prostrate before Adam"; so they prostrated, except for Iblis. He refused and was arrogant and became of the disbelievers. The Cave [18:50] [ سورۃ ۱۸] الکھف ‏وَإِذْ قُلْنَا لِلْمَلَٓئِكَةِ ٱسْجُدُوا۟ لِآدَمَ فَسَجَدُوٓا۟ إِلَّآ إِبْلِيسَ كَانَ مِنَ ٱلْجِنِّ فَفَسَقَ عَنْ أَمْرِ رَبِّهِ ۵۰ And when We said to the angels;"Prostrate to Adam." So they prostrated except Iblis. He was one of the jinn; he transgressed the boundaries of his master Disbeliever The word کافر comes from the word کفر which means to intentionally hide something. If one accidentally hides something, or if one denies the truth based on a lack of knowledge its called نسیان . The word کافر is based on a phonetic pattern that indicates repetition. The Arabic word for a farmer is کافر because he is someone who hides hides the seeds in the ground - with intent. Transgressor The word فاسق comes from the word فِسقْ which means to break an agreement; to go out of bounds. فاسق also being on the same phonetic pattern as " کافر " means someone who breaks agreements and transgresses intentionally and constantly The disbelief of Iblis was in his disobedience

title: The gift of guilt
text: Guilt takes away the pleasure of the sin - what's called گناہ بےلزت in Urdu. It makes the sin not worth it. The more intensity this feeling of guilt develops, the more fierce the نفس becomes in its battle against its passions. Spirituality in modern discourse has been replaced by psychology. Science takes problems of the unseen, and tries to find solutions in the seen. Allah begins سورۃ القیامۃ by saying: The Rising of the Dead [75:1-2] [ سورۃ ۷۵] القیامۃ ‏لَآ أُقْسِمُ بِيَوْمِ ٱلْقِيَمَةِ ‎ ۱ I swear by the Day of Resurrection; ‏وَلَآ أُقْسِمُ بِٱلنَّفْسِ ٱللَّوَّامَةِ ‎ ۲ And I swear by the self-reproaching soul. The purpose of an alarm is to warn us of a danger. Guilt is the alarm that Allah(Subhanahu wa Ta'ala) has placed within our فطرۃ . When we violate our internal spiritual programming, this alarm called ٱلوم - guilt goes off inside us. Guilt is actually a gift from Allah, that tells us that what we're doing is violating your soul. We do not feel the danger of this violation until the day of judgment, when its effects will no longer be ignored.

title: Importance of Intellect and its perfection
text: Second sentence: قال رسول اللہ صلی اللہ علیہ وآلہ و سلم ثمّ قال: وعزّتي وجلالي! ما خلقت خلقاً هو أحبٌ إلىً منك ؛ ولا أكمَلتُكِ إلاّ فِي من أُحِبٌ Then He said: Go back! So it went back. Then he said: By My power and majesty! I did not create any creature dearer to me than you Allah swears by two of his attributes: عزۃ and جلال - power and majesty Intellect has the ability to recognize Allah's عزۃ and جلال his power and majesty through the study of his signs in the horizon and the soul Intellect also creates these two particular qualities in the human soul Intellect is a gift from Allah He used the word کمال which means perfection instead of تمام which means complete There is a difference between complete intellect and perfect intellect Intellectual perfection is in recognizing the عزۃ and the جلالۃ - the power and majesty of Allah(Subhanahu wa Ta'ala) It alludes to a hierarchy and levels of love and closeness to Allah.

title: Summary Of Quranic Theme
text: There is no mention of a messenger, a prophet or a divine book in these 8 verses. None of the metaphysical realities have been mentioned. The union of reflection and revelation has been beautifully described in the فاتحۃ Allah immediately responds to that plea in the next سورۃ in the following سورۃ البقرۃ The Cow [2:1-2] [ سورۃ ۲] البقرۃ ‏الٓمٓ ‎ ۱ ‏ذَلِكَ ٱلْكِتَبُ لَا رَيْبَ ۛ فِيهِ ۛ هُدًۭى لِّلْمُتَّقِينَ ‎ ۲ ( 1 ) Alif-Lam-Mîm. . ( 2 ) That Book about which there is no doubt, is a guidance for those conscious of Allah The book only provides guidance to those who are conscious of Allah. Who wish to walk the straight path. The word َ فَسَق means to rot. If the فطرۃ - the natural predisposition has rotten, one cannot find guidance from this book. Intellect is like the ceiling of a roof In the light of pure reason alone, one can easily stray from the right path

title: AQBALA and ADBARA
text: First sentence of HADEES قال رسول اللہ صلی اللہ علیہ وآلہ و سلم أول ما خلق اللہُ عزّ وجلّ العقل۔ ثمّ قال له: أقبل! فأقبل ثمّ قال له: أدبر! فأدبر The first thing that Allah created is the intellect. When Allah created the Intellect, He examined it. He then said to it: Step forward, so it stepped forward. Precedence determines closeness as stated by Quran The Event [56:10-11] [ سورۃ ۵۶] الواقعۃ ‏وَٱلسَّبِقُونَ ٱلسَّبِقُونَ ‎ ۱۰ And those foremost will be foremost. ‏أُو۟لَٓئِكَ ٱلْمُقَرَّبُونَ ‎ ۱۱ These will be those nearest to Allah. أقبلۃ means to come face to face while أدبرۃ means to turn one's back Intellect was asked to demonstrate obedience and disobedience There are multiple levels of obedience and disobedience Face manifests the essence of human spirit through the 5 senses. There is nothing beneficial in the back In this context, to face Allah means to recognize Allah through the faculties of the 5 senses To turn away from Allah means to focus on everything other than Allah Intellect has the ability to understand a command, interpret and then respond. Calls can be of two types Call of obedience asking us to face Allah Call of disobedience asking us to turn away from Allah

title: Human body is a metaphor for this world
text: Just as the soul is higher in the hierarchy than the body, the afterlife (آخرۃ) is higher in rank than this physical world ( دنیا ) The word دنیا comes from the word دنی which means "low", or "inferior". آخرۃ comes from the root word خیر which means better or superior. Everything in this world is naturally designed to move towards corruption Most beautiful mansions, best cars, clothes, delicious food, physical beauty, everything deteriorates with time Rasul Allah(Salallahu alayhi wa aalihee wa sallam) said: جعل اللہ لِمَا یَخرُج مِن الأبنِ آدَمْ مَثَلَ لِی الدُّنِیَا Allah has made this world - this physical world a metaphor for what the son of Adam defecates. Allah made the process of digestion and excretion a metaphor for this world. Because the nature of this world is consumption and excretion: anabolism catabolism. It's all about breaking things down, extracting from it, and then casting it away. In the end everything we use goes to destruction. Just as the body will never be permanently satiated no matter how much food we provide, the desires of the soul can never be fully satisfied through material objects. Newer things will always replace their older versions When we look at our children, we are looking at our replacement The young whippersnapper that we train, is the new replacement for our jobs, Why are we all serving this treacherous master (body) so faithfully? Why are we choosing to be غلام when we can choose to become a عبد ?

title: Slow Deception
text: The Heights [7:22] [ سورۃ ۷] الاعراف ‏فَدَلَّىٰهُمَا بِغُرُورٍۢ ‎ ۲۲ So he misled them with deception. The word دلا in Arabic is a verb. The infinitive verb تدلیۃ is used for a bucket with a rope attached to it. When one pulls the bucket slowly towards, in an unnoticeable manner - that's دلا If pulled hard and fast ادلا is used. The word دلا suggests Iblis didn't immediately call them to the حرام . He got them to it very slowly, in a very calculated manner. Iblis slowly and very patiently tempts us into doing wrong.

title: Ilah
text: The word Allah comes from the root word الہ . It is a very generic term - like a "book" or a "car" The word الہ is a generic, indefinite term that can be used for many things. By applying the definite article "the" to it, which is ال in Arabic; it generates the word اللہ . The word " اللہ " is the specific form of the generic word الہ . Allah is actually ال + الہ .

title: Composite Words
text: Composite word is like a jigsaw puzzle. Every piece of the puzzle has a separate independent meaning that come together to create a complete image of what the word means. If the word الہ has 4 different meanings (A, B, C and D), then the sum of those meanings will give us the complete understanding of the word الہ , The meaning of الہ is not "A" or "B" or "C" or "D". It is the sum of all those meanings. It is A + B + C + D.

title: Allah Doe Not Need Gratitude
text: So Allah continues to say: وَمَن يَشْكُرْ فَإِنَّمَا يَشْكُرُ لِنَفْسِهِ Gratitude only helps the one being grateful. It makes him a happier and satisfied person. The glass is always half full. The demand of the Quran is wisdom. وَمَن كَفَرَ فَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ غَنِىٌّ حَمِيدٌۭ The word کفر is used in the context of ingratitude (instead of belief) because To the Arab a disbeliever and an ungrateful person are the same thing The ingrate is deliberately and forcefully hiding his sense of gratitude this is rising from within his heart. It is impossible to be completely grateful to Allah for a single blessing

title: Gratitude To Allah
text: Luqman was a wise man who combined the purity of his heart's فطرۃ with his intellect to generate his faith in his creator. His faith was rewarded by Allah(Subhanahu wa Ta'ala) in the form of wisdom - وَلَقَدْ آتَيْنَا لُقْمَنَ ٱلْحِكْمَةَ Wisdom is to gain beneficial knowledge and act upon it. Faith and wisdom in a sense are directly proportional to each other. أَنِ ٱشْكُرْ لِلَّهِ Be grateful to Allah Gratitude is the product of a healthy heart with an intact فطرۃ Recognition of the benefactor is the function of the intellect وَمَن يَشْكُرْ فَإِنَّمَا يَشْكُرُ لِنَفْسِهِۦ ۖ وَمَن كَفَرَ فَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ غَنِىٌّ حَمِيدٌۭ The one who is grateful, only benefits himself. And if one is ungrateful, then Allah is completely self-sufficient and forever praiseworthy. Allah has no need for the gratitude of creation

title: Do Not Commit Shirk
text: Scholars agree that Luqman gave this advise to his son on his death bed. A dying man talks about the things that are extremely important to him. Things that represent the essence of his life يَبُنَىَّ لَا تُشْرِكْ بِٱللَّهِ My son! do not associate partners with Allah. The word شِرک and شُکر are made up of the exact same letters. If you do not place your شُکر where it belongs, you will inevitably end up in شِرک . Sound Intellect draws the conclusion that there can only be one God. The act of شِرک is a grave injustice, which can have only one of two forms: We commit شِرک in the following areas in our daily lives. Worship Obedience Trust Love The root cause for شرک can be traced back directly to a flaw in our شکر

title: Conclusion
text: Expiation Of Sins Elevation of Rank Reminder for the power of Lordship over us Reminder of our abject servant-hood and our complete state of resignation and brokenness before the will and power of Allah(Subhanahu wa Ta'ala) Makes us sincere with Allah Helps us removes distractions Provides us with the opportunity of self reflection Puts us in a humbled state before God and prevents us from arrogance and from evil, pride and tyranny Teaches us forbearance Enables us to earn the love of Allah though patience Makes us grateful for what he had Makes us joyful thinking of all the benefits we're gaining out of it at the end Creates reliance on Allah Provides an opportunity for us to repent and forgive others Makes us more compassionate, and reminds us of Allah's compassion for us Makes us content with the decree of Allah

title: Accountability
text: Conversation returns back to Luqman's story. Luqman [31:16] [ سورۃ ۳۱] لقمان ‏يَبُنَىَّ إِنَّهَآ إِن تَكُ مِثْقَالَ حَبَّةٍۢ مِّنْ خَرْدَلٍۢ فَتَكُن فِى صَخْرَةٍ أَوْ فِى ٱلسَّمَوَتِ أَوْ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ يَأْتِ بِهَا ٱللَّهُ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَطِيفٌ خَبِيرٌۭ ‎ ۱۶ [And Luqman said], "O my son, indeed if wrong should be the weight of a mustard seed and should be within a rock or [anywhere] in the heavens or in the earth, Allah will bring it forth. Indeed, Allah is Subtle and Acquainted.

title: Why religion feels uncomfortable?
text: Imagine you are sitting in a movie theater watching a 3D Disney movie. You are completely engrossed in the movie; dazzled by the beautiful colors on the screen; mesmerized by the music. Your emotions and senses are heightened, and your heart is racing with anticipation. You just can't tear your eyes away from the screen. In that state, I come to you and start shaking you and saying, "Hey! You think this is beautiful? Let me show you what beauty is! Come outside with me. There is this incredibly beautiful rainbow that is stretching across the entire horizon. There is a beautiful river flowing under that rainbow and the weather is simply incredibly! But it's only going to last for a few minutes. So come on! Leave this dark dingy room and come outside with me! All these colors that you're seeing here, are just projections from a lens. The real beauty is outside this dark room. Come with me!" But because you're only interested in watching the movie, my invitation will feel like an interruption to you. You won't be too happy about it. Why? Am I not speaking the truth? Isn't it true that you're just watching a movie and the real world is in fact outside the movie theater? Then why are you getting upset? Because right now, you want to watch the movie. Everything I'm calling you to might interest you *after* the movie is done; but right now, you can't be bothered about it. And then finally the movie ends, but now when you step out - it's all dark and the sun has set. It is exactly the same thing with the Quran. We are people who are completely seduced and engrossed - intoxicated by the movie and drama of our lives. Our minds, our thoughts, our goals, our plans, all revolve around our universe, our bodies and our comforts - our likes and dislikes. In this hypnotic state, the Quran comes and shakes us up and says, "Wake up! Wake up! This world isn't real! It's just temporary! It's going to end! I have prepared a whole other world for you, that is permanent and infinitely better than this world! Don't fall in love with the fleeting passions of this world! Make me the object of your desire and I'll show you what real love is! I will show you what fidelity is. Take my hand. Come with me!". And we respond with rejection! "Not right now! I'm not ready for it yet! I know what you're saying is true, but it's not for me right now. This is not a good time. I'm too busy with the drama of my life. Maybe later!" Why do we do that? Not because we disagree with the Quran. Not because what the Quran says does not make sense. Quran touches the hearts and intellects of most people. The reason we find it difficult, and the reason we reject it, is because what the Quran asks us to do, does not feel comfortable. Because the Quran tries to wake us up from our deep, sweet, dreamy slumber; and nobody likes waking up from it. Quran takes us out of our comfort zones; it asks us to stand against our desires and passion; to redefine our priorities - none of which feels pleasant. Because we're all trapped, and we don't even see the trap.

title: Clothing For The Body
text: The verses continue, and now Allah says: The Heights [7:26] [ سورۃ ۷] الاعراف ‏يَبَنِىٓ آدَمَ قَدْ أَنزَلْنَا عَلَيْكُمْ لِبَاسًۭا يُوَرِى سَوْءَتِكُمْ وَرِيشًۭا ‎ ۲۶ O Children of Adam! We have bestowed raiment upon you to cover yourselves and as an adornment Allah after telling us the story of our parents, begins talking to us Allah compares his gift of clothing with revelation through the use of the word أَنزَلْنَا The word ریش is the Arabic word for a peacock's feathers when it opens up its feathers Clothing is used for beautification. Allah wants us to dress nicely to show him our appreciation of his gift

title: Clothing For The Soul
text: The Heights [7:26] [ سورۃ ۷] الاعراف وَلِبَاسُ ٱلتَّقْوَىٰ ذَلِكَ خَيْرٌۭ ۚ ذَلِكَ مِنْ آيَتِ ٱللَّهِ لَعَلَّهُمْ يَذَّكَّرُونَ ‎ ۲۶ and the raiment of righteousness, that is better. Such are among the Ayat of Allah, that they may remember. The clothing of تقوی (piety) is better; referring to the awareness of Allah's presence and his watchful eyes at all times Allah honored us with clothing for our body, and clothing for our soul. Both types of clothing are from the signs of Allah so that we can remember him and our purpose on earth The Heights [7:27] [ سورۃ ۷] الاعراف ‏يَبَنِىٓ آدَمَ لَا يَفْتِنَنَّكُمُ ٱلشَّيْطَنُ كَمَآ أَخْرَجَ أَبَوَيْكُم مِّنَ ٱلْجَنَّةِ يَنزِعُ عَنْهُمَا لِبَاسَهُمَا لِيُرِيَهُمَا سَوْءَتِهِمَآ ۗ إِنَّهُۥ يَرَىٰكُمْ هُوَ وَقَبِيلُهُۥ مِنْ حَيْثُ لَا تَرَوْنَهُمْ ۗ إِنَّا جَعَلْنَا ٱلشَّيَطِينَ أَوْلِيَآءَ لِلَّذِينَ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ ‎ ۲۷ O Children of Adam! Let not Shaitan deceive you, as he got your parents out of Paradise, stripping them of their raiments, to show them their private parts. Verily, he and Qabîluhu see you from where you cannot see them. Verily, We made the Shayatin Auliya' for those who believe not. يَبَنِىٓ آدَمَ لَا يَفْتِنَنَّكُمُ ٱلشَّيْطَنُ كَمَآ أَخْرَجَ أَبَوَيْكُم مِّنَ ٱلْجَنَّةِ Oh children of Adam, don't let شیطان do to you what he did to your parents. In other words, learn the lesson from the story of your parents that happened before you. The Arabs say: السَّعِیدُ مِنَ الطَّعَتْ بِغَیرِہِ True happiness is for the one who learns his lessons from other people. We don't have to prove those lessons for ourselves. This is the idea that those who don't know their history are condemned to repeat it over and over again. This is why we're being told this story. Because while we're on this earth there is a constant struggle is going on. And there are two components of this struggle. Our desire - because without desire we cannot be convinced even to try something. So you and I have this thing called ھوی caprice or desire. That is the first component. The second are these unseen forces constantly working in the world against us. Constantly whispering and making evil suggestions to us; urging us to explore that ھوی . To test it. To try it. That's ذوق or ذاق The desire to check it out. See what it is like. That's what got our parents expelled from جنۃ Do you remember what Allah stated in the story of Adam? فَدَلَّىٰهُمَا بِغُرُورٍۢ ۚ فَلَمَّا ذَاقَا ٱلشَّجَرَةَ بَدَتْ لَهُمَا سَوْءَتُهُمَا Iblis deceived them by urging them to desire an eternal stay in heaven فَلَمَّا ذَاقَا ; and when they explored that desire. Same word used here - فَلَمَّا ذَاقَا . You see, they had this desire in them to live in جنۃ forever. If they did not have that desire they would never have listened to Iblis. Even when he swore to them, they wouldn't have listened. If they had no desire! But they did have that desire and Iblis took advantage of that. He made them explore it. But it was just the taste, the ذوق that constituted as their disobedience. Then Allah says: يَنزِعُ عَنْهُمَا لِبَاسَهُمَا لِيُرِيَهُمَا سَوْءَتِهِمَآ He pulled off their clothes so that he could expose their ugliness . Look at these words يَنزِعُ he pulled off their clothes. The word نزع means to pull out. You know how when someone is about to die, in their last moments, they say he is in the state of نزع . It means his soul is being pulled out of their body. This verse says: Iblis pulled their clothes off of them. Now that would indicate that he had more power over them! Yes or no? Suggesting to take off clothes is one thing; pulling off clothes is something else. So how come Allah says Iblis pulled off their clothes? This ayah is teaching us - and please listen to this very carefully. This ayah is teaching us that disobeying Allah grants authority to شیطان . Disobeying Allah grants power to شیطان over us. Every time you and I dress inappropriately, in both the physical and spiritual sense, we give more and more power to شیطان . He gets more power over us, until he becomes so strong and powerful that he can simply command us to do what he wants us to do; and we obey and comply like his slave. He simply commands and we obey! The only power Allah had gave شیطان originally was to do what? وسوسۃ to whisper. But we give him the real power of command by listening; by accepting and then complying with his whisper. And the more we do it, the more power he gains over us. He keeps getting strong and stronger and stronger until he gains complete authority over us. He can now simply command us to do something. Because Allah says. ٱلشَّيْطَنُ يَعِدُكُمُ ٱلْفَقْرَ وَيَأْمُرُكُم بِٱلْفَحْشَآءِ شیطان promises you bankruptcy. Bankruptcy where? Before Allah! He promises that by the time you stand before Allah, I will make sure you do not have a single good deed to your name. يَعِدُكُمُ ٱلْفَقْرَ and how does he do that? وَيَأْمُرُكُم بِٱلْفَحْشَآءِ He commands you to lewdeness, to shamelessness. Allah didn't say یُوَسوِسُ لکم الفحشاء . یَنسِحُو لکم ان تفشحو He suggested lewdness to you. He whispered shamelessness to you. No no no no no. Allah says. He commands you to shamelessness. How does he command you? People have said to me: I just can't stop watching porn. I can't stop smoking. I know you can't! Because شیطان is commanding you; because you keep disobeying Allah. We have to cut it off. That's the only way for شیطان to let go of us. We've got to stop - cold turkey. We have to take back that control by giving that control back to Allah -where it belongs. Because Allah said to شیطان إِنَّ عِبَادِى لَيْسَ لَكَ عَلَيْهِمْ سُلْطَنٌ You will have no authority, and no power over my slaves. What does that mean? If they're not really my slaves, you will have complete authority over them. ‏إِنَّ عِبَادِى لَيْسَ لَكَ عَلَيْهِمْ سُلْطَنٌ . this is our legacy in this world. Then Allah says. إِنَّهُۥ يَرَىٰكُمْ هُوَ وَقَبِيلُهُۥ مِنْ حَيْثُ لَا تَرَوْنَهُمْ شیطان is constantly watching you - he and his tribe of followers. Did he not say to Allah that he will sit on the straight path, waiting to ambush us? And when somebody is waiting to ambush, can you see them or not? Allah says I made that part of your test! He is watching you! He is waiting for you. He and his entire tribe are ready to ambush you - مِنْ حَيْثُ لَا تَرَوْنَهُمْ from a place where you can't even see them. إِنَّا جَعَلْنَا ٱلشَّيَطِينَ أَوْلِيَآءَ لِلَّذِينَ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ All the devils, and legions of them have made friends with people who don't believe. People who don't believe will accept that whisper, and they will enjoy that whisper. They will say, my parents aren't home right now - I can watch whatever I want! I can do whatever I want! Nobody will ever know. I can smoke, I can do drugs, I can take a tictac later. Nobody will know. But Allah will know. So after narrating the entire story of Adam and Iblis, Allah talks to us - the children of Adam, and he warns us - Don't repeat the same mistake your parents made. Don't listen to شیطان . He got your parents kicked out of جنۃ . He will do the same to you. So be careful! I warned them too! They didn't listen. I'm warning you guys now. Be careful! Don't disobey me. And if you do end up disobeying me, then follow the footsteps of your parents. Accept your fault. Take responsibility for your actions, and then repent before me. Don't follow the footsteps of Iblis and come up with logical explanations, rationalizations and justifications for your disobedience. Because he will entice you. He will attack you. And once you give him power through your constant disobedience to me, and your constant obedience to him - he will then make you his slave. He will command you to sin, and you will comply. Why does he want you to sin? Because he wants to dishonor you before Allah. He wants to take off your clothes. Clothing - that Allah has equated with revelation. Clothing for our body as well as our soul. I have made physical clothing, not just a covering for your ugliness, but also a means of adornment for you. So that you can look beautiful. And I have made piety - تقوی a clothing for your soul, so that it not only covers up your sins, your weaknesses, but also adorns your personality and makes you respectable before Allah and his creation. And this spiritual clothing of تقوی is far better than the physical clothing - وَلِبَاسُ ٱلتَّقْوَىٰ ذَلِكَ خَيْرٌۭ . Why? Because in order to be pious, you will need to first learn to dress appropriately. You will need to dress modestly. You can't dress in a lewd manner and be pious. That's why it is better between the two. Also because it adorns your soul, which will eventually return back to me. Your body is going to decay in the dirt. So what's more important? Clothing of the body or clothing of the soul? Clothing of the soul! But you can't have one without the other. Now how do we create this spiritual clothing? What's the raw material for us to create this spiritual clothing- this تقوی ? It is this revelation - this Quran. That's why I sent down revelation, and this is why I sent down clothing.

title: Shifting Blame
text: The Heights [7:28] [ سورۃ ۷] الاعراف ‏وَإِذَا فَعَلُوا۟ فَحِشَةًۭ قَالُوا۟ وَجَدْنَا عَلَيْهَآ آبَآءَنَا وَٱللَّهُ أَمَرَنَا بِهَا ۗ قُلْ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَا يَأْمُرُ بِٱلْفَحْشَآءِ ۖ أَتَقُولُونَ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ مَا لَا تَعْلَمُونَ ‎ ۲۸ And when they commit a Fahisha , they say: "We found our fathers doing it, and Allah has commanded us of it." Say: "Nay, Allah never commands of Fahisha . Do you say of Allah what you know not? The people of Quraish shifted blame by saying that their parents did it This verse is talking about us, because our children will say the same thing to us, if we do not stop shifting blame on our parents On judgment day, this shift of blame will not help us. We will be abandoned by all our loved ones Allah clearly states that he does not command us to shamelessness.

title: Justice & Prayer
text: The Heights [7:29] [ سورۃ ۷] الاعراف ‏قُلْ أَمَرَ رَبِّى بِٱلْقِسْطِ ۖ وَأَقِيمُوا۟ وُجُوهَكُمْ عِندَ كُلِّ مَسْجِدٍۢ وَٱدْعُوهُ مُخْلِصِينَ لَهُ ٱلدِّينَ ۚ كَمَا بَدَأَكُمْ تَعُودُونَ ‎ ۲۹ Say :My Lord has commanded justice and that you should face Him only in each and every place of worship, in prayers, and invoke Him only making your religion sincere to Him by not joining in worship any partner to Him and with the intention that you are doing your deeds for Allah's sake only. As He brought you in the beginning, so shall you be brought into being. Allah commands us to justice and prayer Justice at a societal level Prayer at a personal level Prayer is a tool to protect us against lewdness and shamelessness If we pray and still commit shameless acts, then we are not truly praying The Heights [7:31] [ سورۃ ۷] الاعراف ‏ يَبَنِىٓ آدَمَ خُذُوا۟ زِينَتَكُمْ عِندَ كُلِّ مَسْجِدٍۢ وَكُلُوا۟ وَٱشْرَبُوا۟ وَلَا تُسْرِفُوٓا۟ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ لَا يُحِبُّ ٱلْمُسْرِفِينَ ‎ ۳۱ O Children of Adam! Take your adornment, while praying and going round the Ka'bah, and eat and drink but waste not by extravagance, certainly He likes not Al-Musrifun . Allah asks us to take our beauty and our clothing seriously. He asks us to dress up appropriately when we stand before him in prayer or visit his mosque Allah wants us to enjoy all the blessings of Allah, but not waste them.

title: Iblis Denies Responsibility
text: ٰIblis is hell bent in proving to Allah that he made a mistake by choosing us over him. His only goal is to destroy us, just as he has completely destroyed so many nations before us. Quran reveals all the ways in which Iblis deceived nations and humiliated them into the hell fire Abraham [14:22] [ سورۃ ۱۴] ابراھیم ‏وَقَالَ ٱلشَّيْطَنُ لَمَّا قُضِىَ ٱلْأَمْرُ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ وَعَدَكُمْ وَعْدَ ٱلْحَقِّ وَوَعَدتُّكُمْ فَأَخْلَفْتُكُمْ ۖ وَمَا كَانَ لِىَ عَلَيْكُم مِّن سُلْطَنٍ إِلَّآ أَن دَعَوْتُكُمْ فَٱسْتَجَبْتُمْ لِى ۖ فَلَا تَلُومُونِى وَلُومُوٓا۟ أَنفُسَكُم ۖ مَّآ أَنَا۠ بِمُصْرِخِكُمْ وَمَآ أَنتُم بِمُصْرِخِىَّ ۖ ‎ ۲۲ And Shaitan will say when the matter has been decided: "Verily, Allah promised you a promise of truth. And I too promised you, but I betrayed you. I had no authority over you except that I called you, so you responded to me. So blame me not, but blame yourselves. I cannot help you, nor can you help me.

title: Do Not Repeat History
text: The Heights [7:27] [ سورۃ ۷] الاعراف ‏يَبَنِىٓ آدَمَ لَا يَفْتِنَنَّكُمُ ٱلشَّيْطَنُ كَمَآ أَخْرَجَ أَبَوَيْكُم مِّنَ ٱلْجَنَّةِ يَنزِعُ عَنْهُمَا لِبَاسَهُمَا لِيُرِيَهُمَا سَوْءَتِهِمَآ ۗ إِنَّهُۥ يَرَىٰكُمْ هُوَ وَقَبِيلُهُۥ مِنْ حَيْثُ لَا تَرَوْنَهُمْ ۗ إِنَّا جَعَلْنَا ٱلشَّيَطِينَ أَوْلِيَآءَ لِلَّذِينَ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ ‎ ۲۷ O Children of Adam! Let not Shaitan deceive you, as he got your parents out of Paradise, stripping them of their raiments, to show them their private parts. Verily, he and Qabîluhu see you from where you cannot see them. Verily, We made the Shayatin Auliya' for those who believe not. True happiness is for the one who learns his lessons from other people. There are two components to our constant struggle Our desire The unseen forces constantly working in the world against us Iblis deceives us by urging us to explore our desires, expressed by the word ذوق The word نزع means to pull out The last moments before death is called the state of نزع when the soul is being pulled out of the body. The word suggests the power of Iblis granted through our disobedience to Allah and our obedience to him Iblis is constantly watching us and attacking us from unseen places The raw material to create our spiritual clothing of تقوی is revelation - the Quran

title: Timelines
text: Physical Day - 24 Hours Religious Day - 57,000 Years Where are we today? SITR - Nutaqa

title: The Three Timeframes
text: Angels As we discussed earlier, this blissful period of 47,000 years is called دور کشف . The word کشف means "Reveal or Unveil,". The reason it's called کشف is because the Imam is always present to guide believers, much like the sun, which graciously shines and constantly provides warmth and light. The sun is a symbol of the Imam. This remarkable era is characterized by happiness, tranquility, and peace. During these 47,000 years, there is no evil, just as darkness cannot exist when the sun shines. This is why this serene period is referred to as "paradise" ( جنۃ ) in many verses of the Quran. Several different types of جنۃ exist; this is merely one example. Similarly, many forms of Hell exist that I will explain at the appropriate time. A crucial point to remember when studying Tavil is that the metaphor does not negate the original, nor does the original dismiss the metaphor. In other words, just because these 47,000 years symbolize paradise does not imply that actual paradise does not exist. What is paradise? Paradise is a place or state of bliss where all your desires are fulfilled, bringing you absolute contentment and peace. There is a spiritual paradise for the soul and a physical paradise for the body. People born during this period have a strong affinity for spirituality . During کشف , knowledge flows freely and abundantly through divine support ( تائید ) and inspiration rather than traditional academic learning methods. High-level, extremely subtle, and sophisticated knowledge is shared openly, like the sun shining brightly for everyone. Scholars explain that gaining knowledge is similar to soaking up the sun, much like we do today. They describe it as a dream-like experience . Just imagine how thrilling it must be to have a question and receive the perfect answer immediately from the Imam himself! Another remarkable aspect of this time is that individuals receive answers tailored precisely to their needs. Everyone has varying levels of tolerance for sunlight; some can bask in the sun's heat far more comfortably than others. Similarly, during this period, each person obtains answers to their inquiries based on their capabilities. This is the reason why spiritual growth blossoms at an exponential rate during this time. The knowledge these people acquire is not veiled in physical and conceptual metaphors . In this دور ستر, we have to focus on physical and material knowledge, which then helps us form abstract ideas. For example, we first learn how the sun lights up the moon and then understand how the moon reflects that light. Once we know this, we can use it as a symbol to recognize that, just like the sun gives light, the ناطق possesses essential knowledge, and the أساس reflects that knowledge, much like the moon. We need to understand physical phenomena before connecting them to deeper spiritual ideas. This is why we have all these systematic and gradual physical practices to support our spiritual faith. So, after a child first learns to pray five times a day, they are later taught the deeper esoteric meaning behind those actions and how they connect to their soul. That’s precisely what we’re doing now. We started with the physical story of Adam and are now using تاویل to uncover its spiritual reality. In کشف , learning doesn’t happen in steps—it’s smooth, lightning-fast, and without debate. There are no arguments , disputes, or debates. We argue when we are unsure or confused by different conflicting ideas in our minds, trying to figure out what’s right. But in کشف , there’s no wrong information, so there’s no conflict, confusion, or disagreement. But here's the most important thing to remember: the knowledge disseminated during this time of کشف is so subtle and lofty that it cannot be veiled into physical actions. This is why there are no physical obligations during this time. This is something people don't necessarily understand about کشف . It's not that the physical obligations are lifted as a favor or a blessing to create ease. They don't exist because the level of knowledge is so high and lofty that physical actions cannot veil it. This is also why no prophets and messengers are required during this period. It's not that they don't exist; they're just not needed. Why? Because the sun is shining brightly in the sky. If you remember, we learned in the Book of Faith that stars represent the prophets and Dua'at. Just as the sun shines brightly and overshadows the moon and stars, the presence and knowledge of the Imam are so intense and readily available that these lower stations become unnecessary for guidance. That doesn't mean they don't exist. Everyone born in this time is a true believer ( مومنین ). They don’t have moments of doubt like we do. Disobedience, arrogance, and pride do not exist during this time. People during this time remain in perpetual awe, worship, and submission out of their own free will. In other words, everyone born during this time is a perfect believer. Now, what other creature does this description remind you of? Interestingly, this is the very description of angels in the Quran! The Bee [16:49-50] [ سورۃ ۱۶] النحل ‏وَلِلَّهِ يَسْجُدُ مَا فِى ٱلسَّمَوَتِ وَمَا فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ مِن دَآبَّةٍۢ وَٱلْمَلَٓئِكَةُ وَهُمْ لَا يَسْتَكْبِرُونَ ‎ ۴۹ ‏يَخَافُونَ رَبَّهُم مِّن فَوْقِهِمْ وَيَفْعَلُونَ مَا يُؤْمَرُونَ ‎ ۵۰ ( 49 ) And to Allah prostate all that is in the heavens and all that is in the earth, of the live moving creatures and the angels, and they are not proud. ( 50 ) They fear their Lord above them, and they do what they are commanded. The Prophets [21:27] [ سورۃ ۲۱] الانبیائ ‏لَا يَسْبِقُونَهُۥ بِٱلْقَوْلِ وَهُم بِأَمْرِهِۦ يَعْمَلُونَ ‎ ۲۷ They speak not until He has spoken, and they act on His Command. Notice how the Quranic portrayal of angels perfectly aligns with the description of the people of Kashf. Angels are ethereal, spiritual, and radiant beings, free from all physical limitations and imperfections. They're beautifully depicted as winged creatures, symbolizing their elevated status in the spiritual realms of the Imam. Their ability to fly represents not only the heights of their knowledge, character, and spirituality but also the remarkable speed of their spiritual advancement. Jinn After these amazing 47,000 years of کشف , a new breed of people begins to emerge over the next 3,000 years, and they start developing a bit of an interest in materialism. While this inclination is very subtle compared to what we see today, the ideas begin to take root. This change gradually clouds mental clarity as people start to yearn for power, wealth, appearance, and social status. When the sun shines brightly overhead at its highest point (noon), objects don’t cast any shadows. But then, as it begins to set, objects start casting shadows, gradually growing longer as the sun continues its descent. Shadows are like footprints left behind when light encounters something in its way, just as evil appears where goodness is hindered or blocked. Darkness isn't an independent force; it only arises when light is blocked. A shadow is reality's illusion—a skewed outline that can sometimes be mistaken for the truth. Just as evil might disguise itself as power or deception, wearing the facade of wisdom, shadows can lead us on a chase towards emptiness, causing us to confuse absence with actual substance. In the same way, spiritual concepts begin to cast a shadow of physicality. Despite this, they maintain a profound spiritual connection and recognize the importance of following the Imam for their spiritual growth. However, the aspiration to learn directly from the Imam devolves into a desire to become teachers themselves. Their total submission gradually transitions towards a subtle longing for self-reliance. Little by little, they begin to feel attracted to the comforts and pleasures of the material world. It is essential to recognize that during the فترۃ period, both types of personalities appear. Similar to the twilight phase, where light and darkness coexist, one group continues to uphold the angelic traits of کشف. In contrast, the other group indicates a mental shift towards materialism and physical pleasure. At this time, no physical actions or commitments are necessary due to the elevated nature of the knowledge. The Quran describes these individuals as جن , beings created from smokeless fire. Unlike نور (light), which is calm, relaxing, and nurturing, fire نار tends to be flashy and captivating with the dance of its flames. While fire may be catchy to the eye, it doesn’t possess the purity of light. The term “smokeless” suggests that these individuals had a very subtle material nature. Proper combustion of dense material is needed to produce fire. Man The conclusion of 3000 years of spiritual twilight ( فترۃ ) brings about the start of 7000 years of Concealment ( ستر ). This timeframe is known as "The Period of Concealment" because the Imam goes into complete hiding. The sun finally sets, and darkness prevails. The people who emerge during this time are focused heavily on materialism and possessions. The conceptual inclinations for materialism established over the past 3,000 years strengthened during this period. This is the time when evil is allowed to prevail and thrive. Hence, the need for prophets arises, similar to the moon and stars appearing at night to guide travelers in the dark. Evil forces grow strong and establish their call toward atheism, agnosticism, materialism, consumerism, nudity, and sexual perversion in the name of freedom. All traits considered detestable in previous timeframes are celebrated and idolized during this period. Wealth, fame, and power, rather than knowledge, become the sought-after possessions. For instance, studying history reveals that singers, dancers, entertainers, and jesters were once viewed as lesser professionals. Kings employed them to entertain the royals and the elite. In contrast, today, these individuals are considered celebrities and are revered. The values and beliefs surrounding them have been reversed. What was once considered flawed and evil is now regarded as good, while what was once regarded as good is now seen as bad. Virtues like piety, modesty, and righteousness have been overshadowed by lewdness, perversion, and immorality. The shift in mindset transitions from submission to arrogance, moving from worshiping Allah to self-adulation. This fosters argumentative knowledge, resulting in confusion and mistrust. Various religions and sects emerge during this time, driven by personal desires, marking a period when evil seems to prevail. Figures like فرعون، ھامان، ابو جھل، ابولھب appear, opposing the Imams and prophets. Similar to harmful reptiles, rodents, and insects that hide in the earth during the day, they emerge from their concealed places as night falls. This reflects the current state of the world. It's not that Allah has lost His control; it’s simply the designated time for evil to rise. During this era, truth weakens while falsehood thrives. Believers born in this time struggle to fortify their faith and are more susceptible to mistakes. This is why humans are termed انسان , derived from نسی , meaning "to forget." The numerous distractions and temptations of this world easily lead us to overlook the spiritual dimension of our existence. The soul is drawn to physical desires and pleasures, losing sight of spirituality. This highlights the importance of having unwavering reminders from the Quran and our religious duties. This underlines why the spiritual laws of شریعۃ have been structured and enforced over these 7000 years.

title: Imamat: The Unbroken Chain Of Guidance
text: The spiritual path is built upon a continuous chain of guidance. This sacred chain begins with Maulana Ali and Maulatina Fatima and extends through the Imams from their progeny. This is not a random historical sequence; it is a core part of Allah's divine plan for humanity, which reveals itself in different cycles or periods, known as a دور . Just as the world has its own distinct seasons, human history also has its own unique spiritual seasons. Each season is led by an Imam and the appointed Prophets of his time. You can also think of it as various chapters in a book. When the chapter of Prophet Nuh and his successors concluded, Allah began a new one by sending Prophet Ibrahim (AS), initiating a new era of guidance for the world, which was approximately 1000 years after Nuh (AS). However, unlike Adam and Nuh (AS), Allah says something interesting about Ibrahim (AS). He honors him with the title of خلیل , which generally means "friend". While it is often translated as 'friend,' its meaning is much deeper. خلیل The term Khalil ( خليل ) originates from the three-letter Arabic root خلل , meaning a gap. From this root, the word الخُلَّة emerges, representing the highest level of love and friendship—one that deeply occupies the heart and soul, leaving no خَلَل (gap) for others. A خليل is thus someone whose love has so thoroughly penetrated their heart that it becomes an integral part of their identity. This is why Ibrahim (AS) is called خليل الله (the intimate friend of Allah), as his heart was entirely filled with Allah's love. So Ibrahim (AS) attained this spiritual station by his absolute devotion, unwavering submission, and profound love for Allah. This unique relationship is highlighted in the Quran as: Women (4:125) ‏ ‏وَمَنْ أَحْسَنُ دينًۭا مّمَّنْ أَسْلَمَ وَجْهَهُ للَّه وَهُوَ مُحْسنٌۭ وَٱتَّبَعَ ملَّةَ إبْرَهيمَ حَنيفًۭا وَٱتَّخَذَ ٱللَّهُ إبْرَهيمَ خَليلًۭا ‎ ‎ Translation: And who is better in religion than one who submits himself to Allah while being a doer of good and follows the religion of Abraham, inclining toward truth? And Allah took Abraham as an intimate friend. What makes this friendship truly special is that it is rooted in genuine affection, not just a gift or favoritism. Ibrahim (AS) gained his high rank through intense spiritual effort and dedication. He fulfilled every command and faced every trial Allah gave him. One of his most notable trials, demonstrating his ultimate submission, was the divine order to sacrifice his beloved son, which will be discussed later. We will see that he did not reserve any space in his heart for anyone but Allah, not even for his son whom he had received through prayer. His steadfast obedience, incredible patience, and complete trust in Allah were the keys to reaching this elevated spiritual level. In contrast, Adam (AS) failed in his obedience, and Nuh (AS) failed in his patience because he lamented to Allah and asked Him not to leave any disbelievers in the world. However, Ibrahim (AS)'s station is exceptional because, despite facing the toughest trials, he remained flawlessly submissive. This earned him the title of خلیل , which is why Allah said: Tha Star (53:37) ‏ ‏وَإبْرَهيمَ ٱلَّذى وَفَّىٰ ‎ ‎ Translation: And [of] Abraham, who fulfilled [his obligations] -

title: Kalimaat: The Divine Words
text: Allah says in the Quran: The Cow (2:124) ‏ ‏ وَإذ ٱبْتَلَىٰ إبْرَهمَ رَبُّهُ بكَلمَتٍۢ فَأَتَمَّهُنَّ ۖ قَالَ إنّى جَاعلُكَ للنَّاس إمَامًۭا ۖ قَالَ وَمن ذُرّيَّتى ۖ قَالَ لَا يَنَالُ عَهْدى ٱلظَّلمينَ ‎ ‎ Translation: And [mention, O Muhammad], when Abraham was tried by his Lord with commands and he fulfilled them. [Allah] said, "Indeed, I will make you a leader for the people." [Abraham] said, "And of my descendants?" [Allah] said, "My covenant does not include the wrongdoers." The term " کلمات " appears frequently in the Quran. For example, in the story of Adam (AS), Allah says: فَتَلَقَّىٰٓ آدَمُ مِن رَّبِّهِۦ كَلِمَتٍۢ فَتَابَ عَلَيْهِ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ هُوَ ٱلتَّوَّابُ ٱلرَّحِيمُ - "Then Adam received from his Lord Words," and His Lord forgave him. Certainly, He is the Forgiving, the Merciful. Understanding the meaning of this term is therefore crucial. What is a word? A word is made up of a sequence of letters arranged in a specific order. Preserving this order is crucial for accurately conveying the word's intended meaning. In esoterics, each letter in a "word" represents a range of concepts—such as revelations, creations, identities, or, as with Ibrahim (AS), a series of tests. Ultimately, this process reveals deeper spiritual realities or, in the case of a word, unveils its ultimate meaning. In every case, the purpose of the words ( کلمات ) is to reveal the divine will of Allah(SWT). A Creative Force: The word ' کلمۃ ' means a word. Its plural is کلمات . It is used in several places in the Quran. The most famous example is Allah's creative act described as كُن فَيَكُونُ . Ya Sîn (36:82) ‏ ‏إنَّمَا أَمْرُهُ إذَا أَرَادَ شَيْـًٔا أَن يَقُولَ لَهُ كُن فَيَكُونُ ‎ ‎ Translation: His command is only when He intends a thing that He says to it, "Be," and it is. Here, the word " كُن " ("Be") isn’t just another command expressed with letters. It’s the very essence of creation itself. It connects divine will with the physical world around us. So, a " كلمۃ " from Allah becomes a powerful act of divine energy, bringing realities into existence. When we see this idea in the context of spiritual truths, they aren’t just passive concepts—they are active, fundamental principles that form the foundation of the spiritual universe. A Living Being: Allah speaks about Isa (AS) in this way. Q|4:171 This shows that a کلمۃ can also be a living being, a perfect embodiment of Allah's will and command in the world. Expressions of an Inner Reality From our own perspective, what is a word? A word is the outward expression of an inner thought or intention that cannot be seen. Before speaking, the idea exists only in our minds. When we say the word, we give that invisible idea a form that others can perceive. Applying this metaphorically, higher spiritual realities exist in the spiritual realm, which is beyond our direct perception. A کلمۃ is the form that this divine reality takes to be understood by humans. It's the expression or manifestation of that eternal truth. For this reason, in تاویل , the Prophets and especially the Imams are considered the highest of the Kalimat. They're the living, human expressions of Allah's eternal guidance and knowledge on earth, making unseen spiritual realities known and accessible. The "Speaking Word" At the heart of our faith is a key principle. The physical Qur'an is referred to as the "Silent Book" ( کتاب الصامت ). Its letters and words are just ink on a page, unable to speak for themselves. To grasp its deepest meanings, we need the "Speaking Book" ( کتاب الناطق ). This was Maulana Ali during the time of the Prophet. Our Prophet (SWS) brought the silent Quran, which is unable to explain its hidden meanings. Therefore, he established Maulana Ali as his successor and the "Speaking Book" to explain the hidden meanings within the physical text. Similarly, every Imam of his period is also the "Speaking Word" of Allah. Quran says: The Cow (2:1-3) ‏ ‏الم ‎ ‏ذَلكَ ٱلْكتَبُ لَا رَيْبَ فيه هُدًۭى لّلْمُتَّقينَ ‎ ‏ٱلَّذينَ يُؤْمنُونَ بٱلْغَيْب وَيُقيمُونَ ٱلصَّلَوٰةَ وَممَّا رَزَقْنَهُمْ يُنفقُونَ ‎ ‎ Translation: Alif, Lam, Meem. This is the Book about which there is no doubt, a guidance for those conscious of Allah - Who believe in the unseen, establish prayer, and spend out of what We have provided for them, What is a book or a کتاب ? It's a collection of words, organized in a specific way to convey meaning. Maulana Ali is the book, whose lineage contains the "words" representing the Imams, who are sent to this world to convey spiritual guidance. That's why the verse refers to " that book " rather than "this book". This verse is not referring to the physical book in our hands, but rather pointing to another entity: the divine book of guidance, which is Maulana Ali and the Imams in his progeny. They are the living embodiment of Allah's message. They are the speaking "word" that makes the "words" of the silent scripture relevant and understandable for every age. So, when the Qur'an mentions that Ibrahim (AS) was tested by certain "Words" ( کلمات ), it refers to the eternal principles of the Imamat that he had to recognize and embody. By doing so, he proved he was worthy of having a line of living, speaking, کلمات —the Imams—descend from him. The Architect: Think of a master architect's blueprint for a magnificent skyscraper. The higher reality is the architect's complete vision—perfect, whole, but intangible. The blueprint is the کلمۃ . The "word" or "command" translates the architect's intangible vision into a tangible, understandable form. It's not the building itself, but it contains all the information and authority needed to construct it perfectly. Without the blueprint, the vision remains locked away and inaccessible. In the same way, the کلمات are the divine "blueprints" that express the will and knowledge of Allah. They are the commanding principles and, in their highest form, the living human guides who translate eternal spiritual truths into practical guidance for humanity. Therefore, "word" is the perfect term for a reality that is creative, commanding, expressive, and revelatory. Why is Ibrahim (AS) a Divine Word? Ibrahim (AS) fulfilled a series of profound promises and trials, described in the Qur'an as کلمات (divine words/commands). His perfect fulfillment of these trials demonstrated a level of submission and trust so complete that it earned him the unique and intimate title of خلیل اللہ , the intimate friend of Allah. The promise was not a single act but a lifelong covenant of absolute obedience, culminating in the most challenging test imaginable. On the outward, these Words represent the series of difficult commands and trials that Ibrahim (AS) had to undergo to prove his absolute submission to Allah. He had to leave his home and family, face persecution from his people, and finally, face the ultimate test of being commanded to sacrifice his beloved son, Ismail. By fulfilling these commands without hesitation, Ibrahim demonstrated the perfection of his submission. On an inward (باطن) level, the "Words" represent the series of divine and spiritual realities, principles, and truths that Ibrahim (AS) had to recognize, embody, and master. In esoterics, these "Words" are connected to the spiritual ranks and the eternal creative command of Allah. They were not merely instructions for action, but rather tests of profound spiritual perception and revelation. Ibrahim's (AS) test was to recognize the spiritual hierarchy of the universe and to embody the virtues represented by these Divine Words . This demonstrated his capacity not just to be a Prophet who delivers a message, but an Imam ( إمام ) who can spiritually guide humanity. An analogy for this is to think of a student being trained for a great responsibility. The outward "words" of the teacher are the assignments and tests in the curriculum. The student must complete these tasks. However, the actual inward reality of these tests is to determine if the student can truly understand and apply the deep principles the curriculum is designed to teach. Simply completing the assignments is not enough; the student must internalize the wisdom to become a master. Ibrahim's (AS) fulfillment of the "Words" was his internalization of divine truths, proving he was ready for the immense responsibility of Imamah ( إمامة ). This covenant Allah stated does not extend to the wrongdoers. The Ultimate Test of Submission His ultimate test of submission was Allah asking him to sacrifice his beloved son Ismail. Those Who Set the Ranks (37:102) ‏ ‏فَلَمَّا بَلَغَ مَعَهُ ٱلسَّعْىَ قَالَ يَبُنَىَّ إنّى أَرَىٰ فى ٱلْمَنَام أَنّى أَذْبَحُكَ فَٱنظُرْ مَاذَا تَرَىٰ قَالَ يَأَبَت ٱفْعَلْ مَا تُؤْمَرُ ۖ سَتَجدُنى إن شَاءَ ٱللَّهُ منَ ٱلصَّبرينَ ‎ ‎ Translation: And when he reached with him [the age of] exertion, he said, "O my son, indeed I have seen in a dream that I [must] sacrifice you, so see what you think." He said, "O my father, do as you are commanded. You will find me, if Allah wills, of the steadfast." This command asked him to prove that his love for and obedience to Allah surpassed all worldly attachments, even the most potent and natural love a father has for his son. By proceeding with this command without hesitation, trusting entirely in Allah's wisdom, he fulfilled his promise to hold nothing back from his Creator. Fulfilling this covenant is precisely what elevated him from the station of a prophet to the unique rank of خلیل (friend). A servant may obey out of duty, fear, or hope for reward. A friend, however, acts out of pure love, innate trust, and a deep, shared understanding. As I explained earlier, خلیل implies an intimacy where love and knowledge "permeate" the heart, leaving no room for doubt, ego, or self-interest. When Ibrahim (AS) was willing to sacrifice his son, he demonstrated that the love of Allah had permeated his entire being. His will became perfectly aligned with the Divine Will. Therefore, he fulfilled the promise of total submission, both in its outward action and its inward spiritual reality. This perfect, all-encompassing fulfillment transformed his relationship with Allah into one of sacred friendship, a bond of unparalleled love and trust. The Promise That Lives On What did Allah do in return? Allah not just made Ibrahim an Imam, but also promised that the unbroken chain of Imamat and the chain of prophethood would continue in his lineage. Quran says: The Cow (2:124) ‏ ‏ وَإذ ٱبْتَلَىٰ إبْرَهمَ رَبُّهُ بكَلمَتٍۢ فَأَتَمَّهُنَّ ۖ قَالَ إنّى جَاعلُكَ للنَّاس إمَامًۭا ۖ قَالَ وَمن ذُرّيَّتى ۖ قَالَ لَا يَنَالُ عَهْدى ٱلظَّلمينَ ‎ ‎ Translation: And [mention, O Muhammad], when Abraham was tried by his Lord with commands and he fulfilled them. [Allah] said, "Indeed, I will make you a leader for the people." [Abraham] said, "And of my descendants?" [Allah] said, "My covenant does not include the wrongdoers." Since Ibrahim (AS) passed this ultimate test, Allah made him an Imam (a spiritual leader ) for humanity and promised that this leadership, the Imamat, would continue forever in his family. By proving himself worthy, he laid the groundwork for the permanent institution of the امامۃ and نبوۃ . This divine promise ensured that humanity will always have a guide. This sacred promise is a direct line from Ibrahim (AS) to the subsequent Prophets, leading to the final Prophet, Muhammad. Then to his successor, Maulana Ali, and then from him the chain of Imamat continues unbroken to the Imam of the present day. So, Ibrahim’s (AS) story is more than just a tale from the past. It's the foundation of the living principle of continuous guidance at the heart of our Sulemani faith. It's a powerful story of a trial passed, a promise fulfilled, and a divine gift—the light of the Imamat—that shines eternally. If you examine this verse قَالَ إِنِّى جَاعِلُكَ لِلنَّاسِ إِمَامًۭا قَالَ وَمِن ذُرِّيَّتِى قَالَ لَا يَنَالُ عَهْدِى ٱلظَّلِمِينَ [Allah] said, "Indeed, I will make you an Imam for the people." [Abraham] said, "And of my descendants?" [Allah] said, "My covenant does not include the wrongdoers." Now this doesn't make sense, right? Allah says, 'I am planning to elevate you to the rank of an Imam!' He asks, "What about my lineage?" -meaning, does your promise apply to the chain of Imamat from my lineage? So how does Allah respond? My covenant does not include the wrongdoers! Why? Ismail and Ishaq (AS) are wrongdoers? Of course not! So then why did Allah say that? This divine response transcended simply naming a specific individual, such as Ismail ( اسماعيل ) or Ishaq ( اسحاق ). Instead, Allah's statement, "My covenant does not include the wrongdoers," provided a profound and universal principle that governs the spiritual leadership (Imamate). This response clarifies that while lineage is important, it is secondary to the spiritual purity and righteousness required for bearing the divine covenant. Allah's answer was not a denial of Prophet Ibrahim's (ابراهيم) descendants' potential for leadership, but a clear condition for it. It established that anyone from his progeny, whether Ismail ( اسماعيل ), Ishaq ( اسحاق ), or their descendants, who holds this sacred office will be free from wrongdoing. Because if they ever did anything wrong, Allah's word would become false. It's incredible how the language contains the depths of profound meanings. This highlights that the Imamate is a spiritual role rooted in infallible moral excellence and divine choice, ensuring that ongoing guidance—referred to as the "living word" and the "Rope of Allah" (حبل الله)—remains pure and uncontaminated for humanity. This is why Allah responded to Ibrahim's ( ابراهيم ) question by revealing the essential requirement (of عصمۃ ) that everyone who inherits this high spiritual duty will possess - Infallibility.

title: The Soul's Journey To The Spiritual World
text: Certain chosen guides of Allah, such as Prophets, are granted the special ability to witness the spiritual world, known as the مَلَكُوت . This is not the physical world perceived by the senses, but a higher, unseen reality that is the origin of all existence. An analogy for this is to think of the physical world as a shadow and the مَلَكُوت as the real object casting that shadow. Ibrahim (AS) was one such Prophet who was shown this spiritual dominion over the heavens and the earth, allowing him to perceive a deeper layer of reality. This profound spiritual experience was not unique to him. Other Prophets, such as Idris, Isa, and our Prophet, Muhammad (SWS), also experienced a spiritual ascent where their souls were elevated to a high station. For Idris (AS), Allah says: ‏وَرَفَعْنَهُ مَكَانًا عَلِيًّا - And we raised him to a high station. Similarly, Isa (AS) was also raised to Allah’s presence. ‏بَل رَّفَعَهُ ٱللَّهُ إِلَيْهِ ۚ وَكَانَ ٱللَّهُ عَزِيزًا حَكِيمًۭا ‏ - But Allah raised him up unto Himself. And Allah is Ever All­Powerful, All­Wise. The most renowned of these spiritual journeys belongs to our Prophet SWS, called the Night Journey, the إِسْرَاء . The Children of Israel (17:1) ‏ ‏سُبْحَنَ ٱلَّذى أَسْرَىٰ بعَبْده لَيْلًۭا مّنَ ٱلْمَسْجد ٱلْحَرَام إلَى ٱلْمَسْجد ٱلْأَقْصَا ٱلَّذى بَرَكْنَا حَوْلَهُ لنُريَهُ منْ آيَتنَا إنَّهُ هُوَ ٱلسَّميعُ ٱلْبَصيرُ ‎ ‎ Translation: Exalted is He who took His Servant by night from al-Masjid al-Haram to al-Masjid al-Aqsa, whose surroundings We have blessed, to show him of Our signs. Indeed, He is the Hearing, the Seeing. When we talk about journeys in our tradition, we're not just thinking about getting on a plane or a car. It's more about an inner, spiritual trip. Your body remains stationary, much like when we're sleeping, but our soul – that light, subtle part of us – is out there exploring and connecting with the spiritual world. It’s kind of like dreaming, right? Our body may be still, but in our dreams, our mind can be anywhere, seeing all sorts of things. The Prophet’s journey, however, was far beyond a typical dream; it was a profoundly spiritual experience where his spirit ascended to the highest heavens and witnessed incredible signs from Allah, even though his physical body remained still. Here's the core idea for us: the real "you" isn't your body. Your body is just like a temporary car that your soul drives around in while you're here on Earth. Your soul is the actual driver, the one experiencing everything. Now, Prophets and Imams are so spiritually pure, they can see things and understand realities that are hidden from most of us. Their spiritual journeys give us a peek into that ultimate reality, which is precisely where every believer's soul is trying to go. The Imam of the Time is like a living bridge to that spiritual world, a direct link to the invisible realm ( مَلَكُوت ). Through his guidance ( تَعْلِيم ), he helps our souls ascend. It's as if he's holding a map, guiding us to understand the true origins of our soul ( أَصْل ) and its ultimate destination. Just as the Prophet's soul took an incredible journey in his ascension ( مِعْرَاج ), our own souls can begin their spiritual climb. How? By focusing on the Imam of the Time and seeking that deep, inner knowledge and recognition ( مَعْرِفَة ) that he provides. As we're learning right now, our souls are connecting with the spiritual realm. The veils are being lifted before our eyes, enabling us to see beyond the superficial. The Experience of Receiving Divine Guidance Receiving divine revelation, or وَحْي , is a compelling spiritual experience. For Rasul Allah (SWS), this divine communication would often begin with true visions during his sleep. These dreams were a form of the divine message he was to receive. When the revelation came to him while he was awake, he experienced immense gravity, described in the Holy Quran as a "weighty word." He felt like he was being pulled into the earth. The Enshrouded One (73:5) ‏ ‏إنَّا سَنُلْقى عَلَيْكَ قَوْلًۭا ثَقيلًا ‎ ‎ Translation: Indeed, We will cast upon you a heavy word. This experience was so intense that it would overwhelm his physical being. He would enter a deep, trance-like state and perspire profusely, even on the coldest of days. This demonstrates that the connection between the spiritual world of revelation and the physical world is so potent that it produces tangible, physical effects. Imagine trying to pour the entire ocean into a small cup; the cup would inevitably overflow. In the same way, the Prophet’s physical body was the vessel receiving a vast and powerful message from the spiritual ocean of Divine Knowledge, and the intensity of this influx caused his body to react very strongly. Some people struggle to comprehend experiences that are far beyond ordinary human understanding. When faced with something completely unfamiliar, a common human reaction is fear or disbelief, leading them to accuse the person of being possessed or unwell. This response highlights the challenges individuals face in grasping a direct manifestation of a higher reality. In our tradition, while revelation ( وحي ), in the form of a new religious law or scripture, concluded with Prophet Muhammad (SWS) as the final Prophet, the need for divine guidance for humanity continues. The Imam of the Time continuously receives divine inspiration and support, known as تأييد , to guide the community. This guidance is the "living word" for the present time, serving as the Rope of Allah ( حبل الله ) that believers are encouraged to hold onto. Just as the Prophet experienced the "weighty word," the Imam of the Time bears the spiritual authority and responsibility of guiding his spiritual children. His guidance illuminates the inner, spiritual meaning ( باطن ) of the Quran and the faith, making these profound realities relevant and accessible in every time and place. This continuous guidance is the Proof ( حجة ) of Allah's ever-present mercy and engagement with His creation in every age.

title: An Imam In Training
text: The first story about Ibrahim (AS) in the Quran describes his journey to recognize Allah. It is a powerful narrative in the Quran that highlights the use of reason, observation, and innate spiritual disposition ( فِطْرَة ) to arrive at the truth of تَوْحِيد . It offers us a beautiful lesson in understanding Allah's oneness ( تَوْحِيد ) by simply looking at the world around us and using our minds. Ibrahim (AS) grew up in a place where people worshipped statues, and even his own father, Azar, was a famous statue maker. But even as a young boy, Ibrahim (AS) knew in his heart that it didn't make sense to pray to things made by humans that couldn't help or hurt anyone. This strong feeling led him to challenge his father, saying: Cattle (6:74) ‏ ‏ وَإذْ قَالَ إبْرَهيمُ لأَبيه آزَرَ أَتَتَّخذُ أَصْنَامًا آلهَةً ۖ إنّى أَرَىٰكَ وَقَوْمَكَ فى ضَلَلٍۢ مُّبينٍۢ ‎ ‎ Translation: And [mention, O Muhammad], when Abraham said to his father Azar, "Do you take idols as deities? Indeed, I see you and your people to be in manifest error." Seeking the True Allah Because he was unhappy with what his people believed, Ibrahim (AS) set out to find the one true Allah who truly deserved to be worshipped. Allah guided him on this search by showing him how to use logic, starting with the stars and planets that his people already worshipped. This journey of discovery occurred over just one night and day, revealing to Ibrahim (AS) the immense expanse of the heavens and the earth, which further strengthened his faith. The Star, the Moon, and the Sun Cattle (6:76) ‏ ‏فَلَمَّا جَنَّ عَلَيْه ٱلَّيْلُ رَآ كَوْكَبًۭا ۖ قَالَ هَذَا رَبّى ۖ فَلَمَّا أَفَلَ قَالَ لَآ أُحبُّ ٱلْآفلينَ ‎ ‎ Translation: So when the night covered him [with darkness], he saw a star. He said, "This is my lord." But when it set, he said, "I like not those that disappear." As night approached, Ibrahim (AS) saw a star. He pondered whether it could be his lord, but when it disappeared, he realized that a true deity couldn't just vanish. He said, "I like not those that disappear." Cattle (6:77) ‏ ‏فَلَمَّا رَآ ٱلْقَمَرَ بَازغًۭا قَالَ هَذَا رَبّى ۖ فَلَمَّا أَفَلَ قَالَ لَىٕن لَّمْ يَهْدنى رَبّى لَأَكُونَنَّ منَ ٱلْقَوْم ٱلضَّالّينَ ‎ ‎ Translation: And when he saw the moon rising, he said, "This is my lord." But when it set, he said, "Unless my Lord guides me, I will surely be among the people gone astray." Next, he watched the moon rise, larger and brighter than the star. He again wondered if it was his true Master. When the moon set, he realized he needed Allah's guidance, understanding that his own reasoning might not suffice without help from the Creator he sought. Cattle (6:78) ‏ ‏فَلَمَّا رَآ ٱلشَّمْسَ بَازغَةًۭ قَالَ هَذَا رَبّى هَذَا أَكْبَرُ ۖ فَلَمَّا أَفَلَتْ قَالَ يَقَوْم إنّى بَرىءٌۭ مّمَّا تُشْركُونَ ‎ ‎ Translation: And when he saw the sun rising, he said, "This is my lord; this is greater." But when it set, he said, "O my people, indeed I am free from what you associate with Allah. As dawn arrived, he saw the sun shining brightly and thought, "This is my Master; this is the greatest." However, when the sun also set, he told his people that he had no part in the objects they worshipped alongside Allah. This moment led him to completely reject all forms of polytheism ( شِرْك ), recognizing that anything that changes or disappears cannot be the ultimate Creator. The Conclusion of Monotheism By carefully and logically ruling out all created things as possible gods, Ibrahim (AS) arrived at the pure and simple truth of Allah's oneness ( تَوْحِيد ). He turned his entire being towards the one and only Creator, who is eternal and absolute. Then, he made his famous and influential declaration, which we still say in our daily prayers today: Cattle (6:79) ‏ ‏إنّى وَجَّهْتُ وَجْهىَ للَّذى فَطَرَ ٱلسَّمَوَت وَٱلْأَرْضَ حَنيفًۭا ۖ وَمَا أَنَا۠ منَ ٱلْمُشْركينَ ‎ ‎ Translation: Indeed, I have turned my face toward He who created the heavens and the earth, inclining toward truth, and I am not of those who associate others with Allah." This story beautifully teaches us that the way to recognize Allah is actually found within everything around us. For anyone with a sincere heart and a clear mind, the signs of Allah are everywhere, all pointing to one single, eternal, and all-powerful Creator. Ibrahim (AS)'s journey is a timeless example of how to use observation and logic to confirm the most basic truth of Islam: there is no god but Allah ( لَا إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا ٱللَّٰهُ ). ESOTERICS In reality, this story of Ibrahim (AS) in the Quran is not just a simple tale of him seeing stars for the first time. This story illustrates how a believer, guided by the spiritual leaders of our faith ( حُدُودُ الدِّين ), can deepen their understanding and draw closer to the ultimate truth. Ibrahim (AS)'s journey begins in "the night," which represents a state where we may not be spiritually aware and divine truths are unclear. In this darkness, we, like Ibrahim (AS), need a light —a guide to show us the way. This movement from darkness to light is similar to climbing a ladder, where each step upward means gaining more spiritual insight from a special guide. the Star (The دَاعِي ) The first light Ibrahim (AS) sees is a "star." Cattle (6:76) ‏ ‏فَلَمَّا جَنَّ عَلَيْه ٱلَّيْلُ رَآ كَوْكَبًۭا ۖ قَالَ هَذَا رَبّى ۖ فَلَمَّا أَفَلَ قَالَ لَآ أُحبُّ ٱلْآفلينَ ‎ ‎ Translation: So when the night covered him [with darkness], he saw a star. He said, "This is my lord." But when it set, he said, "I like not those that disappear." This star represents the دَاعِي , who is like a missionary or a caller who invites people to our faith. For someone new to the faith, the دَاعِي is the first source of spiritual light and knowledge. Their guidance is so powerful that the new believer might exclaim, "This is my master." Here, the word رَبّ doesn't mean Allah. Instead, it means "my master," "my guide," or "the one who nurtures me." When the "star" sets, it doesn't mean it failed. It, in fact, shows its success! It indicates that the دَاعِي has completed their role, which is to lead the seeker to a higher source of knowledge. A great teacher's success is preparing their student for the next, more advanced level of learning. The Moon (The حُجَّة ) After the star, Ibrahim (AS) sees the "moon." Cattle (6:77) ‏ ‏فَلَمَّا رَآ ٱلْقَمَرَ بَازغًۭا قَالَ هَذَا رَبّى ۖ فَلَمَّا أَفَلَ قَالَ لَىٕن لَّمْ يَهْدنى رَبّى لَأَكُونَنَّ منَ ٱلْقَوْم ٱلضَّالّينَ ‎ ‎ Translation: And when he saw the moon rising, he said, "This is my lord." But when it set, he said, "Unless my Lord guides me, I will surely be among the people gone astray." The moon, being brighter than the star, symbolizes the حُجَّة , or the "Proof." The حُجَّة holds a higher rank in our spiritual leadership and has a deeper level of knowledge. Ibrahim (AS) is once again filled with wonder at this new source of spiritual light. However, the moon also "sets," showing that the حُجَّة , too, is a guide who points to yet another, even higher source. At this point, Ibrahim (AS) has an important realization: without guidance from the very top of this spiritual leadership, he would surely be lost. لَئِن لَّمْ يَهْدِنِي رَبِّي لَأَكُونَنَّ مِنَ الْقَوْمِ الضَّالِّينَ Unless my Imam guides me, I will surely be among the misguided people. The Sun (The Imam of the Time) Finally, after imparting his knowledge, the حُجَّة directs the seeker to the "sun." Cattle (6:78) ‏ ‏فَلَمَّا رَآ ٱلشَّمْسَ بَازغَةًۭ قَالَ هَذَا رَبّى هَذَا أَكْبَرُ ۖ فَلَمَّا أَفَلَتْ قَالَ يَقَوْم إنّى بَرىءٌۭ مّمَّا تُشْركُونَ ‎ ‎ Translation: And when he saw the sun rising, he said, "This is my lord; this is greater." But when it set, he said, "O my people, indeed I am free from what you associate with Allah. The sun, which illuminates our entire world and makes all other lights seem dim, represents the Imam of that Time, whose name was Saleh (AS) . As the highest spiritual authority on earth, the Imam is the living, clear source of divine guidance and knowledge. When Ibrahim (AS) recognizes this supreme rank, he declares that this guide is "greater" ( هَٰذَا أَكْبَرُ ) than all who came before. From the Creation to the Creator The ultimate spiritual lesson unfolds when the sun also "sets." قَالَ يَقَوْمِ إِنِّى بَرِىٓءٌۭ مِّمَّا تُشْرِكُونَ - My people, indeed I am free from what you associate with Allah. What does this mean? Let's break down this spiritual lesson using an analogy. Let's think about the sun in the story of Ibrahim (AS). When the sun "sets," it doesn't mean it's gone forever or that it failed. Instead, it signifies that its job is complete for that part of the journey. In our spiritual path, the Imam of the Time, who is likened to the sun, plays a crucial role: to guide us through all the different levels of creation and spiritual ranks, so that we can truly recognize Allah, the ultimate Creator. Think of it like this: Imagine a ship at sea trying to find its way to port. It sees different lights on its journey. The Star ( دَاعِي ): At first, the ship might see a small light, perhaps from a distant buoy or another boat. This light helps it realize it's not entirely lost in the dark ocean. This is like the دَاعِي , who gives us our first spiritual light and helps us understand the initial concepts of faith. They are essential, but they aren't the final destination. The Moon ( حُجَّة ): As the ship gets closer to shore, it might see a brighter, steadier light, like a small lighthouse. This light gives clearer direction and more detailed guidance. This is like the حُجَّة , who provides a deeper level of knowledge and guides us further along the path. The Sun (Imam of the Time): Finally, as the ship approaches the main harbor, it sees the biggest, most powerful light – the main lighthouse at the entrance of the port. This is the most precise and most comprehensive guide, showing the direct way in. This is like the Imam of the Time, who is the living, perfect manifestation ( مَظْهَر ) of divine light and knowledge on earth. He shines the brightest light on our path. Now, what does it mean when the "sun" (the Imam) also "sets"? It doesn't mean the Imam disappears or is no longer needed. Instead, it means that the Imam has fulfilled his ultimate purpose: to guide us to the source of all light, which is Allah Himself. The Imam, despite being the highest spiritual authority, never calls us to worship him . He always points us towards Allah. It's as if the lighthouse's job isn't to prevent ships from crashing into it, but to guide them safely into the harbor. Maulana Ali (AS) beautifully expressed this when he said, أَنَا عَبْدٌ مِنْ عَبِيدِ اللَّهِ ("I am a slave of Allah from among his slaves"). Even the highest created being is a servant of Allah, demonstrating that Allah is the ultimate goal. After this spiritual journey, Ibrahim (AS) attained a profound understanding. He didn't reject the star, moon, or sun as useless. Instead, he understood their real purpose: they were all guides, intermediaries leading him step-by-step to the one and only source of all light and existence – Allah. He then declared with absolute conviction, Cattle (6:79) ‏ ‏إنّى وَجَّهْتُ وَجْهىَ للَّذى فَطَرَ ٱلسَّمَوَت وَٱلْأَرْضَ حَنيفًۭا ۖ وَمَا أَنَا۠ منَ ٱلْمُشْركينَ ‎ ‎ Translation: Indeed, I have turned my face toward He who created the heavens and the earth, inclining toward truth, and I am not of those who associate others with Allah." This entire experience shows how Allah fulfilled His promise to reveal to Ibrahim (AS) the spiritual dominion ( مَلَكُوت ) of reality. Cattle (6:75) ‏ ‏وَكَذَلكَ نُرى إبْرَهيمَ مَلَكُوتَ ٱلسَّمَوَت وَٱلْأَرْض وَليَكُونَ منَ ٱلْمُوقنينَ ‎ ‎ Translation: And thus did We show Abraham the realm of the heavens and the earth that he would be among the certain [in faith] By successfully navigating these earthly levels of guidance ( مَلَكُوت الْأَرْض ), Ibrahim (AS) became worthy of understanding the celestial truths ( مَلَكُوت السَّمَاوَات ) and reached a state of absolute, unwavering faith.

title: The Divine Appointment Of The Imam
text: At this point, after completing his spiritual tests, which were essentially steps in his soul's ascent toward the spiritual realms, Ibrahim (AS) was lifted to a special spiritual position by Allah. Allah appointed him as an Imam. Who is Allah here? His own father, Imam Saleh (AS) . The Quran tells us about this special appointment: The Cow (2:124) ‏ ‏ وَإذ ٱبْتَلَىٰ إبْرَهمَ رَبُّهُ بكَلمَتٍۢ فَأَتَمَّهُنَّ ۖ قَالَ إنّى جَاعلُكَ للنَّاس إمَامًۭا ۖ قَالَ وَمن ذُرّيَّتى ۖ قَالَ لَا يَنَالُ عَهْدى ٱلظَّلمينَ ‎ ‎ Translation: And [mention, O Muhammad], when Abraham was tried by his Lord with commands and he fulfilled them. [Allah] said, "Indeed, I will make you a leader for the people." [Abraham] said, "And of my descendants?" [Allah] said, "My covenant does not include the wrongdoers." What are these كَلِمَتٍۢ ? What are these strings of Alphabets? The star, the moon, and the sun. Only after successfully navigating these earthly levels of guidance ( مَلَكُوت الْأَرْض ), Ibrahim (AS) became worthy of understanding the celestial truths ( مَلَكُوت السَّمَاوَات ) and reached a state of absolute, unwavering faith. This is when he is appointed the next Imam by his father, Imam Saleh (AS) - قَالَ إِنِّى جَاعِلُكَ لِلنَّاسِ إِمَامًۭا [Allah] said, "Indeed, I will make you a leader for the people." قَالَ لَا يَنَالُ عَهْدِى ٱلظَّلِمِينَ Ibrahim, now Imam Ibrahim (AS), asks, قَالَ وَمِن ذُرِّيَّتِى. What about my progeny? Allah says قَالَ لَا يَنَالُ عَهْدِى ٱلظَّلِمِينَ My covenant does not include the wrongdoers. This verse reveals a couple of things: First, it shows that the Imamate isn't just a position in this world; it's a divine promise. That's why the word عَهْد is used. This is a promise that was made in a world of pure light, before anything else was created. It's a very high rank given to an ethereal soul that is pure from the very beginning.
