So he, peace be upon him (meaning Ja'far al-Sadiq), said: "Indeed, we speak of one word in seven aspects." فَقَالَ الرَّجُلُ مُتَفَكِّرًا: سَبْعَةً يَا ابْنَ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ؟ So the man said, thoughtfully: "Seven, O son of the Messenger of Allah (SWT)?" فَقَالَ: « نَعَمْ وَسَبْعِينَ، وَلَوِ اسْتَزَادَنَا لَزِدْنَاهُ ». He said: "Yes, and seventy. And if they had asked us for more, we would have given more." فَوُجُوهُ هَذَا الْعِلْمِ بِمِقْدَارِ حُدُودِهِ، فَيَعْلَمُ ذَلِكَ مَنْ سَمِعَهُ وَانْتَفَعَ بِهِ وَرَقَا فِي دَرَجَاتِهِ. So the aspects of this knowledge are according to the measure of its limits, and that is known by whoever hears it and benefits from it and ascends in its levels. وَهَذَا كِتَابٌ قَدْ بَسَطْنَاهُ فِي أُصُولِهِ وَهُوَ أَوَّلُ حَدٍّ مِنْ حُدُودِهِ سَمَّيْنَاهُ كِتَابَ "أَسَاسِ التَّأْوِيلِ"، وَقَصَدْنَا بِهِ شَرْحَ مَا أَثْبَتْنَاهُ فِي كِتَابِ "دَعَائِمِ الْإِسْلَامِ"؛ لِيَكُونَ هَذَا أَصْلًا لِلْبَاطِنِ، كَمَا كَانَ ذَلِكَ أَصْلًا لِلظَّاهِرِ، وَبِاللَّهِ نَسْتَعِينُ وَلَا حَوْلَ وَلَا قُوَّةَ إِلَّا بِاللَّهِ الْعَظِيمِ. This book presents its principles and outlines its foundational limitations. It is titled "Foundation of Interpretation" and aims to clarify the concepts found in "Pillars of Islam." Thus, it serves as a groundwork for understanding deeper meanings, similar to how the latter work establishes a basis for the visible. We turn to Allah (SWT) for assistance, as there is no strength or power except through Allah (SWT), the Great.

title: Syedna Moyyed's explation of Hadees
text: Body Our body is made entirely from this earth It is made from the very same elements that this earth is made from Fire Water Air Earth The dimensions of the body and the four elements within the body, are negligible in comparison to the dimensions of the earth Body exists in 3 dimensions and is surrounded by 6 sides Soul In addition to the body we also have a soul which is energy Soul is an extremely powerful energy Like the body, it must also have a source Like the body, the it must also be a minute fraction of its source (which is the spiritual world or heavens) Just as the body returns to its source upon death, the soul must also return to its original source (heavens) Soul & Body The body is in complete submission, obedience and servitude to the soul. Worship ( عبادۃ ) is a combination of two things Obedience Free will (love and trust) The relationship between the soul and body, manifests the very purpose of our creation in this world وَمَا خَلَقْتُ ٱلْجِنَّ وَٱلْإِنسَ إِلَّا لِيَعْبُدُونِ And I did not create the jinn and human beings, except for the purpose of my worship.

title: Understanding Allah
text: By the negation and the destruction of these self created idols, emerges the understanding of Allah. Allah is everything other than what we take for our الہ Our creedal formula of لا الہ الا اللہ is the idea of negating the multiplicity of our self-created الہ to affirm the one true الہ which is Allah. It is the exercise and the practive of making الہ into ال الہ . This means that now: لا الہ الا اللہ now means لا یعلی الا اللہ , that Allah is the only one I worship and obey. He is going to be the only one that I will be completely devoted and loyal to in my life لا الہ الا اللہ means لا أَلَہَ الا اللہ , Allah is the only one I turn to in utter desperation for all of my needs and comforts. لا الہ الا اللہ means لا وَلَہَ الا اللہ , Allah is the one I am passionately in love with. لا الہ الا اللہ means لا أَلِہَ الا اللہ , Allah is now the only one I lean on for support in every way shape or form لا الہ الا اللہ means لا وَلِہَ الا اللہ , Allah is the only one that astounds and impresses me. The concept of توحید is not that Allah is the ultimate deity in a hierarchy of deities. NO! The concept of توحید is that there is no other deity. The 5 صواۃ has the concept breaking idols within its very nature.

title: Meaning Of Rab
text: The root for the word رب is actually رَبَبٌ . The word رب is a composite word, just like the word الہ It can be used for Allah, as well as human beings and other things.

title: MALIK (Owner)
text: The first meaning is مالک , which means owner. The property is called ملکیۃ Ultimate and absolute ownership belongs only to Allah.

title: The Divine Pattern Of Fours
text: Allah's creation is filled with signs and a perfect order. One of the most essential patterns found throughout the universe is the number four. This pattern is not a coincidence; it is a sign of Divine Wisdom, designed to help humanity understand the connection between the physical world we see and the unseen spiritual world. When we examine the physical world, we find that many of its fundamental components are grouped in fours. The Four Natures: The basic qualities of all physical things are heat, cold, moisture, and dryness. The Four Elements: From these natures come the four elements that make up the world: fire (hot and dry), air (hot and moist), water (cold and moist), and earth (cold and dry). The Four Humors: In the human body, these elements were believed to produce four vital fluids, known as humors : blood, phlegm, yellow bile, and black bile. A healthy person had these four humors in perfect balance. The Four Seasons: The year is divided into four seasons—spring, summer, autumn, and winter—each with its own unique characteristics that correspond to the four natures. The Four Cardinal Directions: The world is mapped by four main directions: east, west, south, and north. This pattern extends from the Earth to the heavens. In the science of the stars, the twelve zodiac signs have four main points of power called أوتاد الافلاک (pegs). These are the most influential positions and are referred to as the Towers of Happiness ( بُرْجَ السَّعَادَةِ ) and the origin of existence ( وَأَصْلَ الكَائِنِ ). These four أوتاد create a stable foundation for the heavens, just as the four elements (fire, air, water, earth) create a foundation for the physical world. Finally, the created world itself is seen as having four levels: Minerals at the bottom, Then plants, Then animals, and finally, Human beings are at the top. This illustrates a progression of life, with each level being more complex than the previous one. ESOTERICS A Mirror of the Spiritual World The reason for this consistent pattern of four in nature is profound. The physical world ( عالم الجسم ) is created to be a reflection, or a mirror, of the higher spiritual world ( عالم الدین ). This concept of correspondence ( مطابقۃ ) teaches that by studying the signs in the physical creation, we can begin to understand the reality of the spiritual realm. Just as the natural world is built upon a fourfold foundation, the spiritual world that exists above it also has four main ranks. These spiritual ranks are not physical bodies and exist outside of time and space. They are: 1. The Creator ( البَارِئ ): At the highest level is Allah, the Originator of all things. Allah is beyond all description and comparison. As the Holy Quran states, "There is nothing like unto Him." Counsel [42:11] [ سورۃ ۴۲] الشوری ‏فَاطِرُ ٱلسَّمَوَتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ ۚ جَعَلَ لَكُم مِّنْ أَنفُسِكُمْ أَزْوَجًۭا وَمِنَ ٱلْأَنْعَمِ أَزْوَجًۭا ۖ يَذْرَؤُكُمْ فِيهِ ۚ لَيْسَ كَمِثْلِهِۦ شَىْءٌۭ ۖ وَهُوَ ٱلسَّمِيعُ ٱلْبَصِيرُ ‎ ۱۱ The Creator of the heavens and the earth. He has made for you mates from yourselves, and for the cattle mates. By this means He creates you. There is nothing like unto Him, and He is the All-Hearer, the All-Seer. 2. The Universal Intellect ( العَقْلُ الکُلیۃ ): The first created being to emanate from the Command of Allah ( أمر اللہ ). The Universal Intellect is a perfect, spiritual light that contains all knowledge. It is often referred to as the "Pen" ( القَلَم ) mentioned in the Quran, which writes down the destiny of all creation. 3. The Universal Soul ( النَفْسُ الکُلیۃ ): Emanating from the Intellect, the Universal Soul desires to attain the perfection of the Intellect. In its effort to do so, it gives motion and form to the physical universe. One can think of the Intellect as a wise teacher and the Soul as a devoted student who, in learning from the teacher, brings the physical world to life. He is referred to as the Tablet ( لوح ) 4. Prime Matter ( الهَيُولَى الأُولَى ): This is not a physical substance but the potential for a physical world to exist. It is the spiritual "clay" from which the Universal Soul shapes the universe of minerals, plants, and animals. The Purpose of the Divine Pattern The fourfold pattern that connects the spiritual and physical worlds is a sign of Allah’s immense wisdom. It enables humanity to transition from knowledge of the visible to knowledge of the invisible. By observing the order in the seasons, the elements, and even our own bodies, we are reminded of the higher spiritual order from which we came and to which we will return. This pattern of four is also reflected in the system of religious guidance on Earth. The ranks of spiritual guidance often appear in fours: Natiq, Asas, Imam, Hujjah . The Imam of the Time is the living guide who helps his followers understand these inner meanings ( batin ) hidden within the outer world ( zahir ). He is the one who can interpret the "book of nature" and show how its fourfold patterns point toward the ultimate reality of Allah. The Creator ( الْبَارِي ) and the Number One: The starting point of all existence is Allah, who is analogous to the number One ( الْوَاحِد ). The number one is not technically a number in the same way others are; rather, it is the principle of all numbers. It generates all other numbers but is itself indivisible, unchangeable, and whole. Imagine you have a single brick. Is that single brick a wall? No. It is the idea or the unit of a brick. A wall, on the other hand, is a collection of bricks. You need at least two bricks to start making a pattern, to create a "collection" that can become a wall. In this philosophical view: The Number One is like that single brick . It is the unit , the fundamental building block. It represents the concept of singularity and wholeness. All Other Numbers (2, 3, 4...) are like the wall . They are collections or multiples of that first unit. The number two is the first actual collection (a group of two "ones"). The number five is a collection of five "ones." Therefore, while in everyday math we call '1' a number, these philosophers saw it as something more fundamental: the source from which all the collections (the other numbers) are made. It's not a collection itself; it's the thing you collect. This perspective is what gives the number one its unique properties in this analogy: "It generates all other numbers." How do you get the number two? You start with one and repeat it, or add another one to it (1 + 1). How do you get three? You take the collection of two and add one more. Every number on the number line is generated by the successive addition of the unit, "one." It is the seed from which the entire tree of numbers grows. "It is itself indivisible" : In this context, indivisible means that the concept of a single, whole unit cannot be broken down into other whole units. A brick is just a brick. You can physically break it, but then it's not a "whole brick" anymore. The concept of "one" is the absolute minimum for counting. You cannot have "half" of a fundamental counting unit. It is the starting point, complete in itself. "It is unchangeable and whole" : Think of a measuring stick. The "one inch" mark is the standard. When you measure something that is five inches long, you are just applying that unchangeable standard of "one inch" five times. The original "one inch" on the ruler remains unchanged, unallocated, and unutilized. It remains a whole and constant principle. In the same way, the number one acts as the constant, unchanging principle that gives rise to all other numbers without ever changing its own nature. Similarly, Allah is the ultimate cause and source of all creation, yet remains utterly transcendent, unchanging, and beyond comparison. He is the Necessary Being from whom all contingent existence flows. The Universal Intellect ( الْعَقْل ) and the Number Two: The first act of creation, an emanation from the "light of Oneness," is the Universal Intellect ( الْعَقْلُ الْفَعَّالُ or الْعَقْلُ الْكُلِّيُّ ). This corresponds to the number Two (الِاثْنَانِ). The Intellect is the first created being, a simple, perfect, and eternal spiritual substance. It contains the forms of all things that will ever exist, much like a blueprint. Just as two is the first real number that emerges from the repetition of one, the Intellect is the first emanation from the Creator's singular command (Amr). It is often identified with the Quranic concept of the Pen (الْقَلَمُ). The Universal Soul ( النَّفْس ) and the Number Three: Emanating from the light of the Intellect is the Universal Soul (النَّفْسُ الْكُلِّيَّةُ), which is analogous to the number Three (الثَّلَاثَة). The Soul receives the universal forms from the Intellect and desires to manifest them in the physical world. It is the principle of life, motion, and particularization. Its yearning for the perfection of the Intellect is what initiates the movement that creates the physical universe. The Soul is often associated with the Guarded Tablet (اللَّوْحُ الْمَحْفُوظُ), upon which the Pen writes the destiny of all things. Prime Matter ( الْهَيُولَى ) and the Number Four: The motion of the Soul brings forth Prime Matter ( الْهَيُولَى الْأُولَى ), analogous to the number Four (الْأَرْبَعَة). This is not matter as we see it, but it's pure, undifferentiated potential. This underlying substratum receives the forms projected by the Soul to become the physical world of stars, planets, and elements.

title: The Blueprint Of Reality: Intellect, Form, And Divine Oneness
text: In this philosophy, a "form" (صورۃ or مثال) is not a physical shape. It is the perfect, eternal, and unchanging blueprint or essence of a thing. Analogy of the Chair: You have seen thousands of different chairs in your life—wooden chairs, metal chairs, soft chairs, hard chairs. They are all different, imperfect, and temporary. However, they all share in the one, perfect idea of "Chair-ness." This idea includes the essence of what a chair is: something with a seat, support, and the purpose of being sat upon. This perfect, unchanging idea—the blueprint for all chairs—is what philosophers call the "Form of the Chair." The physical chairs we see are just temporary, imperfect copies or shadows of that one perfect Form. How the Intellect "Contains" These Forms The Universal Intellect is understood as a pure, spiritual intelligence. It doesn't "contain" these forms like a box contains items. Rather, its very existence is to perfectly and simultaneously know all of these perfect forms. Analogy of the Architect's Mind: Imagine a master architect who is about to design an entire city. Before any land is cleared or any bricks are laid, the complete city exists as a perfect idea in her mind. She has the blueprint for every building, the design of every park, and the layout of every street, all held together as one unified vision. The entire city, in its perfect form, is "contained" within her mind as a complete thought. The Universal Intellect ( عقل کل ) is like this architect's mind, but on a cosmic scale. It contains the perfect "Forms" or "blueprints" for everything in creation—not just chairs, but also trees, stars, animals, mountains, and even abstract concepts like Justice, Beauty, and Goodness. All these Forms exist within the Intellect, not as separate items, but as part of a single, unified, and eternal act of knowing. The Pen and the Blueprint This is why the Universal Intellect is often identified with the Quranic symbol of the Pen ( القلم ). The Pen [68:1] [ سورۃ ۶۸] القلم ‏نٓ ۚ وَٱلْقَلَمِ وَمَا يَسْطُرُونَ ‎ ۱ Nun . By the pen and what the write. The function of a pen is to write or draw out a plan. The Universal Intellect, as the Cosmic Pen, holds the perfect plan (ink) for all of creation. It "writes" or inscribes these eternal forms onto the Universal Soul , which is symbolized by the Guarded Tablet ( اللوح المحفوظ ). This act of "writing" is how the perfect, unified ideas within the Intellect are transmitted to the next level of being, which then projects them into the physical world. The Soul, receiving these perfect blueprints, then projects them into the physical world, giving rise to the temporary and imperfect copies that we experience with our senses. So, when we say: "the Intellect contains the forms of all things," it means that the first level of creation is a perfect, spiritual realm of pure ideas or blueprints, and this realm is the Universal Intellect ( عقل کل ) . Everything that exists in our physical world is just a reflection of a perfect Form that exists eternally within that higher Intellect. The Emanation of Multiplicity from Unity Just as all numbers from five to ten (and beyond) are simply combinations of the first four numbers (1+4= 5 , 2+4= 6 , 2+4+1= 7 , etc.), the entire diversity of the created world is a composition stemming from these four foundational principles. This elegant analogy explains how a universe of immense complexity and multiplicity can arise from a source of absolute Unity without that source being diminished or changed. When writing words, a few basic strokes combine to form the entire alphabet, which in turn forms all language and knowledge. The Unchanging Creator The most critical theological conclusion of this analogy is the absolute transcendence of Allah. While Allah is the Creator and Sustainer of all things—and their existence depends entirely on Him—He does not change, become part of creation, or get divided, as the text states, "He did not change from the Oneness He was upon before His creation and origination of them." This is a direct refutation of any belief that would compromise Allah's absolute unity. Allah is beyond even Being and Non-Being, and the language of emanation is a way for the human mind to grasp a process that is ultimately beyond its full comprehension. The analogy of the number One is perfect for this: One is the source of all numbers, but adding, subtracting, or multiplying numbers does not alter the essence or value of One itself. Likewise, the universe emanates from Allah's power, but Allah remains eternally unchanged and unparalleled in His absolute Oneness.

title: Decimal Vs Pythagorean System
text: Decimal System: Outward The text first establishes the system familiar to most people, which is built upon four fundamental ranks: آحَادٌ (Units): The foundation of all numbers (1, 2, 3...). عَشَرَاتٌ (Tens): The first order of multiplication (10, 20, 30...). مِئَاتٌ (Hundreds): The second order (100, 200...). أُلُوفٌ (Thousands): The third order (1000, 2000...). This four-part structure is the bedrock of everyday commerce, measurement, and science. It is practical, tangible, and sufficient for nearly all affairs in the physical world ( الظَّاهِرُ ). Its focus is on counting and quantifying the created world that we can see and touch. Pythagorean System: Inward In profound contrast to the standard four-rank system of mathematics used for worldly affairs (الظَّاهِرُ), the Pythagoreans' system is not intended for practical counting or commerce; its purpose is purely contemplative and esoteric ( الْبَاطِنُ ). This system introduces a series of specific philosophical terms for immense numbers, scaling up to a quadrillion (10¹⁵). These names, such as غَايَاتٌ (limits), هَنِيَّاتٌ (moments), and مَارُو , are not standard Arabic words but are used to give the mind a foothold when trying to grasp scales far beyond human experience. ESOTERICS It serves as a symbolic ladder designed to train the soul to move beyond the confines of the material world and stretch the imagination toward the concept of infinity. To understand the purpose of the two mathematical systems, think about the difference between your normal eyesight and a powerful telescope. The standard four-rank system of numbers (units, tens, hundreds, etc.) is like your eyesight; it is a practical tool necessary for the physical, observable world (الظَّاهِرُ). In profound contrast, the sixteen-rank system attributed to the Pythagoreans is like a mental telescope. It is not intended for practical commerce but is a tool for the inner, spiritual world ( الْبَاطِنُ ), designed to show you a reality far grander and more vast than you could otherwise imagine. This system’s purpose is purely contemplative; it serves as a symbolic ladder to train the soul to move beyond the confines of the material world and stretch the imagination toward infinity. After the first four familiar ranks, it introduces a series of specific philosophical terms for immense numbers that scale up to a quadrillion. These names, such as غَايَاتٌ (limits) and مَارُو , are not used for counting physical objects but are employed to provide the mind with a foothold when engaging in a spiritual exercise. By trying to wrap your mind around such a vast number, the imagination is forced beyond its everyday limits, leading to a profound sense of humility and awe at the boundless creative power of Allah. This contrast perfectly illustrates the relationship between the exoteric ( الظَّاهِرُ ) and the esoteric ( الْبَاطِنُ ). The sixteen-rank system is a tool for the intellect to journey toward higher realities. This numerical ladder is an analogy for the spiritual hierarchy, the حُدُودُ الدِّينِ (the Ranks of Faith). Just as a believer ascends from simple truths to more profound realities, the mind ascends the sixteen ranks of number. This journey from counting simple units ( آحَادٌ ) to contemplating immense numbers ( مَارُو ) is a metaphor for the soul's journey from the sensory world to the purely intelligible realm ( عَالَمُ الْإِبْدَاعِ ). The esoteric purpose of mathematics, therefore, is not merely calculation but the purification and elevation of the soul, which is the essence of esoteric interpretation ( التَّأْوِيلُ ). Pop Quiz Can you name the four basic qualities (natures) of all physical things? The four basic qualities are heat, cold, moisture, and dryness. What is the significance of the number one in the provided philosophical view, and how does it relate to Allah? In this philosophical view, the number one ($1$) is considered the principle of all numbers, generating all others but remaining indivisible, unchangeable, and whole. It is analogous to a single brick, which is the unit from which a wall (all other numbers) is built. Similarly, Allah is the ultimate cause and source of all creation, yet remains utterly transcendent, unchanging, and beyond comparison, just as the number one remains unchanged regardless of the numbers it generates. Explain the concept of "forms" (صورۃ or مثال) as described, and how the Universal Intellect (عقل کل) "contains" these forms. A "form" (صورۃ or مثال) is defined as the perfect, eternal, and unchanging blueprint or essence of a thing, distinct from its temporary, imperfect physical manifestation. The Universal Intellect (عقل کل) doesn't physically "contain" these forms. Instead, its very existence is to perfectly and simultaneously know all of these perfect forms, much like a master architect's mind contains the complete and unified blueprint for an entire city before it is built. It is this perfect realm of pure ideas that the Universal Intellect represents. How do the "Four Natures" lead to the "Four Elements," and what are these elements, along with their corresponding qualities? The four basic qualities of all physical things—heat, cold, moisture, and dryness—combine to form the four elements. These elements are: fire (hot and dry), air (hot and moist), water (cold and moist), and earth (cold and dry). Compare and contrast the Decimal System and the Pythagorean System of numbers as presented in the text, highlighting their respective purposes and how they relate to the exoteric (الظَّاهِرُ) and esoteric (الْبَاطِنُ) . The Decimal System, with its four fundamental ranks of units (آحَادٌ) , tens (عَشَرَاتٌ) , hundreds (مِئَاتٌ) , and thousands (أُلُوفٌ) , is primarily outward-focused (الظَّاهِرُ) . It is practical and tangible, used for everyday commerce, measurement, and quantifying the observable physical world. In contrast, the Pythagorean System is inward-focused (الْبَاطِنُ) and purely contemplative. It introduces philosophical terms for immense numbers (up to a quadrillion), such as غَايَاتٌ and مَارُو , not for practical counting but as a symbolic ladder. Its purpose is to train the soul to move beyond the material world and stretch the imagination towards the concept of infinity, serving as a metaphor for the soul's journey from the sensory world to the intelligible realm (عَالَمُ الْإِبْدَاعِ) . The Decimal System is like normal eyesight for the physical world, while the Pythagorean System is like a mental telescope for the inner, spiritual world.

title: The Story Of Saleh And The People Of Thamud
text: The People of Thamud and Their Land After the destruction of the people of 'Ad, the tribe of ثمود rose to prominence. They were blessed by Allah with great strength and ingenuity, inhabiting the land of Al-Hijr, located in the northwestern Arabian Peninsula. They were successors to 'Ad and were known for their unique ability to build magnificent dwellings. They constructed grand palaces on the plains and skillfully carved beautiful, secure homes directly into the mountains . Just as history repeats itself, despite these blessings, the people of ثمود became arrogant. They fell into idolatry, worshipping false gods instead of the One True God who had provided for them. The Call of Saleh (AS) Allah, in His infinite mercy, sent to them a messenger from among their own people, a man known for his wisdom and integrity: Saleh (AS). He called them to abandon their idols and to worship Allah alone, reminding them of the bounties they enjoyed. Hud [11:61] [ سورۃ ۱۱] ھود ‏ وَإِلَىٰ ثَمُودَ أَخَاهُمْ صَلِحًۭا ۚ قَالَ يَقَوْمِ ٱعْبُدُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ مَا لَكُم مِّنْ إِلَهٍ غَيْرُهُۥ ۖ هُوَ أَنشَأَكُم مِّنَ ٱلْأَرْضِ وَٱسْتَعْمَرَكُمْ فِيهَا فَٱسْتَغْفِرُوهُ ثُمَّ تُوبُوٓا۟ إِلَيْهِ ۚ إِنَّ رَبِّى قَرِيبٌۭ مُّجِيبٌۭ ‎ ۶۱ And to the Thamud [We sent] their brother Saleh. He said, 'O my people, worship Allah; you have no deity other than Him. He has created you from the earth and settled you in it, so ask forgiveness of Him and then repent to Him. Indeed, my Lord is near and responsive. The Heights [7:74] [ سورۃ ۷] الاعراف ‏وَٱذْكُرُوٓا۟ إِذْ جَعَلَكُمْ خُلَفَآءَ مِنۢ بَعْدِ عَادٍۢ وَبَوَّأَكُمْ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ تَتَّخِذُونَ مِن سُهُولِهَا قُصُورًۭا وَتَنْحِتُونَ ٱلْجِبَالَ بُيُوتًۭا ۖ فَٱذْكُرُوٓا۟ آلَآءَ ٱللَّهِ وَلَا تَعْثَوْا۟ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ مُفْسِدِينَ ‎ ۷۴ And remember when He made you successors after the 'Aad and settled you in the land, [and] you take for yourselves palaces from its plains and carve from the mountains, homes. Then remember the favors of Allah and do not commit abuse on the earth, acting as corrupters We also see the mention of these two nations in Surah Fajr أَلَمْ تَرَ كَيۡفَ فَعَلَ رَبُّكَ بِعَادٍ (٦) إِرَمَ ذَاتِ ٱلْعِمَادِ (٧) ٱلَّتِي لَمْ يُخْلَقْ مِثْلُهَا فِي ٱلْبِلَادِ (٨) وَثَمُودَ ٱلَّذِينَ جَابُوا۟ ٱلصَّخْرَ بِٱلْوَادِ (٩) Have you not considered how your Master dealt with 'Aad - They who had built lofty pillars, The likes of whom had never been created in anyland? And withThamud, who carved out the rocks in the valley? Arrogance and Rejection The leaders and the arrogant elite of ثمود rejected his call. They saw him as merely a human being like themselves and could not accept that Allah would choose him as a messenger. They accused him of being bewitched or simply lying. The Heights [7:75-76] [ سورۃ ۷] الاعراف ‏قَالَ ٱلْمَلَأُ ٱلَّذِينَ ٱسْتَكْبَرُوا۟ مِن قَوْمِهِۦ لِلَّذِينَ ٱسْتُضْعِفُوا۟ لِمَنْ آمَنَ مِنْهُمْ أَتَعْلَمُونَ أَنَّ صَلِحًۭا مُّرْسَلٌۭ مِّن رَّبِّهِۦ ۚ قَالُوٓا۟ إِنَّا بِمَآ أُرْسِلَ بِهِۦ مُؤْمِنُونَ ‎ ۷۵ ‏قَالَ ٱلَّذِينَ ٱسْتَكْبَرُوٓا۟ إِنَّا بِٱلَّذِىٓ آمَنتُم بِهِۦ كَفِرُونَ ‎ ۷۶ Said the eminent ones who were arrogant among his people to those who were oppressed - to those among them who believed, "Do you know that Salih is [truly] a messenger from his Lord?" They said, "Indeed we are believers in that with which he has been sent." The ones who were arrogant said, "Indeed, we are disbelievers in that which you have believed." They challenged him, demanding a miracle to prove that he was truly a messenger of God. They wanted a sign so extraordinary that they could no longer deny his prophethood. The Heights [7:73] [ سورۃ ۷] الاعراف ‏وَإِلَىٰ ثَمُودَ أَخَاهُمْ صَلِحًۭا ۗ قَالَ يَقَوْمِ ٱعْبُدُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ مَا لَكُم مِّنْ إِلَهٍ غَيْرُهُۥ ۖ قَدْ جَآءَتْكُم بَيِّنَةٌۭ مِّن رَّبِّكُمْ ۖ هَذِهِۦ نَاقَةُ ٱللَّهِ لَكُمْ آيَةًۭ ۖ فَذَرُوهَا تَأْكُلْ فِىٓ أَرْضِ ٱللَّهِ ۖ وَلَا تَمَسُّوهَا بِسُوٓءٍۢ فَيَأْخُذَكُمْ عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌۭ ‎ ۷۳ And to Thamud their brother Salih. He said: "O my people! Worship Allah! You have no other Ilah but Him. . Indeed there has come to you a clear sign from your Lord. This she­camel of Allah is a sign unto you; so you leave her to graze in Allah's earth, and touch her not with harm, lest a painful torment should seize you. In other sources of Hadith, the particulars of the miracle they requested have been mentioned. The people of ثمود did not just ask for any sign. In their arrogance and desire to set an impossible task for Saleh (AS), they made a series of very specific demands for the kind of miracle they wanted to see. According to these accounts, the leaders of ثمود gathered and challenged Saleh (AS), demanding that he bring forth a camel from a specific, large, isolated rock they pointed to . They then added several "impossible" conditions that the camel must have: The camel had to be female. She had to emerge miraculously from a particular solid rock they designated, a rock known in some traditions as الكاتبة . She had to be heavily pregnant , specifically ten months along, which is near the end of a camel's gestation period (13 months). This was to ensure it was a unique creation and not just a stray animal. She had to be of a reddish color, tall, and have attractive, noticeable features. Some narrations add that upon emerging, she was to give birth to a calf that was just like her. Their goal in setting these particular and seemingly impossible conditions was to prove that Saleh (AS) was incapable of producing such a miracle, thereby discrediting him and his message in front of everyone. They were certain he would fail. The Miracle of the She-Camel In response to their demand, Saleh (AS) prayed to Allah, and by his command, the rock split open, revealing a she-camel with the exact attributes they had demanded. It was a stunning and undeniable miracle. This was no ordinary animal. She was a clear and indisputable sign from Allah. Saleh (AS) presented her to his people with a specific set of divine instructions. Once Saleh (AS) produced this miracle by the command of Allah, he gave the people of ثمود certain specific instructions: Hud [11:64] [ سورۃ ۱۱] ھود ‏وَيَقَوْمِ هَذِهِۦ نَاقَةُ ٱللَّهِ لَكُمْ آيَةًۭ فَذَرُوهَا تَأْكُلْ فِىٓ أَرْضِ ٱللَّهِ وَلَا تَمَسُّوهَا بِسُوٓءٍۢ فَيَأْخُذَكُمْ عَذَابٌۭ قَرِيبٌۭ ‎ ۶۴ And O my people, this is the she-camel of Allah - [she is] to you a sign. So leave her to eat within Allah's land and do not touch her with harm, or an impending punishment will take you. The condition was that the she-camel would have her designated day to drink from their well, and the people would have their own designated day. They were strictly forbidden from harming her in any way. The Poets [26:155] [ سورۃ ۲۶] الشعرائ ‏قَالَ هَذِهِۦ نَاقَةٌۭ لَّهَا شِرْبٌۭ وَلَكُمْ شِرْبُ يَوْمٍۢ مَّعْلُومٍۢ ‎ ۱۵۵ He said, 'This is a she-camel. For her is a [day's] share of water, and for you is a [day's] share of water, [each] on a known day. So the conditions were: Let her roam freely on the land. Neither she nor her calf was to be harmed in any way. People of ثمود had to alternate days with the she-camel. She was to have an entire day to drink water alone from their well. The next day, they would drink from the well, and the she-camel would not. In return for the water, she would give miraculous milk, enough to feed everyone. The Transgression and Defiance For a while, the people of Thamud abided by the rule. However, the arrogant disbelievers grew resentful of the she-camel. Her presence was a constant reminder of Saleh's (AS) truthfulness and their own disbelief. They saw her as a threat to their position. Some were jealous, fearful of losing control, while others were rebellious toward divine authority. Despite the clear warnings, they decided to defy Allah's command and kill the she-camel. Their perfect fulfillment of impossible conditions made their act of killing the camel an even more serious and unforgivable act of defiance against Allah. In other accounts, it is mentioned that nine conspirators plotted to kill her. Their leader was a man named قيدار بن سالف , who was the Iblis of his era. This group not only carried out her murder, but their methods were particularly gruesome. The Quran states that they hamstrung her. The Moon [54:29] [ سورۃ ۵۴] القمر ‏فَنَادَوْا۟ صَاحِبَهُمْ فَتَعَاطَىٰ فَعَقَرَ ‎ ۲۹ But they called their companion, and he took [a sword] and hamstrung [her]. The Heights [7:77] [ سورۃ ۷] الاعراف ‏فَعَقَرُوا۟ ٱلنَّاقَةَ وَعَتَوْا۟ عَنْ أَمْرِ رَبِّهِمْ وَقَالُوا۟ يَصَلِحُ ٱئْتِنَا بِمَا تَعِدُنَآ إِن كُنتَ مِنَ ٱلْمُرْسَلِينَ ‎ ۷۷ But they hamstrung her and were insolent toward the command of their Lord and said, 'O Saleh, bring us what you promise us, if you should be of the messengers. "Hamstringing" is the act of cutting or severing the tendons behind an animal's legs, usually the hind legs. This causes the animal to collapse instantly, suffer paralysis, intense pain, and eventually die. They used a sword to cut the she-camel's tendon as she was returning from her day of drinking. She let out a loud, painful bellow that echoed through the valley. Some accounts even say the Earth shook at the sound. After killing the mother, they chased her calf, which tried to escape into the mountains. It cried out three times for help, but these horrible people caught and slaughtered it as well, ignoring all warnings. The Final Warning Now look at the mercy of Allah. After they had killed the sacred she-camel, Saleh (AS) came to them with a final, ominous warning. Their act of defiance had sealed their fate. He gave them a specific timeframe before their destruction would arrive. Hud [11:65] [ سورۃ ۱۱] ھود ‏فَعَقَرُوهَا فَقَالَ تَمَتَّعُوا۟ فِى دَارِكُمْ ثَلَثَةَ أَيَّامٍۢ ۖ ذَلِكَ وَعْدٌ غَيْرُ مَكْذُوبٍۢ ‎ ۶۵ So he said, 'Enjoy yourselves in your homes for three days. That is a promise not to be denied. This period was not a pointless delay; it served several crucial purposes, demonstrating divine justice and mercy, proving the certainty of Allah's promise, and allowing for the full manifestation of their spiritual state. A Final Act of Divine Justice The three-day period served as a final, formal notice. It established, beyond any doubt, that their destruction was a just consequence of their own choices. It also represented a final, slim window of opportunity for repentance. Had they shown remorse, they would have been demonstrating the sincerity that was absent all along. Their failure to do so confirmed their absolute rejection of faith, making them fully accountable for their fate. This period underscores Allah's justice ( عدل ) and mercy ( رحمۃ ), which gives every chance for redemption before punishment is enacted. Proof of an Unbreakable Promise The respite also functioned as a terrifying, unfolding proof of Prophet Salih's truthfulness. He didn't just warn them of doom; he gave them a specific timeline. This timeline was accompanied by signs that would confirm the warning was real. Salih (AS) told them what would happen to their bodies on each of the three days. This progression turned their initial mockery into escalating horror. Day 1: Their faces were to turn a yellowish hue. When this occurred, a sense of fear and sickness began to replace their arrogance. Day 2: Their faces turned red. This intensified their terror as they saw the second sign manifest exactly as foretold. Day 3: Their faces were to turn black. This signified their complete descent into despair and hopelessness, as they knew their doom was now sealed and imminent. By the time the final punishment arrived on the fourth day, the Thamud had witnessed the absolute and inescapable truth of Allah's promise. The prophecy had been proven, leaving no room for doubt. The Manifestation of Spiritual Death Symbolically, those three days symbolized the journey of spiritual death becoming visible for all to see. The shifting colors of their faces reflected their inner decline—yellow for fear, red for terror, and black for despair—highlighting how their souls were already fading away. The destructive force of the mighty blast was just the final confirmation of a spiritual breakdown that had been unfolding over those three days. Essentially, they were being shown the consequences of their choices—an impactful reminder that spiritual decisions have tangible and unavoidable outcomes. The Ant [27:50] [ سورۃ ۲۷] النمل ‏وَمَكَرُوا۟ مَكْرًۭا وَمَكَرْنَا مَكْرًۭا وَهُمْ لَا يَشْعُرُونَ ‎ ۵۰ And they planned a plan, and We planned a plan, while they perceived not The Divine Punishment and Salvation Allah protected Saleh (AS) and the believers who were with him from the plot of the disbelievers. On the appointed day, after the three-day reprieve had passed, Allah's punishment descended upon the people of Thamud. The Qur'an describes the destruction with terrifying imagery. It came as a mighty blast or an awful cry ( ٱلصَّيْحَةُ ) from the heavens, combined with a violent earthquake ( ٱلرَّجْفَةُ ). The punishment was so swift and absolute that it wiped them out completely. The Heights [7:78] [ سورۃ ۷] الاعراف ‏فَأَخَذَتْهُمُ ٱلرَّجْفَةُ فَأَصْبَحُوا۟ فِى دَارِهِمْ جَثِمِينَ ‎ ۷۸ So the earthquake seized them, and they lay, prostrate in their homes. Hud [11:67-68] [ سورۃ ۱۱] ھود ‏وَأَخَذَ ٱلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا۟ ٱلصَّيْحَةُ فَأَصْبَحُوا۟ فِى دِيَرِهِمْ جَثِمِينَ ‎ ۶۷ ‏كَأَن لَّمْ يَغْنَوْا۟ فِيهَآ ۗ أَلَآ إِنَّ ثَمُودَا۟ كَفَرُوا۟ رَبَّهُمْ ۗ أَلَا بُعْدًۭا لِّثَمُودَ ‎ ۶۸ ( 67 ) And awful cry overtook the wrong-doers, so they lay, prostrate in their homes, - ( 68 ) As if they had never lived there. No doubt! Verily, Thamud disbelieved in their Lord. So away with Thamud! Allah saved Saleh (AS) and the small community of believers who had heeded his call. The mercy of Allah spared them the horrific fate of their people. Hud [11:66] [ سورۃ ۱۱] ھود ‏فَلَمَّا جَآءَ أَمْرُنَا نَجَّيْنَا صَلِحًۭا وَٱلَّذِينَ آمَنُوا۟ مَعَهُۥ بِرَحْمَةٍۢ مِّنَّا وَمِنْ خِزْىِ يَوْمِئِذٍ ۗ إِنَّ رَبَّكَ هُوَ ٱلْقَوِىُّ ٱلْعَزِيزُ ‎ ۶۶ So when Our command came, We saved Saleh and those who believed with him by a mercy from Us and from the disgrace of that day. Indeed, it is your Lord who is the Powerful, the Exalted in Might After the destruction, Saleh (AS) turned away from the ruins of his people's homes, lamenting their choice to reject guidance. The Heights [7:79] [ سورۃ ۷] الاعراف ‏فَتَوَلَّىٰ عَنْهُمْ وَقَالَ يَقَوْمِ لَقَدْ أَبْلَغْتُكُمْ رِسَالَةَ رَبِّى وَنَصَحْتُ لَكُمْ وَلَكِن لَّا تُحِبُّونَ ٱلنَّصِحِينَ ‎ ۷۹ And he turned away from them and said, 'O my people, I had certainly conveyed to you the message of my Lord and advised you, but you do not like advisors.'

title: Esoterics
text: Prophet Salih's mission to the Thamud bears a striking resemblance to that of Prophet Hud, sharing a fundamental approach. He is lovingly described as their "brother," a term that signifies both a literal physical kinship ( ظاهر ) and a deep spiritual connection ( باطن ). On the surface, he shares their lineage, but esoterically, his brotherhood extends to all who embrace his call to faith, creating a bond that is both evident and heartfelt. Hud [11:61] [ سورۃ ۱۱] ھود ‏ وَإِلَىٰ ثَمُودَ أَخَاهُمْ صَلِحًۭا ۚ قَالَ يَقَوْمِ ٱعْبُدُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ مَا لَكُم مِّنْ إِلَهٍ غَيْرُهُۥ ۖ هُوَ أَنشَأَكُم مِّنَ ٱلْأَرْضِ وَٱسْتَعْمَرَكُمْ فِيهَا فَٱسْتَغْفِرُوهُ ثُمَّ تُوبُوٓا۟ إِلَيْهِ ۚ إِنَّ رَبِّى قَرِيبٌۭ مُّجِيبٌۭ ‎ ۶۱ And to Thamud their brother Salih. He said: "O my people! Worship Allah, you have no other Ilah but Him. He brought you forth from the earth and settled you therein, then ask forgiveness of Him and turn to Him in repentance. Certainly, my Lord is Near, Responsive." His call, " O my people, worship Allah; you have no deity other than Him ." This is the fundamental message shared by all prophets. On the surface, it's a straightforward message; however, fully grasping this concept of Tawhid cannot be achieved through superficial thinking alone. It requires an inner, esoteric understanding ( تأويل ), which can only be acquired through a divinely appointed guide. Without such guidance, a person attempting to comprehend Allah risks falling into error—either by likening Allah to His creation ( تشبيه ), thereby limiting Him, or by denying His essential attributes ( تعطيل ), to avoid such similarity. Genuine understanding of Allah cannot stem from personal speculation or from reading books; it must be received through the authorized source—the Imam of the Time or his designated Proof ( حُجَّة ). This is why the Quran begins with: The Cow [2:1-2] [ سورۃ ۲] البقرۃ ‏الٓمٓ ‎ ۱ ‏ذَلِكَ ٱلْكِتَبُ لَا رَيْبَ ۛ فِيهِ ۛ هُدًۭى لِّلْمُتَّقِينَ ‎ ۲ ( 1 ) ALM . ( 2 ) THAT is the Book about which there is no doubt, a guidance for those conscious of Allah Allah calls it " that " book because guidance cannot be found solely through the words of the Quran. Proper guidance is only possible through "that" book (referring to Maulana Ali and the Imams), who can interpret the Quran correctly for us. The Imam serves as " that " living, speaking Quran , revealing the true meaning behind the Quran's silent words, and guiding believers to stay on the right path. Building a Spiritual Homeّ The story uses the symbols of land and homes. When the Quran speaks of the believers being "settled in the land," it refers to their souls being firmly established on the true foundation of faith. Land symbolizes the Asas, just as heaven represents the Natiq. On this foundation, they are instructed to build. The Heights [7:74] [ سورۃ ۷] الاعراف ‏وَٱذْكُرُوٓا۟ إِذْ جَعَلَكُمْ خُلَفَآءَ مِنۢ بَعْدِ عَادٍۢ وَبَوَّأَكُمْ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ تَتَّخِذُونَ مِن سُهُولِهَا قُصُورًۭا وَتَنْحِتُونَ ٱلْجِبَالَ بُيُوتًۭا ۖ فَٱذْكُرُوٓا۟ آلَآءَ ٱللَّهِ وَلَا تَعْثَوْا۟ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ مُفْسِدِينَ ‎ ۷۴ "And remember when He made you successors after 'Ad and gave you habitations in the land, you build for yourselves palaces in plains, and carve out homes in the mountains. So remember the graces from Allah, and do not go about making mischief on the earth." "Constructing in its plains castles " symbolizes the establishment of the external, public institutions of the religious mission, or دعوة . These "castles" are the centers of learning and community that they built. The capacity to build magnificent structures was, in itself, a blessing and a bounty from Allah. When Salih (AS) reminded them, "You take for yourselves palaces from its plains ," he was pointing to a gift they had been given. The potential existed for these structures to be centers of a righteous community, built in gratitude to Allah and by His guidance. However, the people of Thamud corrupted this blessing. They built these structures for their own purpose, driven by arrogance and a love for the material world. Their intention was not to create public structures for the دعوة , but to display their own power and wealth. The palaces became symbols of their worldly vanity and their rejection of the Prophet's spiritual message. They were hollow achievements, utterly void of the guidance of Allah's Prophet. The Quran highlights this attitude in another chapter, where Prophet Salih challenges their misplaced priorities: The Poets [26:128-129] [ سورۃ ۲۶] الشعرائ ‏أَتَبْنُونَ بِكُلِّ رِيعٍ آيَةًۭ تَعْبَثُونَ ‎ ۱۲۸ ‏وَتَتَّخِذُونَ مَصَانِعَ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَخْلُدُونَ ‎ ۱۲۹ ( 128 ) "Do you build high palaces on every high place, while you do not live in them? ( 129 ) "And do you get for yourselves palaces as if you will live therein forever. They were building for amusement and with the foolish hope that their stone buildings would grant them immortality, forgetting the true permanence that comes from faith. Therefore, in the symbolic meaning, the "castles on the plains" represent a choice . When built with pure intention and under the guidance of the Imam's حُجَّة , they become strongholds of faith. But when constructed out of ego, for worldly status, and without spiritual guidance—as the people of Thamud did—they become empty palaces that testify to a community's spiritual sickness and offer no refuge from the final divine judgment. Carving Homes in Mountains ( Thamud ) For the people of Thamud, the ability to " carve from the mountains homes " was both a literal reality and a profound spiritual test. Allah granted them unique strength and skill, allowing them to build magnificent, secure dwellings directly from the rock cliffs of their land. Prophet Salih reminded them of this specific blessing as a sign of Allah's favor, a gift that should have led them to gratitude and faith. The Heights [7:74] [ سورۃ ۷] الاعراف ‏وَٱذْكُرُوٓا۟ إِذْ جَعَلَكُمْ خُلَفَآءَ مِنۢ بَعْدِ عَادٍۢ وَبَوَّأَكُمْ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ تَتَّخِذُونَ مِن سُهُولِهَا قُصُورًۭا وَتَنْحِتُونَ ٱلْجِبَالَ بُيُوتًۭا ۖ فَٱذْكُرُوٓا۟ آلَآءَ ٱللَّهِ وَلَا تَعْثَوْا۟ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ مُفْسِدِينَ ‎ ۷۴ "And remember when He made you successors after 'Ad and gave you habitations in the land, you build for yourselves palaces in plains, and carve out homes in the mountains. So remember the graces from Allah, and do not go about making mischief on the earth." This physical ability was meant to be a symbol for a spiritual potential: just as they could shape stone , they were invited to shape their souls with the eternal truths of faith. What does a house provide? Security! Therefore, the security of their rock-hewn homes was intended to remind them of the ultimate protection that comes only from Allah. The Arrogance of Material Skill The Thamud failed this test. They became mesmerized by their own technical skill and physical power. Instead of seeing their ability as a gift from Allah, they saw it as a product of their own genius. They mastered the literal, physical act of carving, but they refused to undertake the more important spiritual carving of the soul. Their focus remained on the external world, and they grew arrogant. Their magnificent homes became monuments to their pride , rather than sanctuaries of faith. They hollowed out the mountains to create secure spaces for their bodies while leaving their souls empty and exposed. They placed their trust in the permanence of stone rather than the permanence of the Imam's guidance . This misplaced pride in their worldly craft is exactly what Prophet Salih criticized when he asked them if they were building merely for amusement and a false sense of immortality. The Illusion of a Stone Fortress Thamud believed their mountain fortresses made them invincible, secure from any harm or accountability. This false sense of security bred defiance. It made them feel powerful enough to mock their Prophet, reject his message, and kill the she-camel, which was the very sign of his divine authority ( حُجَّة ). They believed their homes, carved from rock, could protect them from the consequences of their actions. Carving Homes in Mountains ( Believers ) For the believers, the same act of "carving from the mountains homes" signifies the dedicated, personal effort required to go beyond the surface of the faith. It is not enough to stand at the foot of the mountain; a believer is called to work with its substance actively. This carving is the process of seeking the profound, eternal truths, the حقائق , that lie at the heart of the revelation. This is the inner meaning of carving homes within the mountain. Think of the outward aspects of religion as the mountain of a mine. A person could spend their entire life on the surface and never find the gold and jewels hidden within. To find the treasure, one needs an expert miner—the Imam and his representatives—who knows where to dig and how to distinguish precious gems from ordinary stone. To "carve a home" is to follow the guidance of this expert , to dig into the mountain of faith and extract the pure, luminous truths ( حقائق ) that give it its true value. It is the active pursuit of spiritual knowledge and its internalization. The ultimate purpose of this spiritual work is to build a "home" for the soul—a safe, permanent, and unshakeable refuge. This is not a physical place, but an internal state of being. It is a condition of profound certainty ( یقین ) and tranquility ( سکینۃ ) built from understanding the eternal realities. This inner refuge protects the believer’s faith from the storms of the outside world: the confusion of conflicting ideas, the winds of doubt, and the temptations of materialism. the She-Camel The central symbol in the story is the miraculous she-camel. Prophet Salih presents her to his people, saying she is a sign, or آيَةٌ , from Allah. The Heights [7:73] [ سورۃ ۷] الاعراف ‏وَإِلَىٰ ثَمُودَ أَخَاهُمْ صَلِحًۭا ۗ قَالَ يَقَوْمِ ٱعْبُدُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ مَا لَكُم مِّنْ إِلَهٍ غَيْرُهُۥ ۖ قَدْ جَآءَتْكُم بَيِّنَةٌۭ مِّن رَّبِّكُمْ ۖ هَذِهِۦ نَاقَةُ ٱللَّهِ لَكُمْ آيَةًۭ ۖ فَذَرُوهَا تَأْكُلْ فِىٓ أَرْضِ ٱللَّهِ ۖ وَلَا تَمَسُّوهَا بِسُوٓءٍۢ فَيَأْخُذَكُمْ عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌۭ ‎ ۷۳ And to Thamud their brother Salih. He said: "O my people! Worship Allah! You have no other Ilah but Him. . Indeed there has come to you a clear sign from your Lord. This she­camel of Allah is a sign unto you; so you leave her to graze in Allah's earth, and touch her not with harm, lest a painful torment should seize you. This she-camel was not just an animal; she was an مثال , or a symbol for the Imam's حُجَّة . The حُجَّة , meaning "Proof," is a high-ranking teacher appointed by the Imam. Just as a strong male camel symbolizes the Imams , the she-camel represents his حُجَّة . The حُجَّة is the one who "carries" the pure spiritual knowledge from the Imam and "delivers" it to the community, nourishing them spiritually, much like a she-camel carries her young and provides milk. The Sacred Trust Prophet Salih gives his people a clear command regarding the she-camel: "So leave her to eat in Allah's earth, and do not touch her with harm." This is a symbolic instruction. To "leave her to eat in Allah's earth" means to allow the حُجَّة to receive his spiritual portion—the inner knowledge ( باطن )—directly from the Imam of the Time, who is Allah's representative on earth. In other words, they are called to respect the حُجَّة's authority and not interfere with his divine connection. Hud [11:64] [ سورۃ ۱۱] ھود ‏وَيَقَوْمِ هَذِهِۦ نَاقَةُ ٱللَّهِ لَكُمْ آيَةًۭ فَذَرُوهَا تَأْكُلْ فِىٓ أَرْضِ ٱللَّهِ وَلَا تَمَسُّوهَا بِسُوٓءٍۢ فَيَأْخُذَكُمْ عَذَابٌۭ قَرِيبٌۭ ‎ ۶۴ "And O my people! This she-camel of Allah is a sign to you, leave her to feed on Allah's earth, and touch her not with evil, lest a near torment will seize you." The warning, وَلَا تَمَسُّوهَا بِسُوٓءٍۢ do not touch her with harm," is a command not to disrespect, challenge, or reject the teachings of the حُجَّة . To harm the she-camel is to attack the channel of guidance from the Imam symbolically. فَيَأْخُذَكُمْ عَذَابٌۭ قَرِيبٌۭ "A swift torment will seize you." He warned them that any act of defiance against the حُجَّة was an act of defiance against Allah's sign, which would lead to their own spiritual and physical ruin. Demanding The Miracle The Thamud's challenge to Prophet Salih was more than a taunt; it was an unintentional confession. By demanding a creature emerge from rock, they subconsciously symbolized their spiritual state—petrified by arrogance, beliefless, and closed to divine light. They sought a miracle from their own spiritual death. In other words, their external challenge was a projection of their internal state. The demand for life from a dead stone was an echo of their soul's deepest, unspoken need for faith to be born from hardened hearts. Isn't it interesting that human actions often betray deeper truths? A person in a fit of rage, when they slam the door, doesn't analyze the symbolism of the slam; they simply act, yet the violence of the act speaks volumes about their inner state. It was the same thing with the Thamud. Lacking the self-awareness to plead, "Our souls have turned to stone; heal us," their pride led them to point at a rock and mock. In doing so, their challenge unintentionally became a prayer—a symbolic plea for a divine cure they were too arrogant to request outright. The Miraculous Birth of the She-Camel From the heart of barren rock, a living miracle emerges by divine command. The pregnant she-camel, symbolizing the sacred proof ( حجۃ ) and his life-giving knowledge, springs forth from the very source of lifelessness. Her birth is a powerful testament that Allah’s mercy can spark spiritual vitality in the most desolate of places, even within the cold, dead confines of disbelief. This transformation is the sacred function of the حجۃ (the representative of the Imam of the Time). He is the bearer of living guidance, a dynamic force sent to challenge the dead hearts symbolized by the rock. As the spiritual physician of his age, his purpose was to break the spell of apathy and unlock the hearts and minds sealed by arrogance. It's like a skilled doctor who doesn't just prescribe a pill; they take the time to understand the patient's condition. The doctor might use the patient's own blood sample—a part of their illness—to create a tailored, life-saving cure. The cure emerges from the context of the disease itself. The true miracle lies in this very confrontation between life and its absence. The Imam's حجۃ stands before the hardened state of his people and, through a divine act, makes it a source of new life. This shows that the guidance carried by the حجۃ can permeate any barrier, animating even the most resistant soul if they're willing to change. Here we're learning that faith isn’t just something built by our own intellects; instead, it’s a special gift from above that energizes, inspires, and motivates our spirits. It helps the waters of understanding flow freely from a place we once believed to be always dry. A Sign Tailored for Its Time The people of Thamud demanded a sign with particular characteristics—that the she-camel be red-headed, hairy, and ten months pregnant . These details emphasize that a divine proof is never vague. It is a precise, undeniable, and tailor-made miracle designed to address the specific spiritual sickness and doubts of the people to whom it is sent. They demanded that the knowledge presented by this حجۃ be the perfect medicine for the ailments of that community, so clear and distinct that it leaves no room for doubt in a heart that is sincere. Red Headed: Red is a conspicuous and influential color, often symbolizing authority, spiritual zeal, and high rank. A "red head" immediately makes the she-camel stand out, signifying that the حجۃ is not an obscure or ordinary figure. He should be a person of immense spiritual rank, clearly designated by the Imam of the Time and made visible to the community. Esoterically, the head is the seat of the intellect (العقل). A "red" head symbolizes an intellect that is not cold or purely human, but one that is ablaze with the fire of divine inspiration ( تائید ). It represents a luminous mind, illuminated by sacred knowledge and spiritual passion, capable of confronting the darkness and coldness of disbelief with undeniable warmth and light. Hairy: Hair, in its abundance, is a symbol of overflowing blessings, graces, and the outward manifestation of a rich inner reality. A "hairy" camel signifies a حجۃ who is covered in and overflowing with the countless details of spiritual knowledge. Each "hair" can be seen as a specific teaching, a proof, or a point of wisdom. The knowledge offered is not sparse; it is a dense and complete system of thought that covers every aspect of faith and life. Furthermore, a coat of hair serves as a protective layer. This symbolizes that the حجۃ and the knowledge he brings provide a spiritual "covering" for the community of believers. This esoteric knowledge is supported by a perfect mastery of the outer law, shields the faithful from the cold winds of doubt and the storms of falsehood, keeping the community spiritually warm, secure, and protected. Pregnant: The "pregnancy" indicates that the knowledge and proof brought by the حجۃ should not be empty or superficial. It should be full, fertile, and carry within it the complete potential for spiritual rebirth and growth. It must be a profound and perfect truth that, if accepted, will lead to the "birth" of new believers and a deeper, more vibrant spiritual community. 10 months: While a camel's normal gestation period is longer (13 months), the specific demand of ten months signifies completion , perfection , and the culmination of a cycle . As Allah says: تلک عشرۃ کاملۃ . Indeed, ten is the perfect number. Therefore, the ten-month pregnancy demanded that the حجۃ be fully formed, mature, and perfect. He should not possess a partial truth but a complete revelation ready to be "born" into the world. It also signifies that the "birth" of a new spiritual reality is imminent. The people of ثمود were at a critical moment in their history; the time for decision had arrived, and the truth was ready to manifest its full benefits. Finally, the ten months also represent ten stages of spiritual understanding, all of which were contained within the sign of the she-camel, offering a complete path to guidance had the people chosen to accept it. The Water & Milk وَنَبِّئْهُمْ أَنَّ الْبِئْرَ قِسْمَةٌ بَيْنَهُمْ ۖ كُلُّ شِرْبٍ مُّحْتَضَرٌ One of the conditions Saleh (AS) imposed on Thamud was that Thamud and the she-camel drink from a single water source on alternate days. In this parable, the well or water source symbolizes the ظاھر —the outer, literal dimension of the divine revelation. It is the text of the holy scripture of Nuh (AS) and the explicit laws of the religion. This " water " is the foundational source of all spiritual life, accessible to the entire community. However, in its raw form, it may not provide its complete nourishment. The Day of the Camel: When the she-camel, a symbol of the age, drinks from the well, she's said to consume all the water. This act represents the complete absorption, understanding, and internalization of the entire divine message. Through divine inspiration from the Imam, the حجۃ can penetrate the literal words and grasp their deepest meaning. This is the process of esoteric interpretation, where the outer text is processed spiritually by the teacher. The Day of the People: After drinking the "water" of the ظاھر , the she-camel provides an abundance of pure milk for the people. This milk symbolizes the باطن —the inner, esoteric knowledge and spiritual wisdom that has been extracted from the literal revelation. Water vs. Milk: Water is a basic element. Milk, by contrast, is a substance that's been transformed and refined into a perfect, life-giving nourishment, especially for those who are still growing and developing. This knowledge isn't a secret kept from the masses, but a source of spiritual nourishment that can feed all souls willing to receive it. Just as physical milk builds the body, this spiritual knowledge strengthens the soul, capable of transforming a spiritually empty and lost community into one that's healthy and thriving in its faith. The Divine Exchange: The community is asked to give up its access to the raw "water temporarily (the literal text) and, in return, receive the "milk" (the inner meaning) from the حجۃ . This purified spiritual sustenance is more potent and readily digestible for the soul. The Ultimate Test of Faith This setup created a divine rhythm for learning, but it was also a test of trust and patience.. The water , which is open to everyone, represents the outer laws of religion. The milk the she-camel provides is a symbol of the hidden, inner spiritual knowledge. This cycle creates a divine rhythm for spiritual learning. There's a time for the community to engage and adhere to the outer law, and there's a time for them to receive inner spiritual knowledge from their teacher. The entire setup is a test of trust, patience, and humility. This spiritual knowledge isn't meant to be a secret for a select few; it's offered to all humble souls (baby). Just as milk builds a healthy body, this divine knowledge builds a strong soul, capable of transforming a spiritually hungry community into one that thrives in faith. The people of Thamud failed this test. Blinded by arrogance and greed, they grew impatient, seeing only that the she-camel was drinking "their" water (taking over their religious control of the community) and failing to appreciate the far more valuable spiritual nourishment she offered. Because they couldn't accept needing a teacher for deeper wisdom, they rejected and killed the divinely appointed representative. In doing so, they cut themselves off forever from both the milk of spiritual knowledge and the water of the external law, ensuring their complete ruin. The Crime of Hamstringing The Camel The greatest crime of the people of Thamud was that "they hamstrung the she-camel." The esoteric meaning ( تأويل ) of this act is far graver than harming an animal. It symbolizes the rejection and overpowering of the divine Proof ( حُجَّة ). Esoterically, "hamstringing" the camel means they murdered this حُجَّة . But these wretched people did not just stop there. They also murdered the calf who was the Hujjat's successor. The one he was training to take his place after his death. This Quranic event is directly connected to the history of our Prophet's family. Rasul Allah (PBUH) prophesized the Martyrdom of Maulana Ali and Imam Hussain by saying: أَشْقَى الْأَوَّلِينَ عَاقِرُ نَاقَةِ ثَمُودَ، وَأَشْقَى الْآخِرِينَ قَاتِلُكَ " The most wretched of the ancients was the one who hamstrung the she-camel of Thamud, and the most wretched of the later ones is your killer ." In this context, Maulana Ali is the she-camel. After the she-camel was killed, her calf let out a mournful cry that is said to have sealed the fate of the Thamud and signaled the beginning of their three-day wait for destruction. This is a direct reference to the martyrdom of Imam Hussain, following that of Maulana Ali, and the 3 days they spent in thirst. Preventing the Imams from their rightful station and overpowering their authority is another form of this spiritual crime of "hamstringing." The Spiritual Hierarchy and Its Opponents According to the Quran, "there were nine people in the city who conspired to commit this heinous crime." The Ant [27:48] [ سورۃ ۲۷] النمل ‏وَكَانَ فِى ٱلْمَدِينَةِ تِسْعَةُ رَهْطٍۢ يُفْسِدُونَ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ وَلَا يُصْلِحُونَ ‎ ۴۸ And there were in the city nine men, who made mischief in the land, and would not reform. This passage suggests that opponents exist at every level of the spiritual hierarchy. The text then describes the nine levels of this sacred organization, which reveal the truth. 1 نطقاء Speaking Prophets 2 أُسُس Foundations/Imams as successors to the Prophets 3 أئمة Imams 4 حُجَج Proofs 5 نُقَبَاء Leaders 6 أيادي Hands/Supporters 7 أجنحة Wings/Assistants 8 مأذونين Authorized Preachers 9 مستجيبين Respondents/Believers In the divine order of the cosmos, a spiritual hierarchy (the Ranks of Religion) is established to transmit truth and guidance to humanity. This structure includes nine ranks leading up to the Imam. For each of these ranks of truth, there exists a corresponding level of opposition—an anti-hierarchy (a shadow)—that seeks to corrupt their work and mislead humanity. These nine corrupters represent the shadow hierarchy working actively to undermine the حجۃ by spreading doubt and rebellion among his people. The timeless lesson to learn here for every generation is to recognize and faithfully follow the Imam of the Time and the spiritual hierarchy that operates under his authority. It teaches us that this chain of divine guidance through the Imamate ( سلسلۃ ) is continuous and unbroken. There is always a living Imam on Earth to guide humanity, as the Quran says: وَاعْتَصِمُوا بِحَبْلِ اللَّهِ جَمِيعًا وَلَا تَفَرَّقُوا And hold firmly to the rope of Allah all together and do not become divided. This " rope of Allah " is the continuous guidance of the Imamate, which will remain accessible to humanity until the appearance of the قائم (the Riser), who will usher in the final Day of Resurrection by revealing the inner truths ( حقائق ) of all religions. True success and salvation lie in holding fast to this unbroken chain, generation after generation. PUNISHMENT: The 3 days of respite & 3 Colors: Hud [11:65] [ سورۃ ۱۱] ھود ‏فَعَقَرُوهَا فَقَالَ تَمَتَّعُوا۟ فِى دَارِكُمْ ثَلَثَةَ أَيَّامٍۢ ۖ ذَلِكَ وَعْدٌ غَيْرُ مَكْذُوبٍۢ ‎ ۶۵ But they killed her. So he said, "Enjoy yourselves in your homes for three days. This is a promise that will not be belied." The esoteric significance of the three colors on the Thamud's faces represents a striking, tangible sign of the spiritual decline that happens when someone abandons this chain of guidance. Each color signifies a specific stage in the soul's swift fall into ruin: Day 1: The Yellow of Spiritual Sickness Yellow is the color of withering and disease. In the باطن , the appearance of yellow on the first day symbolizes the sickness of doubt ( شک ) finally infecting their arrogant certainty. Their mockery begins to fade, replaced by a gnawing fear that the Prophet's warning is true. Esoterically, this yellowing represents the soul being severed from its source of life. Just as a leaf cut from a branch turns yellow and dies, their souls began to wither the moment they cut themselves off from the divine nourishment offered by the حجۃ (Prophet Salih) by killing the she-camel. It is the color of their hope beginning to fade, like the last light of a setting sun. Day 2: The Red of Burning Terror Red is the color of fire, blood, and intense emotion. The second stage signifies the progression from a sickening fear to a consuming, fiery terror. The truth of their impending doom is no longer a suspicion; it is a certainty that burns in their souls with the heat of profound regret. In the inner meaning, this red symbolizes a spiritual hemorrhage. Having "spilled the blood" of the sacred she-camel—the proof of the pure soul—they now witness their own spiritual lifeblood draining away. The red on their faces represented the outward sign of this internal bleeding of their spiritual vitality, the violent death throes of their rebellious ego ( نفس ) as it faces its own destruction. Day 3: The Black of Final Despair Black is the color of absolute darkness, finality, and the absence of light. This final stage represents the complete extinction of the Light of Faith ( نور الایمان ) from their souls. It signifies their arrival at a state of total spiritual death and hopelessness. Esoterically, the blackness symbolizes the ultimate veil ( حجاب ) that has now fallen between their souls and Allah's mercy. Their actions have forged an impenetrable barrier, and they are now completely enveloped in the darkness they have created. Their faces have become a reflection of the grave, marking the death of their souls even before their bodies have perished. It is the color of a void, signifying that their spiritual reality has been utterly annihilated. In essence, the three colors map the soul's journey into its own self-made abyss: from the yellow of withering hope, to the red of burning regret, and finally to the blackness of a permanent spiritual death. Destruction of Thamud: The Earthquake ( ٱلرَّجْفَةُ ) In the ظاھر , the earthquake was a violent tremor that shook their homes and the very ground beneath their feet. In the باطن , this represents the complete and utter collapse of their false spiritual foundations. The people of Thamud built their sense of security on material wealth, their physical strength, and the magnificent homes they carved out of the mountains. They put their faith in the tangible world ( دنیا ) instead of in Allah. The esoteric earthquake symbolizes the moment these false foundations were violently shattered . Their trust in worldly power and stone fortresses was revealed to be worthless when confronted by divine reality. It represents the convulsion of the soul when its entire worldview, built on arrogance and falsehood, is shaken to its core and utterly destroyed. The Scream ( الصیحۃ ) The scream was a single, deafening, inescapable blast that annihilated them. Esoterically, it was the overwhelming utterance of Truth ( الحق ) itself. Throughout their story, the Thamud had the choice to listen to the gentle, guiding voice of the Prophet Salih—the call to faith ( دعوۃ ). They chose to ignore and mock it. The صیحۃ is the consequence of that choice. It is the Word of Allah ( کلمۃ اللہ ) experienced not as merciful guidance, but as shattering, final judgment. It is the single, irresistible "scream" of reality that obliterates all falsehoods, shattering the illusions of pride, security, and power they had so carefully constructed. It is the sound of truth that the soul can neither ignore nor survive once it has rejected all prior invitations. Together, the earthquake and the scream represent a total spiritual annihilation. The رجفۃ is the collapse from below, destroying the false ground they stood upon. The صیحۃ is the blast from above, destroying the false beliefs they held. An Ironic Prostration The Quran states that the people were found in their homes, fallen prostrate, motionless. The esoteric meaning of this final posture ( جَثِمِينَ ) is a powerful and grim irony. جَاثِمِينَ The word جَاثِمِينَ is an active participle derived from the triliteral root جثم . It primarily means "to crouch," "to lie prone," "to kneel," or "to remain fixed in a place." It often implies a heavy or motionless presence. The choice of جَثِمِينَ is highly significant. It goes beyond merely stating that the people died. It specifically describes their posture and state: Immobility and Weight: The root conveys a sense of being weighed down and immobilized. After the earthquake, they had not merely fallen; they had been pressed down, unable to move, symbolizing the overwhelming force that struck them. Prostration and Helplessness: Their prostrate position signifies utter helplessness and defeat in the face of divine power. It's a posture of submission, albeit forced in this context by the punishment. Lifelessness: While the word doesn't explicitly mean "dead," in this context, when combined with "seized them" (أَخَذَتْهُمُ) and "in their homes," it strongly implies that they were left lifeless and stiff in that prostrate state. It paints a vivid picture of corpses lying flat on the ground. The linguistic precision of جَثِمِينَ thus powerfully conveys the devastating and final nature of the divine punishment, leaving the disobedient utterly crushed and lifeless in their dwellings. In life, their defining spiritual sickness was arrogance ( کبر ). They saw themselves as mighty and refused to humble themselves and prostrate before Allah in worship. In death, they are forced into a permanent, lifeless "prostration" of utter defeat and humiliation. Their final state is a dark mockery of the genuine, faithful prostration they rejected. They are eternally frozen in a posture of submission, but it is the submission of the vanquished and the damned, not the willing submission of the believer. It is the ultimate testament to the Quranic principle that all pride is destined to be brought low before the absolute power of Allah.

title: The Slave
text: A teacher cannot benefit a student, until the student acknowledges and accept him as his teacher! A doctor cannot help a patient until the patient accepts and submits to the prescribed treatment of the doctor. The doctor is helpless despite all his knowledge, will and desire to benefit the patient, until the patient completes the relationship. Allah is helpless in providing us with the benefit he desires for us unless and until we complete the relationship with him. I have to complete the relationship by submitting myself as his عبد .

title: Hierarchy of spiritual components
text: We began this topic of the soul (or self) with the حدیث of Rasul Allah(Salallahu alayhi wa aalihee wa sallam) in which he said: قال رسول اللہ صلی اللہ علیہ وآلہ و سلم مَن عَرَفَ نَفسَہُ فَقَدْ عَرَفَ رَبُّہُ The one who recognizes his soul, to that very extent, he recognizes his master It is impossible to understand the capabilities of the soul which makes it impossible to understand Allah(Subhanahu wa Ta'ala) The goal of this self reflection is to understand the "intent" of the creator, and not the creator himself. A spiritual hierarchy exists in the soul In the default state man is similar to animals or worse The Criterion [25:44] [ سورۃ ۲۵] الفرقان ‏أَمْ تَحْسَبُ أَنَّ أَكْثَرَهُمْ يَسْمَعُونَ أَوْ يَعْقِلُونَ ۚ إِنْ هُمْ إِلَّا كَٱلْأَنْعَمِ ۖ بَلْ هُمْ أَضَلُّ سَبِيلًا ‎ ۴۴ Or do you think that most of them hear or understand? They are only like cattle; nay, they are even farther astray from the Path. Animals stay within their limits, but human beings can cross all limites

title: Gratitude
text: Allah says: Luqman [31:12] [ سورۃ ۳۱] لقمان ‏وَلَقَدْ آتَيْنَا لُقْمَنَ ٱلْحِكْمَةَ أَنِ ٱشْكُرْ لِلَّهِ ۚ وَمَن يَشْكُرْ فَإِنَّمَا يَشْكُرُ لِنَفْسِهِۦ ۖ وَمَن كَفَرَ فَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ غَنِىٌّ حَمِيدٌۭ ‎ ۱۲ And indeed We bestowed upon Luqman wisdom (commanding him) "Be grateful to Allah," and whoever is grateful gives thanks, is grateful for his own self. And whoever is ungrateful, then indeed Allah is self-sufficient and worthy of all praise. The pinnacle and essence of all wisdom is gratitude Gratitude is obligatory The sign and proof of a healthy heart and an intact فطرۃ is that the heart feels gratitude and mercy The sign and proof of a sound intellect is that one recognizes the one who is worthy of our gratitude. If one's فطرۃ is corrupt, that person cannot benefit from the Quran. Because Allah states: The Cow [2:1-2] [ سورۃ ۲] البقرۃ ‏الٓمٓ ‎ ۱ ‏ذَلِكَ ٱلْكِتَبُ لَا رَيْبَ ۛ فِيهِ ۛ هُدًۭى لِّلْمُتَّقِينَ ‎ ۲ ( 1 ) Alif-Lam-Mîm. . ( 2 ) That Book, whereof there is no doubt, a guidance to those who are pious تقوی is the internal sense of right of right and wrong that comes from the فطرۃ which resides in the spiritual heart. There are certain favors that can be repaid and others that are impossible to repay When gratitude increases in magnitude it reaches the state of worship

title: The Forbidden Tree
text: After dismissing and expelling Iblis from his divine presence, Allah(Subhanahu wa Ta'ala) says to Adam. The Heights [7:19] [ سورۃ ۷] الاعراف ‏وَيَٓـَٔادَمُ ٱسْكُنْ أَنتَ وَزَوْجُكَ ٱلْجَنَّةَ فَكُلَا مِنْ حَيْثُ شِئْتُمَا وَلَا تَقْرَبَا هَذِهِ ٱلشَّجَرَةَ فَتَكُونَا مِنَ ٱلظَّلِمِينَ ‎ ۱۹ "And O Adam! Dwell you and your wife in Paradise, and eat thereof as you both wish, but approach not this tree otherwise you both will be of the Zalimun ." The word زوج means a "companion" or a partner - which is a very beautiful word describing the intent behind a marital relationship. Allah situated both within جنۃ and permitted them to enjoy all the blessings paradise has to offer He forbade them for approaching a particular tree Sin is always near indicated by the word هَذِهِ Allah clearly made the forbidden distinct from the permitted - both for Adam and his children The word ظالمون comes from ظلم and means oppression. The forbidden oppresses the self The word ظلم is defined as وضع شئی غیر محل . To place something in the wrong place. Allah did not forbid the fruit of the tree, but rather the proximity of the tree Allah forbade the fruit of the tree because Adam and Hawwa were not yet ready for it The permissible, far exceeds the forbidden in Islam.

title: Allah does not prove his existence in the Quran
text: Allah never users the Quran to proves his existence He discusses doubt in revelation, in messengers, in the after life, in all matters of faith that stems from believing in an unseen God. But he never discusses doubt in his own existence Because Allah's declaration in the Quran, is that I have already provided you with a different set of آیات (signs) that will bring you to that conclusion yourself. Because there are clear, valuable, meaningful signs in the universe for people of intellect The Family of Imran [3:190] [ سورۃ ۳] آل عمران ‏إِنَّ فِى خَلْقِ ٱلسَّمَوَتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ وَٱخْتِلَفِ ٱلَّيْلِ وَٱلنَّهَارِ لَآيَتٍۢ لِّأُو۟لِى ٱلْأَلْبَبِ ‎ ۱۹۰ Verily! In the creation of the heavens and the earth, and in the alternation of night and day, there are indeed signs for men of understanding. The creation of universe asks us: How can order and structure come out of an explosion? How can beauty exists as a result of an explosion? The way in which Quran asks us to approach Allah is through his signs that he created for us in the horizons and within ourselves. The آیات of the Quran push us outside into the world, to examine the آیات and signs that Allah has created for us outside of the book. And the آیات that are outside in the world, they push us back into the Quran to learn about the one the signs point to! SubhanAllah.

title: Comforting Friend
text: The word رفیق in Arabic, which comes from رفقۃ or رفق and it means comfort. The pillow that is placed behind your back when it's hurting is called مِرفِقَۃ as they take the pressure off your back and help you relax. A رفیق is a friend that you turn to when you are stressed out, in serious trouble, or going through a difficult time Women [4:69] [ سورۃ ۴] النسا ‏وَمَن يُطِعِ ٱللَّهَ وَٱلرَّسُولَ فَأُو۟لَٓئِكَ مَعَ ٱلَّذِينَ أَنْعَمَ ٱللَّهُ عَلَيْهِم مِّنَ ٱلنَّبِيِّۦنَ وَٱلصِّدِّيقِينَ وَٱلشُّهَدَآءِ وَٱلصَّلِحِينَ ۚ وَحَسُنَ أُو۟لَٓئِكَ رَفِيقًۭا ‎ ۶۹

title: Default state of the soul
text: Soul ( نفس ) There is a transition that takes place from body to spirit in terms of density (mass) to subtlety (energy). Material objects are made from the exact same material as our bodies: Fire, Air, Water, Earth. When we refer to ourselves, we are actually referring to the spiritual component called نفس . We ( نفس ) feel good or bad through the sensations of our body. Material body loves material things - and the greater the quality of that material thing, the more my نفس will enjoy it Finer things in life provide greater sensations of pleasure. Heart ( قلب ) The spiritual heart ( قلب ) is at the center of human emotions, desires and will. Happiness is when there is no conflict between the heart and the intellect Unhappiness is when there is a conflict between the heart and the intellect Rasul Allah(Salallahu alayhi wa aalihee wa sallam) said in a HADEES: ألا وَ اِنَّ فِی الجَسَدِ مُضغَۃً۔ وَأِذَا صَلَحَت صَلَحَ الْجَسَدُ کُلُّہُ؛ وَأِذَا فَسَدَتْ فَسَدَ الْجَسَدُ کُلُّہُ۔ اَلا وَ ھِیَ القَلْبُ Indeed within the body of a human being, there is a lump of flesh. When it is sound, the entire body is sound; and when it is corrupt the entire body is corrupt. Know that it is the spiritual heart! The ultimate goal of every human heart is the exact same thing - Satisfaction اطمئنان .
